          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 43/54 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The new `_log_xp_to_charts` helper is correctly wired for 4 of the 5 XP-grant paths, but ONE endpoint is missing the call: regular /challenge/complete. Detailed findings:\n\n  ✅ S3 Focus session — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='focus_session', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /focus/session L7287. /stats/weekly today.xp went 0→5 after 25-min completed session. by-area.mindset=5. task_log row with _source='focus_session' xp_awarded=5 confirmed via direct Mongo read.\n\n  ✅ S5 Challenge late-answer — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='challenge_late', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /challenge/past/{id}/answer L2768. Seeded an auto_uncompleted=true row directly in db.challenge_completions, then POST /challenge/past/{id}/answer with completed=true → awarded_xp=30, today.xp=30, _source='challenge_late' row present.\n\n  ✅ S6 Confidence track — `_log_xp_to_charts(source='confidence_track', focus_area='mindset')` fires from /confidence/complete L6043. POST /confidence/complete {track:'social'} → ok=true, xp_awarded=15, today.xp=15, _source='confidence_track' row present with xp_awarded=15.\n\n  ✅ S7 Gift XP correctly NOT logged into task_logs — admin gift of 50 XP increments today.gifted_xp by 50 but today.xp is unchanged AND zero task_log rows have any _source field for that user. profile.total_xp moved by 50.\n\n  ✅ S9 Regression — regular task complete still works AND does NOT create a duplicate _source row. Default 'Morning reflection' task complete → xp_awarded=15, today.xp ≥15, NO _source rows created (as designed — the regular task path writes a normal task_log row at L1579 without the _source tag), regular task_log row present.\n\n  ✅ S10 Cleanup — /profile, /stats/daily, /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area all 200 OK.\n\n  ❌ **CRITICAL BUG — S4 challenge_complete is missing _log_xp_to_charts**: /challenge/complete (server.py L2607-2661) does NOT call `_log_xp_to_charts`. Only the LATE-answer path (L2768) and the streak-bump call (L2647) fire after challenge XP is awarded. Verified by direct Mongo read: after POST /challenge/accept then POST /challenge/complete {completed:true,difficulty:'easy'} → awarded_xp=30 + profile.total_xp=30, BUT db.task_logs.find({user_id, _source:'challenge_complete'}) returns 0 rows AND /stats/weekly today.xp=0 (NOT 30 as the spec requires). FIX: add `await _log_xp_to_charts(user_id, awarded_xp, source='challenge_complete', focus_area='mindset')` immediately after `await _bump_streak_for_xp(user_id)` at server.py L2647.\n\n  ❌ S8 Aggregation correctness fails BECAUSE OF S4: today's xp aggregated from focus(5) + challenge(30) + step(30) should be 65. Test got 35 because the challenge 30 XP did not appear on the chart — direct downstream consequence of the S4 bug. Once S4 is fixed, S8 will pass automatically.\n\n  ⚠️ S1.5/S1.8 — TEST EXPECTATION (NOT BACKEND BUG): the spec said 'goal completion → total_xp 0→100' but the actual capped value for non-admin user with unit='days' is 30 (per GOAL_XP_CAPS in server.py L815). Furthermore, completing a unit='days' goal in ONE tick with target_value=1 ALSO awards step XP (30) on top of the completion bonus (30, capped from 100), so total_xp=60 — this is correct chained behaviour. The chart correctly shows 60 with both _source='goal_step' AND _source='goal_complete' rows present. NO BACKEND ACTION needed; spec assertion 'total_xp == awarded_xp' was naive.\n\n  ⚠️ S2 daily-step (3 ticks) is impossible to test as written — backend (correctly!) enforces a CYCLE LOCKOUT on unit='days' goals: after the 1st tick, /goals/{id}/progress with current_value=2 returns 429 'cycle_locked' until the next day cycle (next UTC midnight). The backend prevents same-day double-ticks of daily goals — this is intentional from a previously-shipped feature. So in this run only the 1st tick succeeded → 1 step row with _source='goal_step' xp_awarded=30, focus_area='fitness' was confirmed. The chart-logging machinery for goal_step is verified working; the spec's '3 ticks in one day' scenario simply can't happen.\n\n  ✅ S1 partial — _source='goal_complete' row WAS created on goal completion with focus_area='social' xp_awarded=30 (the capped completion bonus). by-area.social=60 (step+complete). The chart-logging path for goal_complete is working.\n\n  Summary: 4 of 5 _log_xp_to_charts call sites are wired correctly (goal_step, goal_complete, focus_session, challenge_late, confidence_track). The MISSING call site is in /challenge/complete (regular completion, not the late-answer path). One-line fix needed."

Endpoints under test: GET /api/library/pricing, POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}, POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/discount, POST /api/library/purchase/{app_id} (server.py L7240-7381). (S1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only, no Bearer) GET /library/pricing → 200 with response.pricing containing all 4 apps {sleep, challenges, spot, confidence} and response.currencies length=10 == [USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, CAD, JPY, INR, RON, CHF, BRL]. For an app with no doc / price=0 the default shape is exactly {price:0, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'', discount_percent:0, discount_active:false, effective_price:0, is_free:true, purchased:false}. (S2) Non-admin user POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} → 403 with detail 'Creator only.'. (S3) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example'} → 200 with body.pricing={price:9.99, currency:'USD', is_free:false, effective_price:9.99, purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example', discount_active:false}. (S4) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (S5) Invalid purchase_url 'ftp://bad' → 400. (S6) Negative price -1 → 400. (S7) Invalid app_id 'foo' → 400. (S7b) Sleep price still 9.99 after rejected requests (no partial writes). (S8) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, discount_starts_at and discount_ends_at non-null ISO timestamps, ends_at exactly 7.000 days ahead of now, effective_price=5.0 (within ±0.01 of expected 4.995 — backend rounds via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` at L7224 so 9.99*0.5=4.995 → 5.0). (S9) Admin POST same /discount {percent:0} → 200 clears: discount_active=false, effective_price=9.99 (full price restored). (S10) duration_value:0 → 400. (S11) percent:120 → 400. (S12) duration_unit:'years' → 400 (only days|weeks|months allowed). (S13) Non-admin POST /discount → 403. (S14) Fresh user A registered (gmail.com): a) GET /library/pricing → A.sleep.purchased=false, A.sleep.is_free=false; b) POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 {saved:true, already_owned:false, is_free:false}; c) Re-POST → {saved:true, already_owned:true, is_free:false} (idempotent); d) GET /library/pricing → A.sleep.purchased=true. (S15) A POST /library/purchase/challenges (free, no doc) → 200 {saved:false, already_owned:true, is_free:true} — backend short-circuits free apps without inserting a library_purchases row (server.py L7368). Verified via subsequent GET: challenges.purchased=false (no row was inserted). (S16) POST /library/purchase/foo → 400 invalid app_id. (S17) Anonymous flow (X-Anonymous-Id only, no JWT): a) GET /library/pricing → 200, sleep.purchased=false initially; b) POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 saved:true; c) Subsequent GET with same X-Anonymous-Id → sleep.purchased=true; d) Different X-Anonymous-Id → sleep.purchased=false (correct device isolation per anon_id). (S18) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 is_free=true; A's GET still shows A.sleep.purchased=true (library_purchases row persists across price changes) AND A.sleep.is_free=true. (S19) Regression — GET /library/ratings still works for both admin and a freshly-registered user; both return ratings dict with all 4 apps. (S20) GET /library/pricing returns pricing for all 4 apps including challenges/spot/confidence with is_free=true by default. Edge cases verified: rounding (effective_price 4.995→5.0 within tolerance), idempotency (re-POST purchase returns already_owned=true), anonymous device isolation (different X-Anonymous-Id values see independent purchase state), persistence across pricing changes (purchased stays true even when admin sets price=0). (1) GET /api/library/ratings — fresh gmail.com user (rate*@gmail.com) → 200 with response shape {ratings:{sleep:{...}, challenges:{...}, spot:{...}, confidence:{...}}}. Each app's stats has EXACTLY 3 keys {average:float|int, count:int, user_rating:int|null}. user_rating is null for fresh user across ALL 4 apps. NOTE on aggregate counts: the spec said 'fresh user with no prior ratings, ALL apps return {average:0, count:0, user_rating:null} initially' but on this live DB count is GLOBAL (not per-user) — there were already prior raters in db.library_ratings (sleep had count=2 at run start). user_rating=null IS satisfied for the fresh user (correct). The implementation aggregates across all users (server.py L6823: `db.library_ratings.find({})` with NO user filter), which is correct for a public 'community average' that everyone sees the same. (2) POST /api/library/ratings — Scenario A: {app_id:'sleep', stars:4} as fresh user → 200 with saved=true, app_id='sleep', stats.user_rating=4 (sleep avg recomputed correctly). Scenario B (update): {sleep, 5} → 200, stats.user_rating=5, count UNCHANGED from A (upsert semantics: same row updated, not inserted) — verified via follow-up GET sleep.count identical. Scenario C (different app): {challenges, 3} → 200, stats.user_rating=3; follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating still 5, challenges.user_rating=3, spot/confidence still null. Scenario D (multi-user aggregation): registered second gmail.com user 'Ryan Chen', their initial GET shows sleep.user_rating=null (correct cross-user isolation); user2 POSTs {sleep, 1} → 200; sleep.count incremented by exactly 1 (2→3 in this run); user1 GET shows sleep.count=3 with user_rating still 5 (their own, NOT affected by user2's vote). Average correctly recomputed (3.33 in this run with 3 raters; would be 3.0 in clean DB with only the two test users — math is correct). Scenario E (validation): all 5 invalid bodies returned 400 with descriptive details — {stars:0}→400 'must be between 1 and 5', {stars:6}→400 same, {stars:'abc'}→400 'must be an integer 1..5', {app_id:'invalid_app',stars:3}→400 'Invalid app_id. Must be one of [...]', {missing stars}→400 'must be an integer 1..5'. Scenario F (anonymous via X-Anonymous-Id header): set header X-Anonymous-Id='anon-test-<uuid>' (no Authorization) → GET works (200 with all user_rating=null initially), POST {sleep, 2} → 200 saved, follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating=2 persisted under that anon id, AND a different X-Anonymous-Id sees sleep.user_rating=null (correct isolation per anon device id). (3) Regression smoke (single test user with JWT): GET /api/profile → 200 with total_xp int; GET /api/stats/weekly → 200 with days length=7; GET /api/stats/monthly → 200 with days length=30; GET /api/stats/by-area → 200 with by_area:{social,fitness,appearance,mindset}; GET /api/library/catalog as admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com) → 200; GET /api/library/catalog as non-admin → 403. (4) Underlying collection name `library_ratings` and document shape (user_id, app_id, stars, updated_at, _id) verified indirectly via correctness — upsert semantics (Scenario B count unchanged) confirms the (user_id, app_id) composite uniqueness; cross-user aggregation (Scenario D) confirms global aggregation works; anonymous mode persistence under same X-Anonymous-Id confirms user_id is correctly derived from get_user_or_legacy. No bugs found."

(3) /api/focus/session now accepts new optional `locked_app_seconds` int field (Android UsageStatsManager-detected time inside blocklist apps). NEW XP rule: -15 XP per locked-MINUTE (rounded down), capped at -300, applies on BOTH completion AND early-exit (opening a blocked app is the violation regardless of whether the timer ran out). When locked_app_seconds is omitted (iOS / older builds), legacy fallback of -2 XP/min on backgrounded_seconds is preserved. focus_sessions DB rows now include locked_app_seconds for analytics. Need testing-agent run against /api/stats/monthly (shape, oldest→newest order, length=30) + /api/stats/by-area (after the regression fix, 200 with all 4 focus areas) + /api/focus/session with new field (penalty math: 4 min locked → -60, 0 locked → +bonus, 21 min locked → -300 cap, omitted → legacy -2/min)."

Default pre-committed: YouTube/Instagram/TikTok. (3) On Start, AppState listener tracks when app goes to background — at that moment we fire an expo-notifications HIGH-priority local notification ('🚨 Focus Mode breach — X min left — get back in and save your XP!') on a dedicated Android channel 'focus_breach' (MAX importance, lock-screen visibility, red vibration pattern). Accumulates `backgroundedSec`. (4) On foreground-return mid-session we show a Focus Wall modal: locked-app gallery with #777 greyed icons + blue LOCK_BLUE lock-badge on each tile + the running countdown. (5) Session-active taps on locked tiles trigger a 6px shake + warning haptic — 'can't open this now'. (6) On completion or End Early (with confirm dialog) we POST the session summary to /api/focus/session and show a Done screen with the XP delta (+5 for complete, −8 for 4min distracted, etc). BACKEND (/api/focus/session POST {planned_minutes, actual_seconds, backgrounded_seconds, completed, committed_app_count}): rule table — completed → +1 XP per 5 planned minutes (clamp 1..30); early exit with bg_min>0 → −2 XP per backgrounded minute (capped at −50 so one bad session can't nuke a user); clean cancel → 0 XP. Updates profile.total_xp + recomputes level, logs xp_events on +delta for leaderboard, writes a row to db.focus_sessions for history/anti-abuse. Smoke tested live at /tmp/focus_test.py — 5/5 assertions PASS (25-min complete=+5, 4-min bg=−8, clean cancel=0, 300-min rejected=400, 10-hour bg capped at −50)."
  - task: "Daylight-aware Spot scheduler (sunrise/sunset slots + multiplayer-broadcast night-skip + /admin/scheduler/daylight)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/notif_scheduler.py + /app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "ENHANCED Phase 1 — replaced fixed 09:00–21:00 surprise window with REAL sunrise/sunset per user. (1) Added `astral` (no API key) — `_user_daylight_today(prof)` resolves the profile timezone via a `_GEOCODE_OVERRIDES` map (29 common tz→city pairs) plus `tz.split('/')[-1]` fallback, then `astral.sun(loc.observer, date=local_today, tzinfo=tz)` — CRUCIALLY passing the LOCAL tz (NOT 'UTC' as I first did) because passing UTC interprets the calendar date as a UTC date and wraps the sunrise to the next day for tz offsets > 0 (the bug surfaced for Sydney where sunrise UTC came back as ~12 h after sunset UTC). The result is converted to UTC. Falls back to 06:00–20:00 local if geocode fails or sunset≤sunrise (high latitude). (2) `_generate_spot_slots_for_user(prof)` now picks 3 random slots inside (max(sunrise, fence_min), min(sunset, fence_max)), where fence is 08:00–21:00 local, with ≥90 min spacing. Slot output is sorted UTC iso strings. (3) `_is_daylight_now(prof)` — boolean gate consumed by /spot/match/create's broadcast loop: only friends whose CURRENT UTC time falls inside their own sunrise→sunset are pushed. Skipped friends are logged via `[match.invite.skip_night]` so the operator can see exactly why a friend didn't get the ping. The match itself is still created (host plays solo if needed). (4) New admin diagnostic GET /admin/scheduler/daylight?target_user_id= → returns `{user_id, timezone, now_utc, sunrise_utc, sunset_utc, is_daylight_now, next_3_random_slots_utc, stored_slots, stored_slots_day, stored_consumed}` for inspection. Live smoke-tested at /tmp/daylight_test.py with 8/8 assertions passing across THREE timezones (Sydney, Tokyo, LA): sunrise<sunset always; all 3 slots fall inside the daylight window; different tz → different sunrise UTC; 403 for non-admin; match-create still 200; broadcast log confirms `pushed=0 skipped_night=1` for a Tokyo friend who happened to be 27 min past sunset at test time → CORRECT behaviour. requirements.txt now pins astral==3.2."

  - task: "Boost activation — 500 fix on Creator-gifted custom boosts (POST /api/boosts/activate)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: true
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "BUG FIX (P0). User reported 'Internal Server Error' when activating a Creator-gifted Special Top-Up 2x boost. Root cause traced in two places: (1) `admin_gift_boost` writes inventory entries with `type='custom_gift'` for the custom path. The activation endpoint then did `cfg = BOOST_DEFS.get(boost_type)` followed by `cfg['duration_days']` — which crashes with `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable` (→ 500) because 'custom_gift' is NOT a key in BOOST_DEFS. (2) Even for preset gifts (e.g. `double_week`), gifts override `duration_days=1` but the activation read 7 from BOOST_DEFS, silently giving the wrong window. FIX: rewrote `boosts_activate` to (a) prefer the inventory entry's own `multiplier` + `duration_days` + `label` (the gifting endpoint already populates these), with BOOST_DEFS as a last-ditch fallback only; (b) validate multiplier 2..10 and duration 1..365 before computing expires (rejects partially-written gifts with a clean 400 instead of 500); (c) add idempotent 409 if the entry is already activated (separate from the 'not in inventory' 404); (d) atomic update guard via `$elemMatch` on the SPECIFIC entry id + `activated:{$ne:true}` to prevent races (previous attempt used `boost_inventory.activated:$ne:true` which incorrectly treated the whole array atomically); (e) wrap the DB update in try/except so any unexpected motor error returns a friendly 500 with logger.exception traceback instead of bubbling raw. xp_boost doc now also stores duration_days + label for the timer card. Verified via /tmp/boost_fix_test.py against the live ingress (https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api): admin gifts CUSTOM (5x for 3 days) → recipient activates → 200 with multiplier=5, expires=+3d. Admin gifts PRESET (`double_week` overridden to 1 day) → activate → 200 with multiplier=2, expires=+1d (correctly honours gift override, not the 7d default). Idempotent re-activation → 409 (was 500). Bogus inventory_id → 404. Legacy {type:'triple_day'} path still 200."

(3) DELETE /api/confidence/dress-history/{entry_id} — returns {deleted:N}, scoped to caller's user_id (so cannot delete other users' entries). (4) GET /api/confidence/weather?lat=&lon= — thin httpx wrapper over open-meteo (no API key) returning {temperature_c, condition, precipitation_mm, wind_kmh, hint}.           comment: "New endpoints + auth-gate integration. (1) `_check_not_suspended(user_id)` is called inside `get_user_or_legacy` so EVERY authenticated request a suspended user makes returns HTTP 403 with detail={error:'account_suspended', message, until, forever, remaining_seconds, suspended_at, suspended_by, reason}. Admin/Creator emails (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com etc.) are exempt — admins can never be suspended. Anonymous + 'main' legacy IDs are no-ops. (2) `/auth/login` calls the same gate AFTER password verification so suspended users can't login. (3) POST /admin/suspend body={user_id, duration_hours?, forever?, reason?}. Creator-only (403 otherwise). 400 on self-suspend, 400 on attempting to suspend another admin, 400 on missing duration when forever=false, 400 on duration > 5 years, 404 on unknown user. Stores `suspended_until` (ISO timestamp or string 'forever'), `suspended_at`, `suspended_by_admin`, `suspension_reason` (≤280 chars). (4) POST /admin/unsuspend body={user_id} → unsets the four suspension fields; returns {ok, user_id, modified}. (5) GET /admin/suspension/{target_id} → admin-only — returns {user_id, suspended:bool, until?, forever?, remaining_seconds?, suspended_at?, suspended_by?, reason?}."

          agent: "testing"

(3) GET /admin/suspension/{U} as U → 403 'Creator-only action.'; as admin → 200 with {user_id, suspended:false}. (4) POST /admin/suspend {user_id:U, duration_hours:0.001, reason:'automated test'} as admin → 200 with body.suspended_until parseable ISO, body.forever=false, body.duration_hours=0.001. (5) Immediately as U: GET /profile → 403 with detail dict containing exactly: error='account_suspended', message non-empty ('This account has been suspended.'), until parseable ISO, forever=False, remaining_seconds=int in 0..5 (observed 3), reason='automated test'. (6) POST /auth/login {U.email, U.password} → 403 with detail dict, detail.error='account_suspended' (same shape, gate fires after password verification at server.py L974).   - task: "Friends Weekly Leaderboard (timezone-scoped Mon-Sat window + Sunday winner + medal grant)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=<offset_minutes> endpoint. Each task completion in POST /api/tasks/{id}/complete now writes a doc to db.xp_events: {user_id, xp, earned_at_utc, tz_offset_minutes, local_week_key='YYYY-Www'}. Leaderboard logic: (a) members = viewer + accepted friends (de-duped). (b) For each member, compute THEIR OWN local Monday 00:00 → Sunday 00:00 window using their stored tz_offset_minutes (falls back to viewer's tz if null), then sum xp_events.xp in that [UTC-converted] range. (c) rows sorted by weekly_xp desc, each row includes: user_id, name, avatar, level, total_xp, weekly_xp, is_self, tz_offset_minutes, is_week_closed (viewer is in local Sunday), medals_count, medals_revoked. (d) If viewer's local-now weekday == Sunday and top row weekly_xp > 0 → winner is declared for the week that just ended; if no majority-supported active report against them, db.leaderboard_medals receives a {user_id, week_key, xp, revoked:false} doc and winner's boost_inventory gets a 'double_day' 2× XP-for-a-day entry appended (idempotent by (user_id, week_key)). If majority cheating-report exists, medal is inserted with revoked=true and NO bonus is granted. Please verify: (1) fresh users show weekly_xp=0; after completing a task the event is logged and sum increases; (2) tz param is persisted to viewer's profile; (3) two users in different tz each get their own Mon-Sat window; (4) winner is NOT declared Mon-Sat (viewer_is_sunday=false); (5) on Sunday, winner auto-gets medal + double_day entry in inventory (check status); (6) re-calling leaderboard on same Sunday doesn't duplicate the medal or boost."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 13/13 assertions. Fresh anonymous user GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows=[1 self row {weekly_xp:0, is_self:true}], reports:[], viewer_is_sunday boolean, week_key 'YYYY-Www'. tz=330 call persists tz_offset_minutes=330 to profile (verified via GET /profile). Completed first default task (xp_value=15) → POST /tasks/{id}/complete returned xp_awarded=15 (no boost). Re-fetched leaderboard → self_row.weekly_xp=15 = awarded XP, confirming xp_events doc was inserted and summation across the local Mon-Sat window works. Idempotency: two consecutive same-day calls return identical medals_count (Mon-Sat → no winner logic triggered, viewer_is_sunday=false today). Sort-order check (rows sorted desc by weekly_xp) verified separately on the 2-member leaderboard in the report-system test below — passes. Sunday-winner medal-grant + double_day inventory push could not be exercised directly since today is Mon-Sat in UTC, but the underlying _grant_winner_medal is keyed idempotently by (user_id, week_key) and re-calls return the existing medal doc by design. No data-integrity issues observed."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-VERIFIED (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) — GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (with valid JWT) → 200 returning {rows:list, reports:list, week_key:str, viewer_is_sunday:bool} and a single self row. Shape and basic flow still healthy."

  - task: "Leaderboard Report-Player System (submit / support / winner-revocation when >50% agree)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "New endpoints: (1) POST /api/leaderboard/report {reported_user_id, reason} → must be on your leaderboard (friend or self), inserts db.leaderboard_reports doc keyed by week_key; reporter auto-added to supporters[]; 400 on self-report or duplicate same-week report. (2) POST /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → adds user_id to supporters. (3) DELETE /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support → removes user_id. (4) Reports surface in GET /api/friends/leaderboard → reports[] array shows active reports visible to viewer with {id, reporter_name, reported_name, reason, week_key, supporters_count, viewer_supported, viewer_is_reporter}. Revocation logic: when viewer is on local Sunday and top-of-leaderboard winner has been reported, we count unique supporters who are ALSO on the leaderboard (viewer+friends). If supporters_count >= floor(N/2)+1 (strict majority) → _winner_report_verdict returns guilty=true → medal is inserted with revoked=true + revoked_reason and NO bonus is granted. Test: (1) report-then-support flow returns expected supporters counts; (2) majority triggers guilty verdict when ≥floor(N/2)+1 supporters on a N-member leaderboard; (3) duplicate report by same reporter in same week → 400; (4) reporting a non-leaderboard member → 400; (5) self-report → 400."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "🚨 CRITICAL BUG — POST /api/leaderboard/report returns 500 Internal Server Error on the FIRST successful submit even though the DB insert succeeds. Root cause (server.py line ~3037): `await db.leaderboard_reports.insert_one(doc); return {'report': doc}` — Motor's insert_one mutates `doc` in-place to add `_id: ObjectId(...)`. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder then chokes on the ObjectId. FIX: add `doc.pop('_id', None)` before `return {'report': doc}`."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS (re-verified after fix) — 20/20 assertions via /app/report_retest.py. Registered two fresh users (Alice Reporter + Bob Reportee), made them friends. POST /api/leaderboard/report from A {reported_user_id:B, reason:'Suspicious XP gain'} → 200 (no 500) returning {report: {id, reporter_id:A, reported_user_id:B, reason:'Suspicious XP gain', week_key:'YYYY-Www', supporters:[A], …}}. Verified the response has NO '_id' field, is fully JSON-serializable (no ObjectId leak), and reporter A is auto-added to supporters[]. POST /api/leaderboard/report/{report_id}/support from B → 200 with supporters_count=2. GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 from A → 200; reports[] contains the report with supporters_count=2 and viewer_is_reporter=true. The `doc.pop('_id', None)` fix is verified in production behavior."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-VERIFIED (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) — POST /leaderboard/report attempting self-report (reported_user_id == reporter_id) → 400 as expected. Other report flows (support/unsupport/duplicate/non-LB) were not re-exercised in this run but the underlying fix (doc.pop('_id', None)) remains in place at server.py L3037."

  - task: "Day Anchor System (timezone + day_start_time lock, tz-aware challenge day, user_today_str propagation, challenge past 24h answer window)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 76/76 assertions (Day Anchor regression run, 2026-04-27) via /app/backend_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api. (1) Profile schema additions — GET /api/profile now returns `day_start_time`, `timezone`, `onboarding_tz_done`. Fresh user has all three null/false. (2) Day-anchor write lock — fresh PUT /api/profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} → 200 with both fields persisted and onboarding_tz_done flipped to true; subsequent PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked' and timezone unchanged; PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; POST /api/profile/reset clears both fields back to null (and onboarding_tz_done back to false), and the original PUT works again post-reset. (3) Timezone-aware GET /api/challenge/today — with timezone=Australia/Sydney + day_start_time=07:00 returns 200 with challenge object containing id+title. (4) user_today_str propagation in POST /api/sleep/checkin — after sleep onboarding, POST /sleep/checkin {rating:7, hours:7.5} → 200; entry.date matches the Sydney-local date computed by user_today_str() (in this run, expected '2026-04-28' while UTC date was '2026-04-27' — confirming tz-aware computation, not raw server UTC). (5) Challenge past 24h answer window — GET /api/challenge/past returns {completions, count}; freshly-completed entry has can_answer:bool (False since it was not auto-uncompleted) and answer_deadline field present. POST /api/challenge/past/{fake-id}/answer → 404 'Past challenge not found'. All write-lock and tz-aware computations are working correctly."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — Day-Anchor onboarding hardening regression (2026-04-28). Ran /app/day_anchor_regression_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api: 46/46 assertions PASS. (1) Fresh anon user (no X-Anonymous-Id history) → GET /profile shows onboarding_tz_done=false (literal False), timezone=null, day_start_time=null — proves new users are STILL gated to the day-anchor-setup flow (no false positive from the new derivation). (2) PUT {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} → 200; subsequent GET shows onboarding_tz_done=true with both source fields persisted. (3) Lock rules intact: PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked'; PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked profile → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; both source fields verified unchanged after the rejected PUTs. (4) POST /api/profile/reset → 200 clears tz/day_start back to null and onboarding_tz_done back to false; a follow-up PUT {Sydney, 07:00} succeeds again (flag flips back to true). (5) LEGACY ADMIN PROFILE — POST /auth/login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200 with token; GET /profile → 200 with onboarding_tz_done=true, timezone='Australia/Sydney', day_start_time='07:00', is_admin=true (the migration backfill that ran on startup, '[migrate] Backfilled onboarding_tz_done=true on 13 legacy profile(s).', is observable in the live profile data). (6) Tz-aware GET /challenge/today returns 200 with challenge {id,title}; POST /sleep/checkin after onboarding returns 200 with entry.date matching the Sydney-local date (verified equal to datetime.now(ZoneInfo('Australia/Sydney')).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), distinct from raw UTC date). (7) Regression sanity all green — /auth/register (gmail.com domain) returns JWT immediately, /auth/login (correct) → 200, /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401, /profile auth → 200, /tasks complete returns xp_awarded>0, /tasks uncomplete returns xp_removed>0, /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 returns rows[] + reports[] + week_key. The hardening is working correctly: legacy users with both source fields are NOT re-prompted, and fresh users without those fields ARE still gated. NOTE: backend rejects emails with example.com domain ('does not accept email') — used gmail.com for fresh registration to bypass this productionised email-deliverability check (unrelated to the day-anchor work)."

Auth: POST /auth/register (full_name+email+password) → 200 returning JWT and user.verified=true (verification step has been disabled, accounts are usable immediately); GET /auth/me with Bearer → 200; POST /auth/login (correct creds) → 200; POST /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401. Profile: GET /api/profile (auth) → 200 includes day_start_time/timezone/onboarding_tz_done. Boosts: unlock with code 'XP270905W20' → 200; status → 200 with boosts_unlocked=true; claim {triple_day} → 200; activate {inventory_id} → 200 with active_boost.multiplier=3. Goals lifecycle: POST /goals (target_value=30, unit='days', xp_reward=30) → 200 (clamped to 30); GET /goals lists it; PUT /goals/{id} title update → 200; POST /goals/{id}/progress {current_value:1} → 200; DELETE /goals/{id} → 200.   - task: "Spot the Object — backend (object selection, vision check, complete, feed, like, comment, random-toggle)"
    implemented: true

    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 104/105 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (the 1 minor non-spot self-assertion is unrelated). Tested end-to-end against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api with anonymous mode (X-Anonymous-Id 36-char ids). Real photos pulled from loremflickr.com (keyword-relevant Flickr JPEGs, ~20KB each, satisfying /app/image_testing.md no-blank-images rule).\n\n  ✅ Profile additions: GET /api/profile fresh user → spot_points=0 (int), spot_random_enabled=False (bool). After /spot/complete success=true, spot_points=1.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/object: returns {object, challenge_id} with object always in the curated SPOT_OBJECTS list. 8 calls produced 8 distinct objects ('anything green', 'cat', 'chair', 'coin', 'flower', 'mug', 'pair of glasses', 'window') — randomization works.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/check: empty photo→400, >8MB b64→400. Real-photo positive case (leaf photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=True, confidence=1.0, can_capture=True. Real-photo negative case (chair photo, target='leaf') → 200 with detected=False, confidence=0.0, can_capture=False. can_capture invariant `detected AND confidence>=0.55` holds. GPT-4o-mini Vision integration via emergentintegrations is working — the LLM returned correct strict JSON for both polarity tests.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/complete: success=true → {entry, points_delta:1, spot_points:1, profile} with profile.spot_points incremented. success=false → points_delta=0, spot_points unchanged. Entry has id (uuid), success, target_object, mode preserved.\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/feed?limit=50: fresh user → entries:[]. After 2 completions → 2 entries each with player_name, player_avatar_base64, player_spot_points, liked_by_you, like_count, comment_count, is_self all populated; is_self=True for self.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/like: toggle 0→1→0 with liked_by_you flipping accordingly.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/{id}/comment: whitespace-only text→400 'Comment can\\'t be empty'. Normal comment 'Beautiful find! 🌿' → 200 with comment doc {id, user_id, user_name, user_avatar_base64, text, created_at}. Long text (350 chars) → 200 with text truncated to exactly 280 chars (no error).\n\n  ✅ GET /api/spot/{id}: returns full detail including comments[] (length=2 after 2 inserts) plus player_* and like_* fields. Bogus id → 404 'Photo not found'.\n\n  ✅ POST /api/spot/random-toggle: {enabled:true} → response shows spot_random_enabled=True; subsequent GET /api/profile reflects True. {enabled:false} → reflected back to False on both response and GET /api/profile.\n\n  ✅ Regression: /api/profile, /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=0 (rows/reports/week_key/viewer_is_sunday + self row), /api/tasks complete+uncomplete cycle (xp_awarded=15 then xp_removed=15) — all healthy. Test: profile of fresh user → medals=[] and is_flagged_cheater=false; after awarding medal via leaderboard flow it appears here; after revocation scenario the revoked flag surfaces and is_flagged_cheater=true."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 8/8 assertions. From registered user A's JWT, GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{B_id}?tz=0 → 200 returning the standard _serialize_player payload (user_id matches B, name='Bob Reportee', level int, total_xp int, friend_status='friends') PLUS the leaderboard-specific extensions: weekly_xp:int, medals:[] (empty for fresh user), is_flagged_cheater:false. GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{random-uuid}?tz=0 → 404 'Player not found'. The medal-attached / revoked path could not be triggered directly (today is Mon-Sat UTC), but the implementation reads from db.leaderboard_medals correctly via _compute_medals() and is_flagged_cheater = any(m.revoked for m in medals) is straightforward and correct by inspection."

  - task: "last_seen_at + UX-fix regression (friends list, players search, player detail, throttle, no-auth fallback, full critical-path)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS with one MINOR gap — 65/68 assertions in /app/last_seen_regression_test.py (run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The 3 failures are all the same single gap below.\n\n  ✅ [last_seen on friends list — PRIMARY UX SURFACE] Registered Nina Chen + Oliver Park, A→B friend request via POST /friends/request {user_id:B}, B accepts via POST /friends/accept {user_id:A}. GET /friends/list as A → 200 with friends[] containing B; B's entry has last_seen_at present and an ISO-8601 string (parses cleanly with datetime.fromisoformat). Mirror direction also works: GET /friends/list as B has A's entry with last_seen_at populated. ✅\n\n  ✅ [last_seen on player search + detail] GET /friends/players?q=Oliver → 200 with B in results; result has last_seen_at key. GET /friends/profile/{B_id} → 200 returning standard _serialize_player payload including last_seen_at. ✅\n\n  ✅ [Throttle correctness — performance] Rapid-fire GET /api/profile 10x within ~1s: all 200, zero errors, last_seen_at value remained STABLE across all 10 calls (1 distinct value), proving the in-process _LAST_SEEN_THROTTLE (60s window) is correctly suppressing redundant DB writes. ✅\n\n  ✅ [No-auth fallback] GET /api/profile with neither Bearer nor X-Anonymous-Id → 200 (no crash). _touch_last_seen('main') executes silently inside get_user_or_legacy → does not throw. Legacy 'main' profile is returned. ✅\n\n  ✅ [Critical-path regression — entire flow green]: POST /auth/register (gmail.com) returns JWT immediately + verified=true; POST /auth/login (correct creds) → 200; POST /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401. PUT /profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} on fresh profile → 200 with onboarding_tz_done=true; PUT timezone again → 400 with detail.error='tz_locked'; PUT day_start_time again → 400 with detail.error='day_start_locked'; POST /profile/reset → 200 clears both; subsequent PUT works again. POST /tasks/{id}/complete with body {date:today} → 200 returning xp_awarded=15; profile.total_xp grew 0→15; POST /tasks/{id}/uncomplete → 200 returning xp_removed=15; profile.total_xp rolled back to 0. POST /goals (focus_area=fitness, target_value=30, unit='days', xp_reward=30) → 200; PUT /goals/{id} → 200; DELETE /goals/{id} → 200. GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows[]+reports[]+week_key+viewer_is_sunday. GET /spot/object → 200 with {object,challenge_id}; POST /spot/check (empty photo) → 400. Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200, /profile shows is_admin=true. ✅\n\n  ⚠️ MINOR — /api/profile (the user's OWN profile) does NOT expose `last_seen_at` in its response. Root cause: server.py L477 `serialize_profile()` was NOT updated when `_serialize_player()` (L2630) gained the field. _touch_last_seen DOES correctly write profile.last_seen_at to MongoDB on every request (verified — friends/list and player detail surface a fresh ISO timestamp), but the user's self-profile dict simply omits the field. This does NOT block the actual UX feature (the 'Last seen X hrs ago' label in Friends/index PlayerCard reads from /friends/list, which DOES expose last_seen_at correctly). Optional 1-line fix in serialize_profile: `\"last_seen_at\": prof.get(\"last_seen_at\")`. Marking working:true because (a) the primary UX surface (friends list + player profiles) works end-to-end, (b) no 500 on /profile, (c) no data corruption, (d) throttle works correctly, (e) no-auth fallback works. The /api/profile gap is a cosmetic omission, not a functional break."

metadata:
  created_by: "testing_agent"
  version: "1.3"
  test_sequence: 4
  run_ui: false

  - task: "Spot the Object — MULTIPLAYER LOBBY (Phase 2): /api/spot/match/* (create, list, get, join, decline, start, cancel, capture) + lazy auto-finalize"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 55/55 assertions in /app/spot_multiplayer_test.py (run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered 3 fresh users (Olivia Carter / Marcus Bell / Sophia Reyes via gmail.com), made Host↔Friend1 friends via POST /friends/request {user_id:F1} → POST /friends/accept {user_id:Host}, left Host↔Friend2 strangers.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/create] (a) friend_ids=[] → 400 detail='Pick at least one friend to invite.' (b) friend_ids=[stranger only] → 400 detail='No confirmed friends in the invite list.' (c) friend_ids=[Friend1, Friend2_stranger] → 200; match.players contains Host+Friend1 only (Friend2 filtered out by friend_requests status=accepted check at server.py L3814-3822); status='waiting'; target_object=null.\n\n  ✅ [SHAPE check — every key the frontend types expect] match dict has id, host_id, status, target_object, started_at, ends_at, finished_at, seconds_left, winner_id, players[], viewer_role, viewer_captures, created_at. Each player dict has user_id, name, avatar_base64, is_host, joined, declined, captures. (Note: the review request mentions viewer_reward — server.py L3723 ONLY sets viewer_reward when status=='finished', otherwise it is omitted entirely from the dict. This is by design since viewer_reward is irrelevant pre-finalize. If the frontend expects it always-present, consider returning 0 instead of omitting — but it does not affect functionality.)\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/list] Host sees the new waiting match. Friend1 sees it (as invitee). Friend2 does NOT see it (was correctly filtered at create).\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/join] Friend2 (uninvited) → 403. Friend1 → 200 with match.players[Friend1].joined=true. Friend1 again → idempotent 200 (no error, $addToSet keeps it idempotent).\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/start] Friend1 → 403 detail='Only the host can start the match.' Host → 200 with status='active', target_object='leaf' (from SPOT_OBJECTS), started_at + ends_at ISO strings populated, seconds_left=120 (in 110-120 range as required). Host start AGAIN → 400 detail='Match is not in a startable state.'\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/capture] Empty photo → 400. >8MB → 400. Friend2 (not in match) → 403. Host capture with REAL Flickr JPEG (loremflickr.com, ~20KB) → 200 with shape {detected:bool, confidence:float, can_capture:bool, captures:int, match:{...}}. can_capture invariant `detected AND confidence>=0.55` correctly enforced. In this run target='leaf' and the LLM happened to return detected=False/confidence=0.0 for that particular Flickr leaf image, so captures correctly stayed at 0 (confirms the conditional-increment branch — the endpoint shape and contract are verified).\n\n  ✅ [Auto-finalize / timer reality] Two GETs spaced 3.5 s apart on the same active match showed seconds_left counted down by ≥2 s (e.g. 119 → 116). Confirms ends_at is a real wall-clock deadline. Did not blocking-wait 2 minutes for the lazy finalize path; review request explicitly accepted the timer-count-down check as sufficient.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/cancel] Created 2nd waiting match. Friend1 → 403. Host → 200 {ok:true}. Host /start on the cancelled match → 400.\n\n  ✅ [/spot/match/{id}/decline] Created 3rd waiting match. Friend1 /decline → 200 {ok:true}. Friend1 /list no longer contains that match (decline removed Friend1 from invited+joined arrays).\n\n  ✅ [REGRESSION on Phase 1 /api/spot/*] /spot/object → 200 with {object, challenge_id}. /spot/check with real Flickr leaf JPG → 200 with detected/confidence/can_capture shape (vision returned detected=False on this run's particular image, but contract correct). /spot/complete success=true → 200 with points_delta=1; /profile.spot_points correctly incremented by 1. /spot/feed → 200 with entries[]. /spot/random-toggle enabled=true → spot_random_enabled=true; enabled=false → false.\n\n  No bugs found. All 7 new /api/spot/match/* endpoints + lazy auto-finalize behave exactly as specified. Phase 1 endpoints unaffected."

  - task: "Direct Messaging + AI safety guard + Admin reports (9 new endpoints in server.py L4055-4424)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 88/88 assertions in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-04-29 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered 3 fresh users (Alice Morgan, Bob Carter, Carol Diaz @ gmail.com). Friend pair Alice↔Bob established via POST /friends/request + POST /friends/accept (note: server exposes /friends/accept, not /friends/respond as stated in the review request — but functionally equivalent). Carol left as a stranger.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/refine] (a) 'hey hows it going lol' → 200 with refined non-empty, severity='none', flagged=false. (b) empty → 200 with refined='', severity='none'. (c) 'shit dude this sucks' → 200 with severity='mild' and refined string (LLM scrubbed profanity). (d) 'send me nudes right now baby' → 200 with severity='severe', refined='', flagged=true. GPT-4o-mini via emergentintegrations is reachable; the JSON schema is correctly parsed by _refine_message_text().\n\n  ✅ [/messages/send] (a) Alice→Bob 'Hey Bob! How's it going?' → 200 with message {id, from_user_id=Alice, to_user_id=Bob, text non-empty, severity='none', read_at=null}. (b) Alice→Carol stranger → 403 'You can only message friends.' (c) Alice→Alice self → 400 'Invalid recipient.' (d) Alice→Bob with empty refined_text → 400 'Message is empty.' (e) Alice→Bob with predatory text 'send me nude pics right now baby' → 400 with detail={'error': 'blocked', ...} AND a row appeared in db.admin_reports for Alice (kind='message_text', severity='severe', viewed_at=null, dismissed_at=null) — verified through the admin endpoint below.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/threads] After Alice's good send: Alice GET → threads:[{friend_id:Bob, friend_name='Bob Carter', last_message:{...}, unread_count:0}]. Bob GET → unread_count=1, last_message.text matches what Alice sent. Carol GET → threads:[].\n\n  ✅ [/messages/thread/{friend_id}] Bob GET /messages/thread/{Alice.user_id} → 200 with messages:[{id, text, …}] in chronological order (single msg this point). Bob GET /messages/thread/{Carol.user_id} → 403 'Not friends.'\n\n  ✅ [/messages/read] Bob POST /messages/read {friend_id:Alice} → 200 with updated=1. Bob's subsequent /messages/threads shows Alice's unread_count=0. Bob POST /messages/read {friend_id:Carol} → 403.\n\n  ✅ [/messages/unread-summary] Alice sends 2 more messages to Bob → Bob GET /messages/unread-summary → 200 with unread_by_friend={<Alice.id>:2}, total_unread=2.\n\n  ✅ [/push/register-token] Bob POST {token:'ExponentPushToken[fakebob]', platform:'android'} → 200 ok:true. Same token re-POST → 200 (upsert idempotent). Empty token → 400 'token required'. Anonymous POST (no auth, no anon-id) → 200 ok:true (binds to legacy 'main').\n\n  ✅ [/messages/check-image] Real loremflickr leaf JPEG (~8KB b64) → 200 with safe=True, severity='none', reason populated. image_base64='' → 400. >8MB string → 400.\n\n  ✅ [Admin endpoints] POST /auth/login {filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599} → 200 with admin token. Admin GET /admin/reports → 200 with {reports:list, new_count:int}. The severe message_text report on Alice from step 2e is present (severity='severe', kind='message_text', reported_user_id=Alice, viewed_at=null, dismissed_at=null). Bob (non-admin) GET /admin/reports → 403 'Admin only.' Admin POST /admin/reports/{id}/view → 200; subsequent GET shows viewed_at=ISO timestamp populated AND new_count decremented (1→0). Admin POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss → 200; subsequent GET no longer includes the dismissed report (filter dismissed_at:null is enforced).\n\n  ✅ [Regression] (a) /auth/login (correct creds) → 200; wrong pw → 401. (b) /api/profile includes both last_seen_at AND onboarding_tz_done keys. (c) /api/spot/match/* smoke run all 200 (create with 1 friend, list, get, cancel). (d) /api/friends/list entry has last_seen_at field populated. (e) /challenge/today: today's deterministic per-user quote was 'He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.' (Nietzsche), so 'is NOW.' did not surface in this run, but verified the source string 'is NOW.' IS present at challenges_data.py:513 — the quote rotation is working.\n\n  No bugs found. All 9 new DM/admin endpoints work as specified, AI safety guard correctly identifies severity='severe' content and files admin reports, defence-in-depth re-pass on /send blocks predatory content even if a malicious client sends a non-refined string, and friends-only gate is enforced on every messaging route. NOTE: review request mentioned /friends/respond — actual route is /friends/accept (existing endpoint), tested with that. NOTE: review mentioned 'NOW' uppercase quote — confirmed present in challenges_data.py:513; whether it surfaces on a given /challenge/today call depends on per-user-per-day deterministic rotation."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "XP-CHEAT FIX VERIFICATION (Goals cycle-lockout persists through un-ticks for ALL user levels) — PASS 69/72 via /app/goals_lock_persist_test.py (run 2026-05-24 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The fix in /app/backend/server.py update_goal_progress (un-tick branch L2261-2302) — which deliberately does NOT clear last_completed_at — is FULLY VERIFIED to work end-to-end for admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com) AND a fresh non-admin user across all 4 functional scenarios:\n\n  ✅ TEST 1 (Weekly, admin): backdated created_at -10d → first-tick window expired → POST /progress cv=1 (200, +225 XP, locked 7d) → POST /progress cv=0 UN-TICK (200, -225 XP refund). **CRITICAL: is_locked STAYS TRUE, next_tick_available_at preserved EXACTLY (diff=0.00s vs pre-untick value), Mongo last_completed_at NOT null after un-tick.** → RE-TICK cv=1 returns 429 with detail.error='cycle_locked' and admin total_xp UNCHANGED. The cycle-lock fires for admin (Level cap=200 is system cap, not admin's current level which is 6 — lock fires REGARDLESS of level).\n\n  ✅ TEST 2 (Monthly, fresh non-admin user): backdated -35d → tick cv=1 (200, +900 XP, locked 30d) → un-tick cv=0 (-900 refund). **CRITICAL: is_locked STAYS TRUE, Mongo last_completed_at NOT null.** → RE-TICK 429 cycle_locked, total_xp unchanged.\n\n  ✅ TEST 3 (Cycle-expiry sanity, admin): tick→un-tick (locked); then pymongo-patched last_completed_at to 8d ago → GET goals shows is_locked=false → re-tick succeeds 200. Confirms legitimate cycle expiry still releases the lock.\n\n  ✅ TEST 4 (Step-progress weekly target=3, admin): ticked 1→2→3 (completed, locked) → partial un-tick 3→2 (200, completed=false). **CRITICAL: is_locked STAYS TRUE, Mongo last_completed_at PRESERVED after partial un-tick.** → Re-tick to 3 returns 429 cycle_locked.\n\n  ✅ TEST 5 (Regression — legitimate timeframe reset, admin): created weekly target=5, ticked to completion (locked) → PUT /goals/{id} {unit:'months'} → 200. **The legitimate reset path at server.py:2106-2115 fires correctly:** Mongo last_completed_at cleared to None, created_at reset to now (age=0.0s), is_locked=true under new monthly first-tick window, unit='months'. The 2 'failures' in this test (re-tick returning 200 instead of 429) are TEST-PLAN ARTIFACTS: backend correctly preserves current_value=5 after PUT, so posting cv=1 is a DECREMENT (un-tick branch), not an INCREMENT — the lock-check at server.py:2145 is intentionally skipped for non-incrementing requests. The lock STATE itself is computed correctly under the new monthly cycle.\n\n  TOTAL: 69 PASS / 3 FAIL where ALL 3 'failures' are TEST-PLAN assertion artifacts (admin's `profile.level==6` from total_xp=1669 vs the misread spec assumption of Level 200, plus Test 5's non-incrementing decrement that bypasses the lock check by design). NO backend bugs. The XP-cheat fix is shipping-ready. Marked task 'Goal time-lock (first tick) + Goal XP add/refund on tick/untick + goal_xp chart segment' working:true, stuck_count:0, needs_retesting:false. Main agent can summarise & ship."

    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Guest-mode → registered-account migration — PASS 29/29 in /app/backend_test.py (run 2026-05-16 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). All 6 sections of the review plan satisfied: (1) auth gate blocks no-auth + anon-only callers with 403 and rejects bad anonymous_id formats with 400/'8-64' detail; (2) format validation accepts 8-char minimal id + 19-char dashed id, rejects 65-char; (3) end-to-end happy path: anon A (anon_id=TESTANONA<hex>) created task+goal+completed task → total_xp=20, registered fresh user B, migrated → 200 {moved:13, collections_touched:5, merged_profile:true}, B's /profile total_xp=20, /tasks shows 17 (8 B-seeded + 9 migrated), /goals shows 1, idempotent re-run returns {moved:0}; (4) has_progress probe correctly returns true/false and gates on JWT (403 without auth); (5) db.users untouched — B login still works, anon-TESTANONA<hex> NOT in /admin/players list; (6) profile MAX-merge with merged_profile:true and full_name preserved. NOTE: review §6 asked for 500 XP via anon — anon-mode XP is capped at 20 per task (server.py:1640 enforces 20-cap for non-admin custom tasks). The MAX-merge semantics are still fully verified with the smaller anon XP. Marked working:true. No backend bugs found — main agent can summarise & ship."
    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Guest mode → registered account MIGRATION. Players can now use the entire app as an anonymous guest (foundation already existed via X-Anonymous-Id header; new module `/app/backend/guest_migration.py` ~200 lines wired in server.py near line 9510). When a guest signs in/registers, AuthContext stashes the old anon id and a post-onboarding modal pops asking 'Continue your guest progress?' — calling POST /api/guest/migrate {anonymous_id}.\n\n  ENDPOINTS (both require REAL JWT — anon ids cannot trigger):\n   • POST /api/guest/migrate {anonymous_id}\n   • GET /api/guest/has_progress?anon_id=...\n\n  TEST FOCUS — see task 'Guest-mode → registered-account migration' for the full 6-section plan. Highest priority: (1) auth gating (anon ids must NOT migrate), (3) end-to-end happy path with ≥3 collections moved + idempotency, (5) verify db.users untouched after migration.\n\n  Use admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599 as fallback. Create A=anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id) + B=fresh registered (POST /auth/register + /verify) for the migration scenario."

agent_communication_archive:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Tested Buried Treasure Phase 2 — Friends Mode (Relay Race) against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api on 2026-05-16. Test file: /app/buried_treasure_friends_mode_test.py. AFTER MAIN'S 2 FIXES: 97/97 PASS — full lifecycle (invite/accept/reject/cancel/bury/find), anti-cheat geo-check, XP awards (Seeker +100, Hider +50), auto-feed posts, 12h tick idempotent expiry, 30-min invite expiry, feed visibility to friends + like toggle. Solo-mode regression 12/12 PASS. Marked working:true."
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Tested Buried Treasure Phase 2 — Friends Mode (Relay Race) against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api on 2026-05-16. Test file: /app/buried_treasure_friends_mode_test.py. Result: 26 PASS / 9 FAIL. Two CRITICAL implementation bugs in /app/backend/buried_treasure.py block end-to-end Friends-Mode lifecycle:\n\n  ❌ BUG #1 (BLOCKS EVERYTHING — all Friends-Mode endpoints return 404): In `attach_routes`, `app.include_router(sub)` is called on line 766 BEFORE the Friends-Mode endpoints (decorated on `sub` lines 769–1193). FastAPI snapshots router state at include time, so /bt/match/invite, /accept, /reject, /cancel, /bury, /find, /matches, /match/{id}, /feed, /feed/{pid}/like all return 404 'Not Found'. Sections 1-5 and 8 of the test plan are NOT TESTABLE until this is fixed.\n  FIX: Move `app.include_router(sub)` from buried_treasure.py:766 to line 1195 (just before `return sub`). One-line move.\n\n  ❌ BUG #2 (BREAKS 12h-TICK XP AWARD): `if not _db` truthy checks on pymongo Database crash with `NotImplementedError: Database objects do not implement truth value testing or bool(). Please compare with None instead`. Locations: lines 340 (`_push_to_user`), 359 (`_profile_summary`), 380 (`_are_friends`), 430 (`_award_xp`). The 12h hunt-expiry tick correctly flips state='expired' / winner='hider' / xp_hider=50 on the bt_matches doc via find_one_and_update, but immediately crashes in `_award_xp` so profile.total_xp is NEVER bumped (X.total_xp delta = 0, expected +50). Stack trace verified in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log line 59637-59645.\n  FIX: Replace `if not _db` with `if _db is None` (and `if not _send_push or not _db:` with `if _send_push is None or _db is None:`) at lines 340, 359, 380, 430.\n\n  ✅ WHAT WORKS:\n   • Section 6 (30-min invite expiry tick) — state flip + idempotency PASS (4/4). Push notify path inside the tick logs '[bt-tick] invite-expired notify failed' due to Bug #2 in _profile_summary but is caught by inner try/except so the state transition still completes.\n   • Section 7 partial — state/winner/xp_hider/resolved_at all set on the doc, idempotency works (because the award never happens twice — neither time).\n   • Section 9 (Solo-mode regression) PASS 12/12 — all solo endpoints unaffected because they're defined BEFORE the misplaced include_router on line 766.\n\n  NOT TESTABLE due to Bug #1:\n   • Section 1: invite validation/duplicate guards (404 instead of 400/403/409/200)\n   • Section 2: accept/reject state machine (404)\n   • Section 3: cancel state machine (404)\n   • Section 4: bury anti-cheat geo-check + photo visibility (404)\n   • Section 5: find XP awards + auto-feed-post (404)\n   • Section 8: feed visibility to friends + like toggle (404)\n\n  After both fixes, re-run /app/buried_treasure_friends_mode_test.py — expect 45+ assertions to pass. Marked working:false with stuck_count=1. DO NOT proceed with frontend integration of Friends Mode until backend is fixed."

      message: "NEW: Buried Treasure Phase 2 — Friends Mode (Relay Race). 1-on-1 Hider/Seeker challenge built on top of the Phase 1 solo mini-app. Gameplay locked with user: 1A (1v1), 2A (Hider must physically walk into hunt-area circle to /bury), 3A (12h expiry → Hider wins +50 XP), 4B (Seeker 100 / Hider 50 XP on find), 5B (auto-post to bt_feed_posts with photo + consent toggle), 6C (sticky push + 30-min auto-expire on backend).\n\n  All Friends-Mode endpoints live in /app/backend/buried_treasure.py (extended attach_routes block); init_buried_treasure now also accepts send_push + friend_ids_fn (wired in server.py:9499-9506). 60-second background tick `_match_expiry_loop` resolves: (a) pending_accept invites > 30 min → state='expired_invite'; (b) in_progress matches past expires_at → state='expired', winner='hider', +50 XP awarded via $inc. State machine uses find_one_and_update for atomic CAS to guarantee idempotency.\n\n  PRIORITY TEST FOCUS — see task 'Buried Treasure Phase 2 — Friends Mode (Relay Race) — invite/accept/reject/bury/find lifecycle' for the full 9-section test plan."

Main agent can summarise & ship."

    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Duo Referral Discounts (v1.0.29) — 19/25 PASS in /app/duo_discount_test.py. ALL 9 duo endpoints work correctly (upsert/clear offer, get-offer, create, join, leave, my, get, admin-purchase-history): admin-only gating, 6-char uppercase code generation, lowercase-code join normalization, 72h expiry, idempotency (already_exists/already_member), full-group blocking, host-leave-cancellation, /library/pricing.duo_offer enrichment, validation of required_people 1..5 and discounted_price<full_price, etc. — ALL VERIFIED.\n\n  ❌ CRITICAL BUG (steps 16/17/18/19/20/23): /api/payments/webhook crashes 500 on signed Stripe payloads at server.py:8527 (the obj.get('metadata') line). When STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is configured (the current production state per /app/memory/test_credentials.md), `_stripe_mod.Webhook.construct_event` returns a stripe.StripeObject — NOT a dict — and `obj.get('...')` raises AttributeError('get'). This bricks the entire Duo payment lifecycle AND breaks all checkout.session.completed flows in production.\n\n  Fix is a 1-2 line patch right after server.py:8511:\n      if not isinstance(obj, dict):\n          obj = obj.to_dict_recursive() if hasattr(obj, 'to_dict_recursive') else json.loads(str(obj))\n  Then every existing obj.get('x') will work as written. After the fix, re-run /app/duo_discount_test.py — expect 25/25 PASS. The 5 cascading failures (17, 18, 19, 20, 23) all depend on the webhook successfully writing the library_purchases row and the duo_groups.members[].paid_at field — they will pass automatically once the webhook handler stops crashing."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler. profile.shift_schedule overrides the static day_start_time when enabled. user_today_str() now uses the per-day shift's wake-up time so daily resets follow the user's pattern. Endpoints (server.py): GET /api/schedule, PUT /api/schedule (validates HH:MM, hex #RRGGBB, percent ranges, ISO dates), POST /api/schedule/reset, PUT /api/schedule/day/{date_iso} {shift:'day'|'night'|'off'|null}, GET /api/schedule/preview?days=N. TEST PLAN: (1) anon GET /api/schedule → 200 default. (2) PUT pattern=['day','day','night','night','off','off','off','off'] enabled=true → 200. (3) invalid pattern entry → 400. (4) bad color #ZZZZZZ → 400. (5) refresh_offset_hours=15 → 400. (6) shift start_time='25:99' → 400. (7) PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-09 {shift:'off'} → 200, manual_overrides has entry. (8) PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-09 {shift:null} → 200, cleared. (9) bad date 'foo' → 400. (10) bad shift 'lunch' → 400. (11) GET /schedule/preview?days=14 → 200 array len 14. (12) After step 7, preview shows date='2026-05-09' is_override=true shift='off'. (13) POST /schedule/reset → 200; subsequent GET enabled=false pattern=[] manual_overrides={} refresh_offset_hours=2. (14) Regression: existing /profile, /tasks, /goals, /library/pricing, /admin/reports still work. Mark working:true if all pass."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Boost pricing endpoints + payment-intent kind='boost' — 63/63 PASS via /app/boost_pricing_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-08). All 17 review-request scenarios verified end-to-end (optional step 18 webhook signature simulation skipped per spec). Endpoints under test live at server.py L7810-7941 + L7481-7575.\n\n  ✅ (1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only) GET /boosts/pricing → 200 with 3 default boosts {triple_day, double_week, double_month} all is_free=true purchased=false; currencies array length=10 (≥10 satisfied).\n  ✅ (2) Non-admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day → 403 'Creator only.' exact match.\n  ✅ (3) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 pricing.price=2.99 is_free=false currency='USD'.\n  ✅ (4) Invalid boost_id 'foo' → 400. (5) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (6) Negative price → 400.\n  ✅ (7) Discount 50% / 7 days → discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price=1.5 (within ±0.01 of expected 1.495 — backend rounds to 2 decimals).\n  ✅ (8) percent=0 clears (discount_active=false, effective_price=2.99). (9) percent=120 → 400. (10) Non-admin discount → 403.\n  ✅ (11) Fresh user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with kind='boost' app_id='triple_day' amount=299 currency='USD' client_secret starts 'pi_test_' customer_id starts 'cus_' publishable_key starts 'pk_test_'.\n  ✅ (12) PI {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  ✅ (13) Admin sets triple_day price=0 → PI {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free — no checkout needed.'\n  ✅ (14) Restore 2.99. Fresh user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} → 200 saved=true is_free=false; GET /profile boost_inventory contains triple_day entry.\n  ✅ (15) Same C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} (still free) → 200 saved=false is_free=true; profile.boost_inventory still ONLY has triple_day (no double_week added — free purchase short-circuits before granting).\n  ✅ (16) POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  ✅ (17) Regression — all prior endpoints 200 OK on normal paths: GET /library/pricing, GET /library/ratings, GET /admin/reports, POST /payments/create-checkout (library), POST /payments/create-payment-intent (default kind=library).\n\n  Webhook step 18 SKIPPED per spec (optional / signature simulation hard). Code-review confirms server.py L7686-7711: payment_intent.succeeded handler with metadata.kind='boost' uses _make_inventory_entry + dedupes on boost_inventory.stripe_payment_intent before $push (idempotent on resend).\n\n  Marked task 'Boost pricing endpoints + payment-intent kind=boost' working:true, needs_retesting:false. No bugs found — main agent can summarise & ship."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Boost Pricing & Purchases (Points+ Boosts shop). Mirrors Library+ pricing endpoints but for the 3 XP boosts (triple_day, double_week, double_month) shown in the Bonus Top Up tab. New endpoints (server.py):\n  • GET /api/boosts/pricing → returns pricing for all 3 boosts + currencies array (same shape as /library/pricing).\n  • POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id} body={price, currency, purchase_url} (Creator-only).\n  • POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}/discount body={percent, duration_value, duration_unit} (Creator-only).\n  • POST /api/boosts/purchase body={boost_id} → trust-based fallback that grants the boost into profile.boost_inventory if priced (free → no-op).\n  • POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent now accepts {kind:'boost', app_id:<boost_id>} → creates a Stripe PaymentIntent with metadata.kind='boost' and metadata.boost_id=<id>. Webhook payment_intent.succeeded handler now branches: kind='boost' → grants boost into inventory idempotently (dedupe on stripe_payment_intent inside boost_inventory); kind='library' → existing library_purchases flow.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Anonymous GET /api/boosts/pricing → 200 with 3 boosts (all default is_free=true, purchased=false), currencies array len ≥10.\n2. Non-admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99} → 403 'Creator only.'\n3. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 with returned pricing.price=2.99, is_free=false.\n4. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/foo {price:1} → 400.\n5. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:5, currency:'XYZ'} → 400.\n6. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 discount_active=true, effective_price≈1.495.\n7. Admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:0} → clears.\n8. Non-admin POST /api/boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount → 403.\n9. As fresh user A, POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200; response has kind='boost', app_id='triple_day', amount=299 (5% off if discount), payment_intent metadata should contain kind='boost' boost_id='triple_day'. (This reads price 2.99 USD set in step 3.)\n10. POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n11. Set triple_day to free → POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free'.\n12. Restore triple_day price 2.99. Webhook test: POST /api/payments/webhook with valid stripe-signature header for event payment_intent.succeeded with metadata={kind:'boost', boost_id:'triple_day', user_id:<B>}. Verify boost is added to user B's profile.boost_inventory (verify via GET /api/profile boost_inventory non-empty). Re-send same webhook → no duplicate (idempotency on stripe_payment_intent field inside the inventory entry).\n13. As fresh user C: POST /api/boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} (trust fallback) → 200 saved=true; profile.boost_inventory now contains the boost.\n14. POST /api/boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} when double_week is free → {saved:false, is_free:true} — no inventory change.\n15. Regression: existing /library/pricing, /library/ratings, /admin/reports, /payments/create-checkout, /payments/create-payment-intent (kind defaults to 'library') all still work.\n\nMark task working:true if all assertions pass."

New endpoints: GET /boosts/pricing, POST /boosts/pricing/{id}, POST /boosts/pricing/{id}/discount, POST /boosts/purchase. Reuses _pricing_doc_to_pub helper from library pricing. The existing POST /payments/create-payment-intent now branches on body.kind ('library' default | 'boost'): for boost kind reads price from db.boost_pricing, for library kind reads from db.library_pricing. PaymentIntent metadata includes both kind and boost_id (or app_id) so the webhook can route the unlock correctly.
