          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 63/63 assertions in /app/boost_pricing_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). All 17 review-request scenarios verified end-to-end (the optional step 18 webhook signature simulation was skipped per spec). Endpoints under test: GET /api/boosts/pricing, POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}, POST /api/boosts/pricing/{boost_id}/discount, POST /api/boosts/purchase, POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent (kind='boost'), all live at server.py L7810-7941 + L7481-7575.\n\n  ✅ (1) Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only, no Bearer) GET /boosts/pricing → 200 with response.pricing containing exactly the 3 boost_ids {triple_day, double_week, double_month}. Each entry has is_free=true and purchased=false (default state with no doc). response.currencies = ['USD','EUR','GBP','AUD','CAD','JPY','INR','RON','CHF','BRL'] (length=10, ≥10 satisfied).\n\n  ✅ (2) Non-admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 403 with detail='Creator only.' (exact string).\n\n  ✅ (3) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day {price:2.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 with body.pricing={price:2.99, currency:'USD', is_free:false, effective_price:2.99}.\n\n  ✅ (4) Invalid boost_id 'foo' → 400 'Invalid boost_id. Must be one of [...]'. (5) Invalid currency 'XYZ' → 400. (6) Negative price -1 → 400.\n\n  ✅ (7) Admin POST /boosts/pricing/triple_day/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price=1.5 (within tolerance ±0.01 of 1.495 — backend rounds via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` at server.py L7239 so 2.99*0.5=1.495 → 1.5).\n\n  ✅ (8) Admin POST /discount {percent:0} → 200 with discount_active=false, effective_price=2.99 (full price restored).\n\n  ✅ (9) percent=120 → 400 'percent must be 0..99'. (10) Non-admin POST /discount {percent:10, duration_value:1, duration_unit:'days'} → 403.\n\n  ✅ (11) Fresh user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with response.kind='boost', app_id='triple_day', amount=299 (Stripe minor units for $2.99 USD), currency='USD'. payment_intent_client_secret starts with 'pi_test_' (live Stripe test key in use), customer_id starts with 'cus_', publishable_key starts with 'pk_test_'. PaymentIntent metadata correctly carries kind='boost' and boost_id='triple_day' (verified via server.py L7546-7553).\n\n  ✅ (12) POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'foo'} → 400 'Invalid boost_id. Must be one of [...]'.\n\n  ✅ (13) Admin sets triple_day to price=0 → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {kind:'boost', app_id:'triple_day'} → 400 'This item is free — no checkout needed.' (substring 'free' satisfies spec).\n\n  ✅ (14) Restore triple_day price=2.99. Fresh user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'triple_day'} → 200 with saved=true, is_free=false. GET /profile as C → boost_inventory contains a triple_day entry (type='triple_day' from _make_inventory_entry helper at server.py L3289 + _grant_boost_to_inventory at L7908).\n\n  ✅ (15) Same user C POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'double_week'} (still free, no doc) → 200 with saved=false, is_free=true. Subsequent GET /profile boost_inventory still has only the triple_day entry — NO double_week added (free-purchase short-circuits at server.py L7937-7938 without granting).\n\n  ✅ (16) POST /boosts/purchase {boost_id:'foo'} → 400 invalid boost_id.\n\n  ✅ (17) Regression — all prior endpoints 200 OK on normal paths: GET /library/pricing (auth), GET /library/ratings (auth), GET /admin/reports (admin), POST /payments/create-checkout (library mode, fresh user with sleep priced 9.99), POST /payments/create-payment-intent (default kind=library) all returned 200.\n\n  ⚠️ Step 18 (webhook with valid Stripe signature) was SKIPPED per spec ('Optional / skip if hard'). The webhook handler logic itself is verified by code-review at server.py L7686-7711: payment_intent.succeeded event with metadata.kind='boost' calls _make_inventory_entry + dedupes on boost_inventory.stripe_payment_intent before $push — idempotent on resend. Live Stripe-CLI signature simulation requires whsec generation outside the test runner; main agent can validate this with Stripe Dashboard webhook test events if needed.\n\n  No bugs found. Marked task working:true, needs_retesting:false."


      message: "NEW: Stripe PaymentSheet support added for in-app native checkout. New endpoint POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id} returns {payment_intent_client_secret, ephemeral_key_secret, customer_id, publishable_key, amount, currency, effective_price, app_id, payment_intent_id} — used by mobile PaymentSheet. The webhook /api/payments/webhook now ALSO handles event type 'payment_intent.succeeded' (in addition to checkout.session.completed) and inserts a library_purchases row idempotently using metadata.user_id + metadata.app_id. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is now configured (whsec_…). Backend behaviour to verify:\n\n  1. Admin sets sleep price to 9.99 USD (POST /api/library/pricing/sleep).\n  2. Fresh user A POSTs /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with non-empty payment_intent_client_secret starting with 'pi_test_…_secret_', ephemeral_key_secret starting with 'ek_test_', customer_id starting with 'cus_', publishable_key starting with 'pk_test_', amount=999, currency='USD', effective_price=9.99, payment_intent_id starting with 'pi_test_'.\n  3. Re-call as same user A → also 200; should re-use the same Stripe customer (users.stripe_customer_id is set). New PaymentIntent each time but same customer.\n  4. /api/payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n  5. Set sleep to free → /api/payments/create-payment-intent → 400 'This app is free'.\n  6. Restore sleep 9.99. Mark user A as already-owning sleep (POST /library/purchase/sleep) → then /payments/create-payment-intent → 409 'You already own this mini-app.'\n  7. Webhook 'payment_intent.succeeded' — register fresh user B. Send POST /api/payments/webhook (no signature header — will fail signature check now since whsec is configured; should return 401). Then send WITH proper Stripe signature: simulate properly-signed event using stripe.WebhookSignature or use Stripe CLI; OR (simpler) temporarily test by removing whsec from env (NOT WANTED). Instead please verify the handler logic with raw payload directly via mongo: insert library_purchases via /library/purchase fallback for the sake of regression, then send webhook with valid sig if possible.\n     Easier alternative: send POST /api/payments/webhook with body={'type':'payment_intent.succeeded','data':{'object':{'id':'pi_test_dummy_b','amount_received':999,'amount':999,'currency':'usd','metadata':{'user_id':'<B>','app_id':'sleep'}}}} — expect 401 (signature missing). Confirm the 401 response.\n  8. Regression — all prior endpoints still PASS (admin pricing CRUD, /payments/create-checkout, /library/ratings, /admin/reports).\n\nMark task working:true if all pass. Stripe SDK version in use: stripe-15.1.0."

  - task: "Stripe PaymentIntent + payment_intent.succeeded webhook"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/server.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Implemented at server.py L7456-L7551. New endpoint POST /api/payments/create-payment-intent creates a Stripe Customer (cached on users.stripe_customer_id), an EphemeralKey, and a PaymentIntent with metadata{user_id, app_id, currency, price}. Returns everything PaymentSheet's initPaymentSheet() needs. Webhook handler extended to cover 'payment_intent.succeeded' (event types now: checkout.session.completed, payment_intent.succeeded). Signature verification active because STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_… is configured — un-signed payloads now return 401 instead of being accepted. Frontend integration: BuyAppModal on Platform.OS!=='web' calls paymentsCreatePaymentIntent and then presentNativePaymentSheet (from src/PaymentSheetNative.ts which uses @stripe/stripe-react-native). On web it falls back to the existing /payments/create-checkout flow. After PaymentSheet returns ok, app shows a green 'Payment successful! ✓ — You now have access to this feature! — Thank you 🎉' success card."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PASS — 36/36 assertions in /app/stripe_pi_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). All 9 review-plan steps green:\n\n  ✅ [S1] Admin login + POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200 with pricing.price=9.99, currency='USD', is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [S2] Fresh gmail.com user A (Maya Patel) → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200. Real Stripe call hit. Response shape verified:\n    • payment_intent_client_secret='pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg_secret_fJErOBzqFEHPsesgiP7mH8Hyk' — starts with 'pi_' (NOTE: actual prefix is 'pi_3' for Stripe test mode, NOT 'pi_test_' as the spec states; spec wording is inaccurate — Stripe test-mode IDs use the live-mode prefix 'pi_3...' with the test key. The '_secret_' substring IS present, which is the meaningful assertion).\n    • ephemeral_key_secret starts with 'ek_test_' ('ek_test_YWNjdF8xVFVx…').\n    • customer_id starts with 'cus_' ('cus_UTqYTNnijfr5eN').\n    • publishable_key starts with 'pk_test_' ('pk_test_51TUqjxJriEuN9Pqi…').\n    • amount=999 (exact), currency='USD', effective_price=9.99.\n    • payment_intent_id='pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg' starts with 'pi_' (same NOTE as client_secret — test-mode prefix is 'pi_3' not 'pi_test_').\n\n  ✅ [S3] Re-call POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} as same user A → 200. Body: same customer_id ('cus_UTqYTNnijfr5eN' both calls — verifies users.stripe_customer_id caching at server.py L7509-7521 works), DIFFERENT payment_intent_id ('pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg' → 'pi_3TUsrUJriEuN9Pqi0Vp7Ax2G' — PaymentIntent is one-shot, new one created each call, exactly as spec requires).\n\n  ✅ [S4] POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'foo'} → 400 with detail=\"Invalid app_id. Must be one of ['sleep', 'challenges', 'spot', 'confidence']\".\n\n  ✅ [S5] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 (sleep is_free=true). User A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 with detail='This app is free — no checkout needed.'\n\n  ✅ [S6] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} (restore) → 200. User A POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 with saved=true, already_owned=false, is_free=false (legacy fallback row inserted in library_purchases). Then user A POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 409 with detail='You already own this mini-app.'\n\n  ✅ [S7 — WEBHOOK SIGNATURE NOW ENFORCED] (a) POST /api/payments/webhook with NO `stripe-signature` header and a valid JSON body {type:'payment_intent.succeeded', data.object:{id:'pi_test_dummy_unsigned', amount_received:999, amount:999, currency:'usd', metadata:{user_id:'fake-user', app_id:'sleep'}}} → **401 {detail:'Invalid signature'}** (was 200 before whsec was set). (b) POST same body with bogus 'stripe-signature: t=123,v1=bogus' → **401 {detail:'Invalid signature'}**. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_wzpkmxkPB7xTYkERRN2QYy51vv6MXUW3 is now active in /app/backend/.env and the verify branch at server.py L7641-7646 correctly rejects un-signed payloads via `_stripe_mod.Webhook.construct_event(body, sig, _stripe_whsec)` raising and being caught.\n\n  ✅ [S8] Discount math: admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'} → 200 with discount_active=true. Fresh user C (Aiden Brooks) → POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with amount=500 (50% of 999 cents = 499.5 → Python's round() banker-rounds to 500), effective_price=5.0 (within ±0.01 of 4.995). Discount cleared after test.\n\n  ✅ [S9 — REGRESSION] All existing endpoints still work for fresh user D (Ryan Chen):\n    • POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with session_id='cs_test_a1r276bi4mxHqz62bNIx3OyCxsRcrUe8rHM8eawttJiBCxsMgIHtQW0Jno', checkout_url='https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_…'.\n    • GET /library/pricing → 200 with pricing dict.\n    • Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD'} → 200.\n    • GET /library/ratings → 200 with ratings dict.\n    • POST /library/ratings {app_id:'sleep', stars:5} → 200.\n    • GET /admin/reports (as admin) → 200 with reports list.\n\n  CAVEAT (one-line, NOT a backend bug): The spec asserted 'payment_intent_client_secret starts with pi_test_' and 'payment_intent_id starts with pi_test_'. Stripe's actual test-mode IDs use the prefix 'pi_3' (e.g. 'pi_3TUsrTJriEuN9Pqi1DcrnXxg'), NOT 'pi_test_'. This is correct Stripe behaviour — the 'pi_test_' prefix doesn't exist in modern Stripe API. The meaningful structural assertions (starts with 'pi_' and contains '_secret_' for client_secret) DO hold. Test relaxed accordingly. No code change needed.\n\n  Marked working:true; webhook signature verification is enforced; PaymentSheet endpoint contract honoured exactly."


      message: "NEW: Real Stripe Checkout integration. Test stripe-15.1.0 SDK is installed and STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is configured (test mode sk_test_…). STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is intentionally NOT yet configured (the user has not registered the webhook in their Stripe dashboard yet) — please verify the backend correctly accepts un-verified payloads in that case (logs a warning) and rejects with 401 once a whsec is configured. Endpoints (server.py around L7400):\n  • POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id} → looks up server-side pricing, creates a real Stripe Checkout Session, returns {session_id, checkout_url, amount, currency, effective_price, app_id}. Errors: 400 if app_id invalid / app is free / effective_price=0; 409 if user already owns; 503 if stripe key missing.\n  • POST /api/payments/webhook → on `checkout.session.completed` with payment_status='paid' inserts library_purchases row idempotently using metadata.user_id + metadata.app_id. Idempotent: same session_id → no duplicate insert. Returns {received:true, type}.\n  • GET /api/payments/session/{id}/verify → fallback for when webhook hasn't arrived; queries Stripe directly (will fail in pure unit tests because session_id cs_test_… isn't a real Stripe session — please test with mocked sessions or live Stripe test mode by hitting create-checkout first).\n  • GET /api/payments/return?status=&session_id= → returns HTML that auto-redirects to xpconfidence://payments/return deep link.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Admin login → POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200.\n2. Fresh user A → POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with session_id starting with 'cs_test_', checkout_url starting with 'https://checkout.stripe.com/', amount=999, currency='USD', effective_price=9.99.\n3. POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'foo'} → 400.\n4. Set sleep back to free (price:0). POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 'This app is free…'.\n5. Restore sleep price=9.99. POST as user A again → 200. Then mark user A as already-owning sleep (use existing /api/library/purchase/sleep). POST create-checkout again → 409 'You already own this mini-app.'\n6. Webhook idempotency: simulate POST /api/payments/webhook with body={'type':'checkout.session.completed','data':{'object':{'id':'cs_test_dummy_xyz','payment_status':'paid','status':'complete','amount_total':999,'currency':'usd','payment_intent':'pi_test_xyz','metadata':{'user_id':<userB_id>,'app_id':'sleep'}}}} (no signature header — backend should accept since whsec is empty, but log warning). First call inserts library_purchases. Second identical call must NOT insert again (no duplicate row for that user_id/app_id pair).\n7. After webhook (step 6), GET /api/library/pricing as user B → sleep.purchased=true.\n8. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'}. Then user C → POST /api/payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → amount must be 500 (50% off 999 cents = 499.5 → rounded), effective_price 4.995 or 5.0 (tolerance ±0.01).\n9. GET /api/payments/return?status=success&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 HTML containing 'xpconfidence://payments/return' and the session_id.\n10. GET /api/payments/return?status=cancel&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 HTML with 'cancelled' wording.\n11. Sanity: existing /library/pricing, /library/ratings, /admin/reports endpoints still work.\n\nNote: skip step 8's edge case if Stripe rejects unit_amount=499 due to minimum currency thresholds — in that case just verify amount<999.\nMark task working:true if all assertions pass."

          comment: "REGRESSION CHECK (2026-05-08, alongside PaymentIntent test run /app/stripe_pi_test.py) — Stripe Checkout integration STILL working. Confirmed via S9 regression block: fresh user POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with session_id='cs_test_a1r276bi4mxHqz62bNIx3OyCxsRcrUe8rHM8eawttJiBCxsMgIHtQW0Jno' and checkout_url starting 'https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_…'. Backend log confirms real Stripe API hit (POST /v1/checkout/sessions response_code=200). NOTE: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_wzpkmxkPB7xTYkERRN2QYy51vv6MXUW3 is now ACTIVE in /app/backend/.env — the webhook handler at server.py L7641-7646 now enforces signature verification on EVERY request. Un-signed POST /payments/webhook now returns 401 'Invalid signature' (it returned 200 in the prior un-configured-whsec test run). This is the CORRECT production behaviour — the previous re-test that posted webhook events without a signature header would now 401, which is expected. Real Stripe-delivered webhooks (signed with the whsec) will continue to be processed normally. No regression."
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Implemented at server.py around L7400 — 4 new endpoints: POST /payments/create-checkout (creates Stripe Checkout Session using server-side pricing), POST /payments/webhook (idempotent purchase recording on checkout.session.completed), GET /payments/session/{id}/verify (frontend fallback), GET /payments/return (HTML page that deep-links into the Expo app via xpconfidence://). Frontend BuyAppModal updated to call /payments/create-checkout, show a premium 'Redirecting to secure checkout…' overlay (spinner + brand + Stripe shield), then Linking.openURL(checkout_url). Frontend StripeReturnHandler subscribes to deep links and polls /payments/session/{id}/verify on return so OWNED state finalises even if webhook hasn't been registered yet."
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "RE-TEST PASS (2026-05-08, /app/stripe_webhook_retest.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api) — 27/27 assertions PASS. Main agent's `import json` fix at server.py L7 resolved the previously broken POST /payments/webhook flow. Step 6 (the only step that previously failed) is now fully green:\n\n  ✅ [6a] Admin login + POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200 with pricing.price=9.99, currency='USD', is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [6b] Registered fresh gmail.com user B (Ryan Chen, ryan.chen.webhook+<rand>@gmail.com); JWT immediate; user_id=8ee73e5f-95f4-4e52-89fe-b21ddf234fb7. Initial GET /library/pricing as B → sleep.purchased=false.\n\n  ✅ [6c] POST /api/payments/webhook (no `stripe-signature` header) with body {type:'checkout.session.completed', data.object:{id:'cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f', payment_status:'paid', status:'complete', amount_total:999, currency:'usd', payment_intent:'pi_test_dummy_b', metadata:{user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'}}} → **200 {received:true, type:'checkout.session.completed'}**. Backend log shows '[stripe] webhook received without whsec verification (set STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)' (correct DEV-only warning) followed by '[stripe] purchase recorded user=8ee73e5f-... app=sleep session=cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f'. The previous `Invalid JSON` error is gone — `json` is now imported at module scope (server.py L7).\n\n  ✅ [6d] GET /api/library/pricing as B → 200 with sleep.purchased=true. Direct DB read via Motor confirmed exactly 1 row in db.library_purchases for {user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'} with paid_amount=9.99, paid_currency='USD', stripe_session_id='cs_test_dummy_b_4613a603163f', stripe_payment_intent='pi_test_dummy_b', source='stripe', purchased_at ISO timestamp, _id is uuid (not ObjectId).\n\n  ✅ [6e] Re-POST the EXACT same payload → 200 same body. db.library_purchases row count for (B, sleep) STILL == 1 (no duplicate insert). Verified via Motor: `count_documents({user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'}) == 1` after the duplicate POST.\n\n  ✅ [6e-bonus] Varied the session id to a new 'cs_test_dummy_b_<rand2>' but kept SAME user_id+app_id pair → 200; library_purchases count STILL == 1 (the $or check at server.py L7439-7442 catches the (user_id, app_id) pair and skips the insert even when stripe_session_id differs). The original row's stripe_session_id was NOT overwritten because the existing row already has a non-empty session_id (only empty session_ids get backfilled per L7445). sleep.purchased remains true throughout.\n\n  Webhook idempotency works exactly per spec — single library_purchases row per (user_id, app_id) regardless of how many duplicate webhook deliveries Stripe sends. The composite-key uniqueness is enforced via the existing-record $or query (no DB unique index needed). Marked task working:true, stuck_count:0 (cleared), needs_retesting:false."
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 37/41 assertions in /app/stripe_checkout_test.py (run 2026-05-08 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). 10 of 11 review-plan steps green; STEP 6 (webhook) FAILS because of one CRITICAL backend bug.\n\n  ✅ [S1] Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:''} → 200; returned pricing.price=9.99, is_free=false.\n\n  ✅ [S2] Fresh gmail.com user A (Maya Patel) → POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200. Real Stripe call hit — backend logs show 'Request to Stripe api method=post url=https://api.stripe.com/v1/checkout/sessions response_code=200'. Response body has session_id='cs_test_a1JahmZzpYzkhXw1eFlAPQzWb6hDOoJL2dk1zlUg7YfiuNoUyu4mv777by' (starts cs_test_), checkout_url='https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_…' (starts https://checkout.stripe.com/), amount=999 (exact), currency='USD', effective_price=9.99, app_id='sleep'.\n\n  ✅ [S3] POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'foo'} → 400 with detail=\"Invalid app_id. Must be one of ['sleep', 'challenges', 'spot', 'confidence']\".\n\n  ✅ [S4] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:0, currency:'USD'} → 200 (sleep is_free=true). User A POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 400 with detail='This app is free — no checkout needed.'\n\n  ✅ [S5] Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD'} → 200 (restore). User A POST /library/purchase/sleep → 200 with saved=true (legacy fallback row inserted in library_purchases). Then user A POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 409 with detail='You already own this mini-app.'\n\n  ❌❌❌ [S6 — CRITICAL BUG, BLOCKS PURCHASE FINALISATION] Webhook idempotency BROKEN. Registered fresh user B (Ryan Chen, gmail.com), captured B's user_id from /auth/register (8ab63bab-e0f5-4ea9-bded-584513dd232e). Confirmed B's GET /library/pricing shows sleep.purchased=false. POSTed to /payments/webhook with NO `stripe-signature` header and a perfectly valid JSON body (verified with `python -c 'import json; print(json.loads(body))'` locally). Expected: 200 {received:true, type:'checkout.session.completed'}. Got: **400 {detail:'Invalid JSON'}**. Re-POST: same 400. Direct DB count via Motor confirmed library_purchases for {user_id:B, app_id:'sleep'} stayed at 0 — B's pricing.purchased remained false.\n\n  Verified directly with curl from outside the test runner against the public ingress: `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{...}' https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/payments/webhook` → HTTP/2 400 `{\"detail\":\"Invalid JSON\"}`.\n\n  ROOT CAUSE (server.py L7559): the un-verified-payload branch does `event = json.loads(body.decode(\"utf-8\") or \"{}\")` BUT the `json` module is NOT imported at module scope in server.py. `grep -n '^import json\\|^from json' /app/backend/server.py` returns NOTHING — the only `import json` in the entire file is a LOCAL import on L2123 inside a different function (won't leak out of that scope). So at request-time, `json` is `NameError: name 'json' is not defined`, which the broad `except Exception:` block catches and re-raises as `HTTPException(400, \"Invalid JSON\")`. The error message is misleading — it's not the JSON that's invalid, it's the missing import.\n\n  ONE-LINE FIX: add `import json` to the top of server.py (or inside the webhook handler). After the fix, re-run /app/stripe_checkout_test.py to verify webhook idempotency (single library_purchases row even after duplicate webhook POSTs).\n\n  ✅ [S7] Discount math: admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:3, duration_unit:'days'} → 200. Fresh user C (Aiden Brooks) → POST /payments/create-checkout {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with amount=500 (in spec band 499..500), effective_price=5.0 (in spec band 4.99..5.00). Server rounds 9.99*0.5=4.995 → 5.0 via `round(max(0, eff), 2)` then 5.0*100=500 cents — correct.\n\n  ✅ [S8] GET /payments/return?status=success&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200 with content-type 'text/html; charset=utf-8'; body contains 'xpconfidence://payments/return', 'cs_test_xyz', AND 'Payment received' (the success H1).\n\n  ✅ [S9] GET /payments/return?status=cancel&session_id=cs_test_xyz → 200; body contains 'cancelled' (in 'Payment cancelled' H1 and 'No charge was made' subtext).\n\n  ✅ [S10] GET /payments/session/cs_test_does_not_exist_xyz/verify → 404 with detail='Session not found: Request req_… No such checkout.session: cs_test_does_not_exist_xyz' (real Stripe API was hit and returned 404 resource_missing — confirmed via backend logs).\n\n  ✅ [S11] Regression: GET /library/pricing (user JWT) → 200; GET /library/ratings (user JWT) → 200; GET /admin/reports (admin JWT) → 200. No regressions on the existing surfaces.\n\n  Marked working:false because S6 webhook is non-functional → real Stripe purchases CANNOT be finalised via the webhook path. The /payments/session/{id}/verify fallback DOES work (S10 hit Stripe and returned 404 correctly), so a paying user could still get unlocked via the frontend deep-link verify path, but the server-side webhook is broken until `import json` is added."


      message: "NEW BACKEND BEHAVIOUR to verify: Library+ Mini-App Pricing & Purchases. Endpoints: (1) GET /api/library/pricing → returns pricing for all 4 mini-apps (sleep, challenges, spot, confidence) with per-user `purchased` flag and a list of supported `currencies`. Default state: every app is FREE (price=0, is_free=true, currency=USD, purchase_url=''). (2) POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id} body={price, currency, purchase_url} — Creator-only; sets a price for the app. price=0 makes it free again. Currency must be in SUPPORTED_PRICE_CURRENCIES (USD/EUR/GBP/AUD/CAD/JPY/INR/RON/CHF/BRL). purchase_url must start with http(s):// when non-empty. (3) POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/discount body={percent, duration_value, duration_unit} — Creator-only; sets a time-limited discount. percent=0 clears. duration_unit ∈ days|weeks|months. discount_active=true only while now ∈ [starts_at, ends_at]. effective_price = price * (1 - percent/100) when discount_active. (4) POST /api/library/purchase/{app_id} — records the caller's purchase (idempotent; trust-based after external Ko-fi/Stripe checkout). Free apps return {already_owned:true, is_free:true} no-op. Paid apps insert into library_purchases. Subsequent GET /library/pricing returns purchased=true.\n\nTEST PLAN:\n1. Anonymous GET /api/library/pricing — should return 4 apps all is_free=true, purchased=false, currencies array length 10.\n2. Non-admin user POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99} → 403 'Creator only.'\n3. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:9.99, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'https://ko-fi.com/example'} → 200, returned pricing has price=9.99, currency=USD, is_free=false, effective_price=9.99.\n4. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:5, currency:'XYZ'} → 400.\n5. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:5, currency:'USD', purchase_url:'ftp://bad'} → 400.\n6. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep {price:-1} → 400.\n7. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/foo (invalid app_id) → 400.\n8. Admin POST /api/library/pricing/sleep/discount {percent:50, duration_value:7, duration_unit:'days'} → 200; discount_active=true, discount_percent=50, effective_price≈4.995≈5.0 (round to 2dp). discount_ends_at ~ 7d from now.\n9. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:0} → clears (discount_active=false, effective_price=9.99).\n10. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:50, duration_value:0} → 400.\n11. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:120} → 400.\n12. Admin POST /.../discount {percent:30, duration_value:1, duration_unit:'years'} → 400 (only days/weeks/months).\n13. Non-admin POST /.../discount → 403.\n14. New user A: GET /api/library/pricing → sleep.purchased=false. POST /api/library/purchase/sleep → 200 {saved:true, already_owned:false, is_free:false}. Re-POST → {already_owned:true}. GET /api/library/pricing → sleep.purchased=true.\n15. POST /api/library/purchase on a still-FREE app (e.g. challenges with price=0) → {saved:false, already_owned:true, is_free:true} no-op (no row inserted).\n16. POST /api/library/purchase/foo → 400.\n17. Anonymous user (X-Anonymous-Id) full purchase flow should also work (trust-based per-anon-id).\n18. Admin sets price back to 0 on sleep (price:0) → is_free becomes true again BUT existing user A's purchased flag should still reflect the historical record (purchased=true). [Acceptable either way — please report observed behaviour.]\n19. Regression: existing /library/ratings still works."

All 11 review-plan steps green:\n\n  ✅ [Step 1] Registered fresh gmail.com user A (Maya Patel). POST /api/feedback {rating:5, text:'Love this app, please add dark theme', level_at_submit:3, platform:'ios'} → 200 with body={saved:true, id:'<uuid>'}.\n\n  ✅ [Step 2] Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200 token (len 237). Admin GET /admin/reports → 200 with {reports:list, new_count:int}.\n\n  ✅ [Step 3] Located the row for user A in reports[]. Row keys observed: ['created_at','dismissed_at','excerpt','id','kind','level_at_submit','platform','rating','reason','reported_email','reported_name','reported_user_id','severity','stars','viewed_at'] — all required fields present.\n\n  ✅ [Step 4] new_count >= 1 (got 1) and the user-A row counts toward it (viewed_at is None).\n\n  ✅ [Step 5] Edge-case validation: {rating:0}→400 'rating must be between 1 and 5'; {rating:6}→400 same; {text:'abc'} (missing rating)→400 same. After EACH failed POST, a re-fetch of /admin/reports confirmed the feedback-row count for A stayed at the baseline (1) — no leaks. Confirms the `excerpt = text if text else '(no comment)'` fallback at server.py L6989 and the stars formatter `'★'*n + '☆'*(5-n)`.\n\n  ✅ [Step 7] GET /api/feedback/me as A → 200 with submitted=True and submitted_at='2026-05-08T14:02:38.352216+00:00' (ISO 8601, parseable).\n\n  ✅ [Step 8] 2nd submission: POST /api/feedback {rating:4, text:'Second piece of feedback'} → 200. /admin/reports now has ≥3 feedback rows for A with ratings {3,4,5} all present — no dedupe enforced (each POST inserts a fresh admin_reports row, as designed).\n\n  ✅ [Step 9] Anonymous flow: POST /api/feedback with header X-Anonymous-Id=anon-feedback-<uuid> (no Bearer) {rating:2, text:'Anonymous user feedback'} → 200. /admin/reports now contains a row with reported_email=='', reported_name=='Anonymous', rating=2, excerpt='Anonymous user feedback'. reported_user_id observed as 'anon-anon-feedback-<uuid>' (the get_user_or_legacy convention: 'anon-' prefix + the X-Anonymous-Id value).\n\n  ✅ [Step 10] Regression /admin/reports flow: non-admin GET → 403 with detail 'Admin only.'; POST /admin/reports/{id}/view by admin → 200 {ok:true}; subsequent GET shows that row's viewed_at populated as ISO timestamp ('2026-05-08T14:02:39.902125+00:00') AND new_count decremented exactly by 1 (4→3). POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss by admin → 200 {ok:true}; subsequent GET no longer contains that row (filter dismissed_at:null at server.py L6283 enforced).\n\n  ✅ [Step 11] Sanity smoke (unchanged endpoints): /profile (JWT) → 200; /stats/weekly → 200; /library/ratings → 200.\n\n  No bugs found. The admin_reports notification side-effect on POST /api/feedback works exactly per spec and is correctly skipped on validation failures (try/except wrap in the notify block does NOT swallow errors that originate before the insert — the HTTPException for invalid rating is raised earlier). The 'fresh user gets count=0 everywhere' part of the spec only holds in a clean DB; the implementation is functionally correct (ratings.x.average is a community average shown to everyone).\n\n  ✅ POST /api/library/ratings:\n    A (create sleep=4): 200 saved=true, app_id='sleep', stats.user_rating=4.\n    B (update sleep 4→5): 200, user_rating=5, count UNCHANGED — confirms upsert (composite key user_id+app_id), NOT insert. Follow-up GET confirms count identical.\n    C (different app challenges=3): 200, user_rating=3; GET shows sleep.user_rating still 5, spot/confidence still null.\n    D (multi-user aggregation): registered second gmail.com user; their initial sleep.user_rating=null (cross-user isolation works); they POST {sleep,1} → 200, sleep.count incremented by exactly 1 (2→3 in this run); user1 GET shows sleep.count=3, user1.user_rating still 5 (their own, NOT affected by user2). Average correctly recomputed (3.33 here with 3 raters; would be exactly 3.0 in a clean DB with only the two test users).\n    E (validation): all 5 invalid bodies returned 400 — stars=0, stars=6, stars='abc', invalid app_id, missing stars. Handler uses raw Body(...) dict and validates manually, so it returns 400 (not Pydantic 422).\n    F (anonymous via X-Anonymous-Id): set header, no Authorization → GET 200 (all user_rating=null), POST {sleep,2} → 200 saved, follow-up GET shows sleep.user_rating=2 persisted under that anon id, AND a different X-Anonymous-Id sees null (correct per-anon-id isolation).\n\n  ✅ Regression smoke (fresh JWT user): /profile→200, /stats/weekly→200 days=7, /stats/monthly→200 days=30, /stats/by-area→200 with all 4 areas, /library/catalog as admin (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com)→200, /library/catalog as non-admin→403.\n\n  ✅ MongoDB collection `library_ratings` and document shape {user_id, app_id, stars, updated_at, _id} verified indirectly via correctness — Scenario B's count-unchanged proves composite (user_id, app_id) uniqueness via upsert; Scenario D proves global aggregation; Scenario F proves anonymous user_id derivation works.\n\n  No bugs found. Marked 'Library+ Mini-App Ratings (GET/POST /api/library/ratings)' working:true, needs_retesting:false. The previous task 'Monthly XP stats endpoint + restored stats/by-area + Focus Mode locked-app penalty' was already passing — not re-run per the explicit review request, but a quick smoke of /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area inside this run confirmed they still return 200 with correct shapes."

B sees 2 rows; admin row has name='Admin · Creator' exactly, level=999, total_xp=-1, is_admin=true, is_admin_view=true. B self row has is_admin=false, is_admin_view=false, real level=1. NEW BACKEND in server.py: 9 endpoints — POST /messages/refine (real-time GPT-4o-mini grammar/safety scrub, returns {refined, flagged, severity, reason}; severe → refined='' and admin reported), POST /messages/check-image (GPT-4o-mini vision check before attaching), POST /messages/send (defence-in-depth re-pass before storing; fires Expo push to recipient via https://exp.host/--/api/v2/push/send; severe content blocks send + files admin report), GET /messages/threads (one row per friend with last-message preview + unread count), GET /messages/thread/{friend_id} (full message list, friend-only), POST /messages/read (mark thread read), GET /messages/unread-summary (per-friend unread counts → drives red dots in Friends list), POST /push/register-token (frontend Expo push token registry, multi-device upsert), GET /admin/reports (Creator-only inbox + new_count for bell badge), POST /admin/reports/{id}/view, POST /admin/reports/{id}/dismiss. New collections: messages, push_tokens, admin_reports. NEW FRONTEND: /messages/index.tsx (threads list w/ unread badges, polled every 4s), /messages/[friendId].tsx (chat screen with debounced AI refinement card ABOVE the input — user can ONLY send refined text; severe content shows red BLOCKED state and disables Send; image picker pre-checks safety; polls 3s for new messages), /admin/reports.tsx (Creator-only inbox with severity pills + dismiss long-press, golden theme). Friends/index PlayerCard now shows red dot with unread count next to friend name, fed by api.messagesUnreadSummary on each tab focus. Friend profile detail modal: 'friends' status now shows BOTH a Message button (cyan, primary, pushes /messages/{user_id}) and the existing 'Already Friends' chip below it. Profile screen renders an AdminNotificationBell in the top-right ONLY for is_admin profiles — 40px golden circle bell with red badge that polls /api/admin/reports every 8s while focused; tap pushes /admin/reports. After Allow-Notifications grant, NotificationPermissionPrompt now also calls Notifications.getExpoPushTokenAsync() and POSTs the token to /api/push/register-token so the recipient device can be woken even when the app is closed. New _layout.tsx files added for messages/, admin/, spot/multiplayer/ (expo-router needs these for nested routes to resolve). All web routes confirmed loading (admin/reports, messages list both render). NEEDS BACKEND TESTING: full DM flow (refine → send → thread → read), severe-content blocking, admin report creation/view/dismiss, friends-only gate (403 on stranger), unread summary correctness, push token register idempotency. NOTE: Expo Push only delivers to mounted devices that already registered tokens — first useful test on real device after rebuild."

      message: "Tested all 4 newly-added features against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api via /app/backend_test.py. 57/58 assertions PASS, 1 CRITICAL FAIL.\n\n  ✅ Boost Inventory (27/27): unlock with wrong code→400, correct code 'XP270905W20'→200; claim before unlock→403 boosts_locked; claim after unlock→200 returns {claimed, profile} with inventory growing; activate by inventory_id→200 sets active_boost (multiplier=3, expires_at, type); status filters out activated entries; bogus inventory_id→404; legacy {type} activate path still works.\n\n  ✅ Weekly Leaderboard (13/13): GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 for fresh anon user → 1 self row weekly_xp=0; tz param persists to profile.tz_offset_minutes (verified 330); after completing default task (xp=15), self.weekly_xp=15 confirming xp_events doc was inserted; idempotent across same-day calls; reports[] field present; rows sorted desc by weekly_xp (verified on 2-member LB in report test).\n\n  🚨 Report System (8/9 — CRITICAL FAIL): POST /api/leaderboard/report returns 500 on the FIRST successful submit. Root cause: server.py ~line 3037 returns the freshly-inserted Mongo doc which Motor mutates in-place to add a non-JSON-serializable ObjectId `_id`. Backend log shows: `ValueError: [TypeError(\"'ObjectId' object is not iterable\")]`. The DB insert DOES succeed (next duplicate-report request correctly returns 400). Fix: `doc.pop('_id', None)` before `return {'report': doc}` (or build a fresh return dict). Every other report-system flow works: self-report→400, non-LB-member→400, duplicate-same-week→400, support→200 with supporters_count=2, unsupport→200 with supporters_count=1, GET /friends/leaderboard surfaces the report with reporter_name/reported_name/reason/week_key/supporters_count/viewer_is_reporter all populated correctly.\n\n  ✅ Player Profile (8/8): GET /leaderboard/profile/{B_id}?tz=0 → 200 returning standard player payload + weekly_xp:int + medals:[] + is_flagged_cheater:false; bogus uuid → 404.\n\n  Existing tests not affected: anonymous mode, 200-level XP, un-tick, custom XP cap, tasks API, sleep, auth still verified by prior runs."
    - agent: "main"
      message: "Added four new feature areas to backend (all user-scoped + anon-aware): (1) Boost inventory system — profile now carries boost_inventory[]; new POST /api/boosts/claim (adds to inventory after code unlock), POST /api/boosts/activate now accepts {inventory_id} (preferred) or legacy {type}, marks the entry activated=true with activated_at/expires_at; GET /api/boosts/status returns the un-activated inventory list. Added a new 'double_day' boost-def (2x for 1 day) reserved for leaderboard winners. (2) XP-event logging in complete_task — each task completion now writes a document to xp_events with {user_id, xp, earned_at_utc, tz_offset_minutes, local_week_key}. Profile now stores tz_offset_minutes (updatable via PUT /api/profile). (3) Weekly leaderboard — GET /api/friends/leaderboard?tz=<offset_minutes> returns rows ranked by weekly_xp using EACH player's own local Mon-Sat window (per-user timezone: players in different timezones each run their own 12:00-to-12:00 Sunday boundary). On the viewer's local Sunday, we compute the winner of the preceding Mon-Sat window and idempotently insert a medal + push a 'double_day' 2x-XP boost into the winner's boost_inventory via db.leaderboard_medals. (4) Report system — POST /api/leaderboard/report {reported_user_id, reason}; POST/DELETE /api/leaderboard/report/{id}/support; these are stored in db.leaderboard_reports keyed by week_key. If a reported player is about to be declared winner and a strict majority (>= floor(N/2)+1 of leaderboard members including friends + self) have supported the report, their medal is revoked (inserted with revoked=true) and no bonus is granted. (5) New endpoint GET /api/leaderboard/profile/{user_id}?tz=<offset> returns player profile + medals[] + is_flagged_cheater. No existing endpoints were altered in breaking ways (boost/activate still accepts legacy {type} for back-compat). Please test these 5 capabilities + verify existing task/profile/sleep flows still pass."

agent_communication:
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Tested all 4 newly-added/modified backend features against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api via /app/backend_test.py. 37/38 assertions PASS, 0 critical fails. (1) 200-level XP system: /api/levels returns the full table with max_level=200, total_xp_cap=1000000, formula 'cum_xp(L) = round(49.6 * L^1.87)', L1=0, L50=74569 (in 73000-76000 band), L200=996340 (in 990000-1000000 band). (2) Un-tick: complete + uncomplete round-trip works perfectly — 15 XP awarded then refunded, profile rolls back, task shows completed=false. xp_removed field returned. (3) Custom XP cap=20: POST 150→20, 10→10, 20→20; PUT custom 999→20; PUT default 80→80 (unrestricted); fresh user defaults intact at [15,40,10,30,15,20,10,20]. (4) Anonymous mode: no-header→main, two distinct X-Anonymous-Id values give isolated profiles (anon A xp=20 after task complete, anon B xp=0), too-short ID falls back to main, JWT request ignores the header. The single 'failure' in the test output ('1000 XP -> level 5 per /levels table :: level for 1000 XP = 4') is a SPEC TYPO, not a backend bug — the spec said 'with 1000 XP, level should be 5 (since L5 cum_xp=1006)' but mathematically 1000 < 1006 means level 4, which is what the backend correctly returns. Backend logic is correct. No fixes needed."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "Shipped Spot the Object — Multiplayer/Lobby (Phase 2). NEW BACKEND: 7 endpoints under /api/spot/match/* — POST /create (host invites confirmed friends, max 7), GET /list (matches user is in: waiting/active + finished/cancelled <24h), GET /{id} (single match incl. seconds_left + leaderboard), POST /{id}/join, POST /{id}/decline, POST /{id}/start (host only — flips to active, picks SPOT_OBJECTS target, sets ends_at = now+120s), POST /{id}/cancel (host, before start), POST /{id}/capture (re-uses _spot_vision_check; on success increments captures[user_id]; lazy-finalizes if past deadline). Lazy auto-finalize: any read of an active match past ends_at settles it — winner = single highest captures (ties = no winner); winner +5 spot_points, all other joined players -1 each (per design picks 3=B and 4=A). Stores in new collection spot_matches. Friendship sanity-check uses db.friend_requests with status=accepted (not the never-existed db.friendships). NEW FRONTEND: 3 screens — /app/spot/multiplayer/index.tsx (hub w/ CTA + LIVE NOW + PENDING LOBBIES + RECENT RESULTS sections, polled every 4 s), /app/spot/multiplayer/new.tsx (friend picker, max 7, empty state deep-links to /friends), /app/spot/multiplayer/[id].tsx (single screen state machine: lobby → camera+leaderboard active phase → results). Polls every 2 s during waiting/active. Camera uses expo-camera CameraView + takePictureAsync(quality:0.5,base64:true). On Web a placeholder panel shows since CameraView capture isn't reliable in browser. Client API methods added in src/api.ts: spotMatchCreate/list/get/join/decline/start/cancel/capture, all typed via new SpotMatch / SpotMatchPlayer / SpotMatchStatus exports. Entry into the feature: /spot main screen now has a third mode card 'Spot with Friends' (cyan, people icon) that pushes to /spot/multiplayer. NEEDS BACKEND TESTING for the 7 new /api/spot/match/* endpoints — focus on full flow (create → invitee join → host start → 2x captures by different players → lazy-finalize when ends_at passes → reward delta on /api/profile.spot_points) plus auth/scoping (non-invitee 403 on join, non-host 403 on start/cancel) plus regression on existing /spot/object, /spot/check, /spot/complete, /spot/feed."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "Two product asks. (1) ALLOW NOTIFICATIONS prompt on app open: new component src/NotificationPermissionPrompt.tsx renders an in-app modal explaining why we'd like to send notifications, then forwards to expo-notifications requestPermissionsAsync. On grant, schedules the daily motivational push (morning/afternoon/evening/night) via existing scheduleMotivationalNotifications(). Persists 'notif_prompt_v1' flag in AsyncStorage so the prompt is shown at most once per install. Mounted globally inside ImmersiveProvider in app/_layout.tsx. Web is a no-op (push not supported). (2) Samsung Health Connect crash on tap of Connect Samsung Health: root cause was that the AndroidManifest was missing the `androidx.health.ACTION_SHOW_PERMISSIONS_RATIONALE` intent-filter that Health Connect uses to discover the app — which causes a native SecurityException when requestPermission is called, killing the app. Fixed by adding the official `react-native-health-connect` Expo config plugin to app.json plugins (the plugin appends the missing intent-filter to MainActivity at prebuild). Also added READ_HEALTH_DATA_IN_BACKGROUND to the android.permissions list, fixed an icon path typo (./asseyouts/images/icon.png → ./assets/images/icon.png) and bumped versionCode 1010→1011 + version 1.0.10→1.0.11. Hardened src/healthConnect.ts requestPermissions(): now bails out cleanly with a friendly Error message (caught upstream by Alert.alert) on every failure mode — non-Android, native module missing (Expo Go), Health Connect not installed, Health Connect needs update, or initialize() fails — instead of letting the native exception crash the app. Per-permission requests are still wrapped in try/catch and granted permissions are re-checked via getGrantedPermissions() as final fallback. Need backend regression: confirm /api/profile, /api/sleep/profile, /api/sleep/onboarding, /api/sleep/checkin, /api/auth/* still work (only frontend + app.json changed)."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "Fixed P0 admin-account crash: clicking 'Add new goal' (and any text-rendering on web while admin yellow override is active) was throwing 'Failed to set an indexed property [0] on CSSStyleDeclaration'. Root cause: src/adminTextOverride.ts patched Text.render globally and appended `_OVERRIDE_STYLE` as an array to `style`, which react-native-web on web tried to hand to a real DOM CSSStyleDeclaration as numeric-indexed entries. Fix: skip the Text.render patch on Platform.OS==='web' (web already uses the injected `<style>` tag w/ `body.admin-yellow-mode *` selector). Also completed the remaining frontend work for Admin 100k XP unlock: tasks.tsx now reads is_admin from /api/profile and forwards isAdmin to TaskModal; goals.tsx now reads is_admin too and forwards to AddGoalModal where the per-unit XP cap (30/225/900) is replaced by 100000 for the admin and the helper text/pill update accordingly. Verified end-to-end via screenshot tool: admin login → Goals tab → tap '+ New' → 'New Goal' modal opens with NO error and shows 'Creator · max 100000 XP' pill plus 'Creator · Premium+ — goals can award up to 100,000 XP each.' hint. Backend caps were already bypassed for admin (server.py lines 1356/1575) so the form will save at >900 XP. No backend changes in this task."
    - agent: "main"
      message: "Day-Anchor onboarding hardening for app updates. PROBLEM: existing users who already chose timezone & morning-start time were being re-prompted to set them again after an app update if their profile document pre-dated the `onboarding_tz_done` flag (the flag was missing or false even though `timezone` and `day_start_time` were both populated). FIX: (1) backend serialize_profile now derives `onboarding_tz_done` as TRUE whenever both source fields are populated, regardless of the stored flag — protects against any legacy profile that pre-dates the flag (server.py around line 470-480). (2) frontend AuthGate's `missing` check no longer requires the flag — `!p.timezone || !p.day_start_time` is sufficient; if both are present, the day-anchor-setup screen is never shown again (frontend/app/_layout.tsx). (3) one-time idempotent migration `_backfill_onboarding_tz_done_flag` runs at backend startup that backfills `onboarding_tz_done=true` on every profile that already has both fields. Migration ran successfully on backend reload — backend log: '[migrate] Backfilled onboarding_tz_done=true on 13 legacy profile(s).' confirming the migration ran against the live DB. NEW USERS are unaffected: they still see onboarding_tz_done=false (no timezone or day_start_time yet) and are still gated to /day-anchor-setup as before. Need backend regression: confirm /api/profile correctly returns onboarding_tz_done=true for legacy profiles (timezone+day_start_time present, flag previously absent) and false for fresh anon profiles (no timezone). Confirm PUT /profile lock rules still work (cannot change timezone/day_start_time once set, only via /profile/reset)."
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Day-Anchor onboarding hardening regression — 46/46 PASS (/app/day_anchor_regression_test.py, run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api).\n\n  ✅ [1] Fresh anon user is STILL gated: GET /profile → onboarding_tz_done=false (literal False), timezone=null, day_start_time=null. Confirms the new derivation does NOT accidentally mark fresh accounts as completed.\n\n  ✅ [2] PUT {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} → 200; subsequent GET → onboarding_tz_done=true with both source fields persisted.\n\n  ✅ [3] Lock rules intact: PUT {timezone:'Australia/Perth'} on locked → 400 detail.error='tz_locked'; PUT {day_start_time:'08:00'} on locked → 400 detail.error='day_start_locked'; both source values verified unchanged after rejected PUTs.\n\n  ✅ [4] POST /api/profile/reset → 200 clears tz/day_start back to null (and onboarding_tz_done back to false); a follow-up PUT {Sydney, 07:00} succeeds again — flag flips back to true.\n\n  ✅ [5] LEGACY ADMIN PROFILE: POST /auth/login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200 + token; GET /profile → onboarding_tz_done=true, timezone='Australia/Sydney', day_start_time='07:00', is_admin=true. Confirms the startup migration backfill ('[migrate] Backfilled onboarding_tz_done=true on 13 legacy profile(s).' in backend logs) is reflected in live profile data.\n\n  ✅ [6] Tz-aware computations: GET /challenge/today returns 200 with challenge {id,title}; POST /sleep/checkin (after onboarding) → 200 with entry.date matching Sydney-local date (verified via datetime.now(ZoneInfo('Australia/Sydney')).strftime('%Y-%m-%d') — distinct from raw UTC date).\n\n  ✅ [7] Regression sanity: /auth/register (gmail.com domain) returns JWT immediately, /auth/login (correct) → 200, /auth/login (wrong pw) → 401, /profile auth → 200, /tasks complete returns xp_awarded>0, /tasks uncomplete returns xp_removed>0, /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 returns rows[]+reports[]+week_key.\n\n  Conclusion: the day-anchor onboarding hardening is working correctly — legacy users with both source fields are NOT re-prompted (whether through the explicit migration or the on-the-fly serialize_profile derivation), and fresh users without those fields ARE still gated. No critical or minor issues found.\n\n  Note for main agent: backend rejects emails with the example.com domain ('does not accept email') as part of pre-existing email-deliverability validation — this is unrelated to the day-anchor work; used gmail.com for registration to bypass."


    - agent: "main"
      message: "Four UX fixes in one pass. (1) FRIENDS+ 'Last seen on this app': new backend `_touch_last_seen(user_id)` helper called from inside `get_user_or_legacy` writes profile.last_seen_at on every authenticated request, throttled in-process to once per minute to avoid hammering Mongo. `_serialize_player` now exposes last_seen_at and `Player` type in api.ts gained the field. Friends/index PlayerCard renders 'Active less than 1hr ago' / 'Active 1.5 hrs ago' / 'Active 3 days ago' / 'Active a while ago' under the stats row, but ONLY for status='friends' (privacy: non-friends and search results never see another user's last-seen). (2) GUEST-MODE day-anchor double-prompt fix: AuthGate's profile-check effect now resets `anchorChecked=false` BEFORE issuing the new GET /profile, so the routing-decision effect waits for fresh data and never routes on stale `anchorMissing=true`. Was making guests answer the timezone+morning-time pair twice on first launch (router.replace('/') fired the segment-change re-mount with stale state). Single answer now sticks. (3) GOALS '+10' button removed completely from goal cards (across signed-in and guest accounts) — only +/- remain. (4) PROGRESS Weekly XP chart: each non-zero bar now displays the day's XP value above the bar; today's bar uses the cyan accent (so it visibly rises in real time as XP is earned). Added a 4-second poll-while-focused so the chart refreshes itself without leaving the tab. Added a second card 'Weekly XP — Trend' rendering the same data as a polyline graph with circle markers (today highlighted) for the trend view the user requested. Need backend regression: confirm last_seen_at field appears on /api/friends/list and /api/players/* responses, that throttling doesn't suppress writes longer than ~60 s, and that all auth + profile + tasks + goals + leaderboard + spot endpoints still pass."

      message: "last_seen_at + UX-fix regression — 65/68 PASS (/app/last_seen_regression_test.py, run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The 3 failures are all the SAME single cosmetic gap (see below), not a functional break.\n\n  ✅ PRIMARY UX SURFACE (the one users actually see — Friends list 'Last seen X hrs ago'): GET /api/friends/list returns each friend's entry with `last_seen_at` populated as an ISO-8601 string (verified parseable via datetime.fromisoformat). Confirmed in BOTH directions after registering Nina+Oliver, A→B request, B accepts. Same field also surfaces on GET /api/friends/players?q= (search) and GET /api/friends/profile/{user_id} (player detail). _serialize_player(L2660) is correctly returning the new field everywhere it's invoked.\n\n  ✅ THROTTLE: 10 rapid /api/profile calls within ~1s all returned 200; last_seen_at value remained STABLE across all 10 calls (1 distinct value across 10 reads), proving _LAST_SEEN_THROTTLE (60s in-process window) correctly suppresses redundant writes. No 500s, no errors.\n\n  ✅ NO-AUTH FALLBACK: GET /api/profile with neither Bearer nor X-Anonymous-Id → 200; _touch_last_seen('main') runs silently inside get_user_or_legacy without crashing. Legacy 'main' profile is returned normally.\n\n  ✅ CRITICAL-PATH REGRESSION (all green): /auth/register (gmail.com) → 200 with JWT immediately + verified=true; /auth/login correct → 200; /auth/login wrong-pw → 401. PUT /profile {timezone:'Australia/Sydney', day_start_time:'07:00'} on fresh profile → 200 with onboarding_tz_done flipping to true; PUT timezone again → 400 'tz_locked'; PUT day_start_time again → 400 'day_start_locked'; POST /profile/reset → 200 clears both; subsequent PUT works again. POST /tasks/{id}/complete with body {date:today} → 200 returning xp_awarded=15; profile.total_xp grew 0→15; POST /tasks/{id}/uncomplete → 200 returning xp_removed=15; profile.total_xp rolled back to 0. POST /goals (focus_area=fitness, target_value=30, unit='days', xp_reward=30) → 200; PUT /goals/{id} → 200; DELETE /goals/{id} → 200. GET /friends/leaderboard?tz=0 → 200 with rows[]+reports[]+week_key+viewer_is_sunday. GET /spot/object → 200 with {object,challenge_id}; POST /spot/check (empty photo) → 400 (endpoint reachable). Admin login (filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) → 200; admin /profile → 200 with is_admin=true.\n\n  ⚠️ MINOR GAP — `serialize_profile()` (server.py L477-525, used by GET /api/profile) was NOT updated to include `last_seen_at`. _serialize_player() (L2630, used by friends list / search / player detail / leaderboard) WAS updated and works correctly. _touch_last_seen DOES write profile.last_seen_at on every request (verified — friends/list shows fresh ISO timestamps), but the user's OWN profile dict simply omits the field. This does NOT block the actual UX feature (the 'Last seen X hrs ago' label in PlayerCard reads from /friends/list, which works correctly). Optional 1-line fix: add `\"last_seen_at\": prof.get(\"last_seen_at\"),` to serialize_profile around line 524. NOT marking working:false because (a) the UX surface works end-to-end, (b) no 500, (c) no data corruption, (d) throttle + no-auth fallback all work.\n\n  No critical issues. Three frontend-only fixes (guest day-anchor double-prompt, goals +10 removal, progress weekly XP chart with bar labels + polyline trend) were not retested per the review request scope."
    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Spot the Object — MULTIPLAYER LOBBY (Phase 2) — 55/55 PASS (/app/spot_multiplayer_test.py, run 2026-04-28 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). Registered 3 fresh users (Olivia Carter / Marcus Bell / Sophia Reyes via gmail.com), made Host↔Friend1 friends via POST /friends/request {user_id} → POST /friends/accept {user_id} (note: actual backend uses these payload shapes, NOT the {to_user_id}/{request_id, action} shapes mentioned in the review request — adapted accordingly).\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/create: empty friend_ids→400 'Pick at least one friend to invite.'; stranger-only→400 'No confirmed friends in the invite list.'; mixed [Friend1, stranger Friend2]→200 with players=[Host,Friend1] only (stranger filtered via friend_requests status=accepted check at L3814-3822); status='waiting'; target_object=null. Match SHAPE has all 13 expected top-level keys (id, host_id, status, target_object, started_at, ends_at, finished_at, seconds_left, winner_id, players, viewer_role, viewer_captures, created_at) and each player has all 7 keys (user_id, name, avatar_base64, is_host, joined, declined, captures). NOTE: viewer_reward is OMITTED from the dict when status != 'finished' (L3722-3724 wraps it in a conditional) — if frontend types expect it always-present, server.py L3723 should return 0 instead of conditionally including it. Did not flag this as a bug because the field is unused before finalize.\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/list: Host sees the new waiting match. Friend1 sees it (as invitee). Friend2 (stranger, filtered at create) does NOT see it.\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/{id}/join: Friend2 (uninvited)→403; Friend1→200 with match.players[Friend1].joined=true; Friend1 again→idempotent 200 ($addToSet keeps it idempotent).\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/{id}/start: Friend1→403 'Only the host can start the match.'; Host→200 with status='active', target_object='leaf' (random pick from SPOT_OBJECTS), started_at+ends_at populated, seconds_left=120 (in 110-120 range). Host start AGAIN→400 'Match is not in a startable state.'\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/{id}/capture: empty photo→400; >8MB→400; Friend2 (not in match)→403; Host with REAL Flickr JPEG (loremflickr.com, ~20KB) → 200 with shape {detected:bool, confidence:float, can_capture:bool, captures:int, match:{...}}; can_capture invariant `detected AND confidence>=0.55` correctly enforced. In this run target='leaf' and the LLM returned detected=False/confidence=0.0 for that particular Flickr leaf, so captures stayed at 0 (correctly verifies the conditional-increment branch).\n\n  ✅ Auto-finalize timer reality: two GETs spaced 3.5s apart on active match showed seconds_left counted down by ≥2s (eg 119→116). Did not blocking-wait 2 minutes for full lazy-finalize path; review explicitly accepted timer-countdown as sufficient.\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/{id}/cancel: 2nd waiting match. Friend1→403; Host→200 {ok:true}; Host /start on cancelled match→400.\n\n  ✅ /spot/match/{id}/decline: 3rd waiting match. Friend1 /decline→200; Friend1 /list no longer contains it (decline removed Friend1 from invited+joined arrays).\n\n  ✅ REGRESSION on Phase 1: /spot/object→200 {object, challenge_id}; /spot/check (real leaf jpg)→200 with detected/confidence/can_capture shape; /spot/complete success=true→200 with points_delta=1 and /profile.spot_points correctly incremented; /spot/feed→200 entries[]; /spot/random-toggle on→spot_random_enabled=true, off→false.\n\n  No bugs found. All 7 new /api/spot/match/* endpoints + lazy auto-finalize behave per spec. Phase 1 endpoints unaffected. Marked task working:true, needs_retesting:false."


    - agent: "main"
    - agent: "main"
      message: "BUG FIX (P0 crash): 'My Friends' subtab in Friends+ closed/crashed the app the moment any friend tried to render. Root cause in /app/frontend/app/friends/index.tsx: the FriendsTab component (defined as a sibling of FriendsScreen) referenced `unreadByFriend[item.user_id]` inside its `renderItem`, but `unreadByFriend` is state declared in FriendsScreen — NOT a prop on FriendsTab. JS scope resolution → `ReferenceError: unreadByFriend is not defined` the instant FlatList tried to render a friend row, which on native crashes the JS bundle and closes the app (web throws but stays on the route, that's why screenshots earlier didn't catch it). Fix: thread `unreadByFriend` from FriendsScreen → FriendsTab as an explicit prop and updated the FriendsTab type signature accordingly. Verified end-to-end on web preview: tapping Players → Friends → My Friends now renders cleanly (admin has 0 friends so we land on the 'No friends yet' empty state with no console errors). renderItem path is now type-safe — `unreadByFriend` is the destructured prop, not a free variable. No backend changes."

      message: "VERIFIED 3-part UX fix via screenshot tool against live web preview (admin login filip.cojocea122@gmail.com). (1) Friends+ Level Shields: confirmed — every player card in /friends Players tab now shows the blue PremiumShield avatar (with shield-icon Lv pill) instead of letter initials. Admin profiles render with the gold ring + Lv 999 shield. (2) Profile 'Always show navigation bar' toggle: confirmed — settings row appears with cyan layers icon + dynamic helper text ('Auto-hides after 5s. Swipe up from the bottom to bring it back.' when OFF, 'Bottom tab bar is pinned and never auto-hides.' when ON). Toggling flips immersiveEnabled in the persisted ImmersiveProvider context (AsyncStorage key 'immersive_enabled_v1'). (3) Android system nav bar auto-hide: code verified Platform.OS-guarded — enableAndroidImmersive() in src/androidImmersive.ts dynamically imports expo-navigation-bar and is a no-op on web/iOS, so the web preview rendered cleanly without crashing. Reassert on AppState 'active' wired up in app/_layout.tsx. No backend changes; no regression expected."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "FEATURE: Admin-only suspension visual badges. Backend `_serialize_player` now accepts `viewer_is_admin` and conditionally adds `is_currently_suspended` (live, computed from suspended_until via _suspension_state) and `was_suspended_ever` (permanent — never unset, set on every suspend call). These fields are OMITTED entirely for non-admin viewers so regular users CANNOT discover that a player has any moderation history. Threaded `viewer_is_admin = await _is_admin_user(user_id)` through `/friends/players` (search), `/friends/profile/{id}`, `/friends/list`, `/friends/requests`, and `/leaderboard/profile/{id}`. Frontend PlayerCard now renders: (a) **red border** around the entire card while is_currently_suspended (overrides the gold admin-view border), (b) small red **SUSPENDED** pill on the name row, and (c) **permanent red dot** to the LEFT of the name once was_suspended_ever — visible to admin only since backend hides the flags otherwise. Visually verified live with 3 fresh users (clean / past-suspended-then-lifted / currently-suspended): admin search results render exactly as designed — clean has nothing, past has only the red dot, active has dot + red border + SUSPENDED pill."


    - agent: "main"
      message: "BUGS-FIRST BATCH: (1) Points+ tab blank — root cause was `sheet` style using maxHeight 90% with no explicit height, which collapsed to zero on RN Web. Changed to height:'85%'. Live verified: Points+ modal now renders with full 'Boost Your Points' sheet + Bonus Top Up / Available Bonuses tabs + shop (3x/2x/2x) unlock cards. (2) Sleep check-in morning popup — sleep/index.tsx now auto-opens the 'How was your sleep?' modal ONCE per sleep-cycle day after the server exposes show_checkin_prompt=true. Tracks last-shown day in AsyncStorage key 'sleep_checkin_auto_v1'. Banner stays visible until user rates. (3) Samsung Health Connect — getAvailability() is now resilient: falsy/undefined initialize() returns no longer short-circuits to 'not_installed'; getSdkStatus() is the authoritative check wrapped in try/catch; requestPermissions() tries BULK first then falls back to per-permission. (4) Notification permission prompt — now persistently re-prompts ONCE PER DAY via LAST_DISMISSED_KEY throttle; only sets PROMPT_FLAG_KEY to '1' after actual GRANT (not after Allow-but-denied or dismiss).  FEATURES: (A) Admin public display renamed to 'Admin · Creator' everywhere (players list, friends, leaderboard, DM headers, gifts sender_name, chat push senders). (B) Admin pinned to top of /friends/players search results. (C) Unfriend confirmation modal — tap 'Currently Friends' → red 'Are you sure you want to unfriend this player?' card + 'You've been friends for X days' subtitle (friended_at threaded from backend) + 'Unfriend Player' / 'Never mind' buttons. (D) 'Send Message to Creator' button rendered on the Creator's public profile modal for any non-friend user (admin DM bypass already exists, just surfaces the button). (E) Goal +1 XP for daily goals — 30 XP per step on unit=='days' goals (stops at target, refunds on un-tick, no XP for non-daily goals). Live verified via smoke test: +1 tick awarded step_xp_delta=30 on a daily goal; non-daily goal returned no step XP. All verified via screenshots."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "PHASE 1 of Spot-the-Object upgrade COMPLETE: Admin Test & Train AI mode. Backend: new `db.spot_training` collection storing per-object samples[{angle, image_base64, captured_at}]. New endpoints: GET /admin/spot/training/objects (status of all 58 trainable objects), POST /admin/spot/training/start {object_name?} (picks specific or next un-trained, falls back to skipped pool, then random), POST /admin/spot/training/capture {object_name, angle, image_base64} (vision-verified — REJECTED if AI doesn't recognise the object, so training data stays clean), POST /admin/spot/training/skip {object_name} (kept in pool but de-prioritized). All endpoints 403 for non-admin. Object-aware angle prompts: scissors→[closed/open/side/tilted 45], cat→[face/profile/top-down/full body], book→[cover/spine/pages open/tilted], etc — 24 specialised + sensible fallback. Reference samples auto-injected into _spot_vision_check() (up to 3 most-recent unique angles) — few-shot prompting that makes everyone's gameplay AI substantially more reliable at recognising objects the Creator has trained. Live smoke tests: 6/6 pass — objects list returns 58 with progress, start picks 'leaf' first (alphabetical), explicit cat works, skip removes from priority, non-admin returns 403. Frontend: new /spot/train.tsx Creator-only screen wired with progress bar, object name + angle prompt, square camera view, 'This is correct object' / 'Skip this object for now' buttons, completion card with 'Test Next Object' CTA. New 'Test & Train AI' card surfaced on /spot hub gated by profile.is_admin. Visually verified live."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "NEW: Confidence — Dress with Confidence AI photo coach (Library+ → Build Self-Confidence → 'Dress with Confidence'). FOUR new backend endpoints to test under /api/confidence/*: (1) POST /confidence/dress-advice {photo_base64?, message, event_context?, weather_hint?, history?[{role,content}]} → 200 {reply:str (<120 words, GPT-4o-mini Vision via emergentintegrations + EMERGENT_LLM_KEY), entry_id:uuid}. Photo (if any) is downscaled to 640px JPEG q=80 before sending; 320px JPEG q=70 thumbnail is persisted to db.confidence_dress_history. System prompt enforces: ONE specific compliment first, factor weather + event in verdict, frame suggestions positively, end with confidence booster. (2) GET /confidence/dress-history?limit=30 → 200 {items:[{id, message, reply, event_context, weather_hint, thumbnail_base64, has_photo, created_at}, ...]} sorted newest-first, scoped to caller. (3) DELETE /confidence/dress-history/{entry_id} → 200 {deleted:N} where N==1 if caller owned the entry, 0 otherwise (no 404 for cross-user attempts — silent no-op so we don't leak existence). (4) GET /confidence/weather?lat=&lon= → 200 {temperature_c, condition, precipitation_mm, wind_kmh, hint:'15°C, light rain, windy (12 km/h)'-style summary} via open-meteo (no API key needed); 502 on upstream failure. ALL endpoints accept JWT or X-Anonymous-Id. Test plan: register A + B (gmail.com); A POSTs dress-advice (with a small JPEG b64) & receives reply + entry_id; A GETs history → contains the new entry with thumbnail_base64 populated and has_photo=true; A POSTs again with no photo → entry has has_photo=false and thumbnail_base64=null; B GETs history → empty (scoping); B DELETE A's entry_id → {deleted:0} (cross-user); A DELETE entry_id → {deleted:1}; A GET history again → entry gone. Plus weather smoke (lat=51.5,lon=0.1 London) returns finite temperature_c + condition string. Existing /confidence/daily and /confidence/complete should still pass."

    - agent: "main"
      message: "BATCH 2026-05-05: Three backend changes need targeted testing. (1) /api/stats/monthly — new endpoint returns 30 days oldest→newest with shape {days:[{date, day, xp, gifted_xp, tasks}, ...]}. `day` should be day-of-month abbreviation (e.g. '1','15','31') NOT weekday. (2) /api/stats/by-area — was 404'ing in the previous fork because the `@api_router.get('/stats/by-area')` decorator was missing; now restored. Should return {by_area:{social:int, fitness:int, appearance:int, mindset:int}}. Also removed a duplicate `@api_router.get('/stats/weekly')` decorator on the /stats/weekly handler (cosmetic, no behavior change). (3) /api/focus/session — accepts new optional `locked_app_seconds` field (defaults to 0). New XP rule: -15 XP per locked MINUTE (rounded down), capped at -300, applies on BOTH completion AND early-exit. Backwards compat: if locked_app_seconds=0 or omitted, legacy fallback of -2 XP/min on backgrounded_seconds is preserved on early-exit. focus_sessions DB rows now include the new locked_app_seconds field. Test scenarios: (a) POST /focus/session {planned_minutes:25, actual_seconds:1500, backgrounded_seconds:0, locked_app_seconds:0, completed:true, committed_app_count:3} → xp_delta=+5 (25/5), reason='focus_complete'. (b) {planned_minutes:30, actual_seconds:300, backgrounded_seconds:0, locked_app_seconds:240, completed:false, committed_app_count:2} → xp_delta=-60 (4 min × 15), reason='focus_distracted_locked_apps'. (c) {planned_minutes:60, actual_seconds:3600, backgrounded_seconds:0, locked_app_seconds:120, completed:true, committed_app_count:1} → xp_delta = +12 (60/5) - 30 (2 min × 15) = -18, reason='focus_complete_with_penalty'. (d) {planned_minutes:60, actual_seconds:3600, backgrounded_seconds:0, locked_app_seconds:1320, completed:true, ...} → 22 min × 15 = 330 capped at 300, then +12 - 300 = -288, reason='focus_complete_with_penalty'. (e) Legacy iOS fallback: {planned_minutes:30, actual_seconds:600, backgrounded_seconds:120, completed:false, committed_app_count:1} (omit locked_app_seconds) → xp_delta = -4 (2 min × 2 from legacy fallback), reason='focus_distracted'. (f) Validate {planned_minutes:0} → 400 and {planned_minutes:200} → 400. (g) Verify the XP delta is reflected in /profile.total_xp and that on negative deltas total_xp never goes below 0. NO REGRESSION expected on /api/profile, /api/auth/*, /api/tasks/*, /api/goals/*, /api/spot/*. Admin creds for any flow that needs them: filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599."


    - agent: "testing"
      message: "ALL XP earnings on Progress charts — 43/54 PASS via /app/backend_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-08). One BACKEND BUG found, plus two test-spec mismatches (not backend bugs).\n\n  ❌ **CRITICAL — /challenge/complete is missing the `_log_xp_to_charts` call.** Server.py L2607-2661 (regular challenge_complete) awards 30 XP and bumps streak but never writes the synthetic task_log row. As a result, XP earned from completing the daily mini-app challenge does NOT appear on /api/stats/weekly, /api/stats/monthly, or /api/stats/by-area for that day. Only the LATE-answer path (/challenge/past/{id}/answer L2768) calls `_log_xp_to_charts(source='challenge_late', focus_area='mindset')`. **One-line fix**: add `await _log_xp_to_charts(user_id, awarded_xp, source='challenge_complete', focus_area='mindset')` immediately after `await _bump_streak_for_xp(user_id)` at server.py L2647 (inside the `if awarded_xp:` block). This is the only missing call site of the 5 paths the spec requires.\n\n  ✅ **WORKING call sites (4 of 5)**: goal_step (L1820), goal_complete (L1837), focus_session (L7287), challenge_late (L2768), confidence_track (L6043). Each was verified end-to-end:\n    • Focus session: POST /focus/session {25min, completed:true, locked=0} → xp_delta=+5; today.xp 0→5; by-area.mindset=5; task_log row _source='focus_session' xp_awarded=5.\n    • Late challenge: seeded auto_uncompleted=true row, POST /challenge/past/{id}/answer {completed:true,easy} → awarded_xp=30; today.xp=30; _source='challenge_late' row.\n    • Confidence: POST /confidence/complete {track:'social'} → ok=true xp_awarded=15; today.xp=15; _source='confidence_track' row xp_awarded=15.\n    • Goal step + goal complete: POST /goals (unit='days', target=1, focus_area='social', xp_reward=100) then POST /goals/{id}/progress {current_value:1} → both _source='goal_step' (30 XP) and _source='goal_complete' (30 XP, capped from 100) rows present; by-area.social=60.\n    • Gift NOT logged: admin gift 50 XP → today.xp unchanged, today.gifted_xp +=50, ZERO _source rows for that user. Profile total_xp +=50 (gift counts toward total but not chart-xp). Correct segregation.\n    • Regression: regular task complete writes a normal task_log row WITHOUT _source field (L1569-1578); chart correctly bumps; no double-counting.\n\n  ⚠️ Two **test-spec mismatches** (NOT backend bugs):\n    1. Spec scenario 1 said 'goal completion → total_xp 0→100'. Actual: non-admin with unit='days' has GOAL_XP_CAPS['days']=30, so xp_reward=100 is capped to 30 (server.py L1722, _clamp_goal_xp). Furthermore, completing a unit='days' goal in a single tick with target_value=1 ALSO awards step XP (30) BEFORE the completion bonus — so total_xp goes 0→60 (step 30 + complete 30). The chart correctly shows 60 with both _source rows. Backend behaviour is correct; spec assertion was naive.\n    2. Spec scenario 2 (3 ticks of a daily goal in one day) is impossible: backend (correctly) enforces a CYCLE LOCKOUT on unit='days' goals — server.py L1769-1781 returns 429 'cycle_locked' on the 2nd same-day tick. So only 1 step XP row (_source='goal_step', xp_awarded=30, focus_area='fitness') was confirmed in this run. The chart-logging machinery for goal_step is verified working; the '3 ticks in one day' scenario simply can't happen.\n\n  ⚠️ Aggregation scenario 8 fails BECAUSE OF the challenge_complete bug — today.xp summed from focus(5)+challenge(30)+step(30) was 35 instead of 65 because the 30 challenge XP didn't appear on the chart. Once the one-line fix lands, S8 will pass automatically.\n\n  ✅ Cleanup endpoints (S10): /profile, /stats/daily, /stats/weekly, /stats/monthly, /stats/by-area all 200 OK.\n\n  Marked task working:false, needs_retesting:false (waiting on main agent fix). Added to test_plan.current_focus."
