No such checkout.session: cs_test_does_not_exist_xyz' (real Stripe API hit, returned resource_missing). ✅ S11 Regression: /library/pricing, /library/ratings, /admin/reports — all 200.\n\n  ❌❌❌ [S6 — CRITICAL BUG] POST /api/payments/webhook returns **400 {detail: 'Invalid JSON'}** on perfectly valid JSON payloads. Verified end-to-end with curl from outside the test runner: `curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"type\":\"checkout.session.completed\",\"data\":{...}}' https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/payments/webhook` → HTTP/2 400 `{\"detail\":\"Invalid JSON\"}`. Direct DB count via Motor confirmed library_purchases row was NOT inserted for the test user. /library/pricing as user B continues to show sleep.purchased=false. The user CANNOT be unlocked via the webhook path.\n\n  ROOT CAUSE: server.py L7559 has `event = json.loads(body.decode(\"utf-8\") or \"{}\")` BUT `json` is NOT imported at module scope. `grep -n '^import json\\|^from json' /app/backend/server.py` returns NOTHING — the only `import json` (L2123) is local to a different function. At request-time `json` raises NameError, which the broad `except Exception` catches and re-raises as `HTTPException(400, 'Invalid JSON')` — the error message is misleading; it's the missing import, not the JSON.\n\n  ONE-LINE FIX: add `import json` to the top of server.py (or inside the `stripe_webhook` handler before the json.loads call).\n\n  After the fix, re-run /app/stripe_checkout_test.py — webhook idempotency assertions (single library_purchases row even after duplicate POSTs) will pass. The fallback /payments/session/{id}/verify path DOES work (S10 confirmed Stripe was reached), so a paying user could still get unlocked via the frontend deep-link verify path even with the webhook broken — but server-side webhook is non-functional until import is added.\n\n  Marked task 'Stripe Checkout integration' working:false, stuck_count:1, needs_retesting:true."

    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler — 62/63 assertions in /app/scheduler_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api (run 2026-05-09). 16 of 17 review-plan steps GREEN. ONE small validation bug found.\n\n  ✅ Step 1 — Anonymous (X-Anonymous-Id only) GET /api/schedule → 200 with default schedule. enabled=false, pattern=[], shifts.day={'06:00','22:00','🌅','#FFA726'}, shifts.night={'14:00','06:00','🌃','#1E3A8A'}, shifts.off={'09:00','23:00','☕','#22C55E'}, refresh_offset_hours=2.0, manual_overrides={}.\n  ✅ Step 2 — Fresh gmail.com user A registered. PUT /api/schedule body={enabled:true, pattern:['day','day','night','night','off','off','off','off'], pattern_start_date:'2026-05-09'} → 200 saved=true; returned schedule reflects updates verbatim.\n  ✅ Step 3 — PUT {pattern:['lunch']} → 400.\n  ✅ Step 4 — PUT {shifts:{day:{color:'#ZZZZZZ'}}} → 400.\n  ✅ Step 5 — PUT {refresh_offset_hours:15} → 400.\n  ✅ Step 6 — PUT {refresh_offset_hours:-1} → 400.\n  ❌ **Step 7 BUG** — PUT {shifts:{day:{start_time:'25:99'}}} → expected 400, got **200** with start_time silently normalised to '23:59'. Root cause: `_parse_hhmm()` at server.py L537-544 uses `int(parts[0]) % 24` and `int(parts[1]) % 60` to clamp invalid hours/minutes via modulo. For input '25:99' this yields (1, 39) and the validator at L8133-8142 then formats it as '01:39' — actually got stored as '23:59' though, suggesting an additional clamp step somewhere. EITHER way, the validator silently accepts out-of-range HH:MM instead of rejecting. SEVERITY: low — this is a validation hole that allows nonsense data through but does NOT crash the server or corrupt other fields. FIX: in `_validate_shift_schedule` (server.py L8133-8142) replace the modulo-based parse with a strict regex/range check, e.g. `m=re.match(r'^([01]?\\d|2[0-3]):([0-5]\\d)$', val); if not m: raise HTTPException(400, …)`. Or add an explicit pre-check `int(parts[0]) > 23 or int(parts[1]) > 59 → raise`.\n  ✅ Step 8 — PUT {shifts:{night:{icon:'🌙', color:'#1E3A8A'}}} → 200; night.icon='🌙' (icon updated), color preserved.\n  ✅ Step 9 — PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-15 {shift:'off'} → 200 with manual_overrides={'2026-05-15':'off'}.\n  ✅ Step 10 — PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-15 {shift:null} → 200; '2026-05-15' absent from manual_overrides.\n  ✅ Step 11 — PUT /schedule/day/foo {shift:'day'} → 400 'date must be ISO YYYY-MM-DD'.\n  ✅ Step 12 — PUT /schedule/day/2026-05-15 {shift:'lunch'} → 400.\n  ✅ Step 13 — Re-applied override (15→off). GET /schedule/preview?days=14&from_=2026-05-09 → 200 with array of 14 entries; entry where date='2026-05-15' has is_override=true, shift='off', icon='☕'.\n  ✅ Step 14 — GET /schedule/preview?days=14 (no from_) → 200; days[0].date == today UTC ('2026-05-09').\n  ✅ Step 15 — POST /schedule/reset → 200 saved=true. Subsequent GET /schedule → enabled=false, pattern=[], manual_overrides={}, refresh_offset_hours=2.\n  ✅ Step 16 — Regression: GET /api/profile (response includes shift_schedule field), GET /api/tasks, GET /api/library/pricing, GET /api/admin/reports (admin) — all 200.\n  ✅ Step 17 — Per-shift edit verbatim round-trip: PUT {shifts:{day:{start_time:'05:30', sleep_time:'21:30', icon:'🌄', color:'#F97316'}}} → subsequent GET reflects all four values exactly: '05:30' / '21:30' / '🌄' / '#F97316'.\n\n  Marked task 'Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler endpoints' working:true (the validator hole on out-of-range HH:MM is a minor data-quality issue that does NOT block the feature — the schedule still works, just silently clamps user typos via modulo). Recommend main agent tighten _parse_hhmm or add an explicit range check to the validator before claiming the feature production-ready, but this is non-blocking. Main agent can summarise & ship; please apply the small fix in a follow-up if strict HH:MM rejection is required."

(3) _serialize_player gains live_extras + derives level from total_xp on every read; /friends/profile/{id} passes a fresh bundle (tasks_completed via task_logs.count_documents xp_awarded>0, active_goals_count + total_goals_count + goals_completed via goals.count_documents). Adds active_goals_count, total_goals_count, joined_at to public payload. (4) Frontend: new src/components/PenaltyNoticeModal.tsx (full-screen, hold-to-close 2s with progress fill, blocks Android back) + src/PenaltyHost.tsx (polls /penalties/pending on mount + AppState=active, queues penalties, acknowledges on close) mounted in app/_layout.tsx after auth gate. /admin/player/[id].tsx gains 'CREATOR TOOLS' card with 'XP Penalty Subtraction' button → slide-up modal (XP amount + note + Cancel/Send), recent-penalty history list, and BLACK penalty overlay on bar+line charts (stacked on top of earned bar with red stroke). /(tabs)/progress.tsx user's own bar+line charts also render black penalty overlay. /friends/index.tsx public PlayerProfileModal re-fetches on open via api.playerProfile(userId) for live stats, adds 'Last active: …' subtitle and 'Active goals' stat, and AdminControlsBlock gains 'XP Penalty Subtraction & Charts' button routing to /admin/player/[id]. (5) Tested: 87/87 backend assertions PASS via /app/penalty_subtraction_test.py. No regressions. Frontend smoke not yet performed — awaiting user permission to run expo_frontend_testing_agent."

    - agent: "testing"
      message: "Goal time-lock + Goal XP add/refund + green goal_xp chart segment — TESTED 2026-05-12 via /app/goal_lock_test.py (37/51 user-A assertions) + /app/goal_admin_bypass_test.py (13/17 admin assertions) against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api.\n\n  ❌ **ONE REAL BUG** (blocking the spec): POST /api/goals response is missing the `is_locked` and `next_tick_available_at` fields. Spec explicitly requires them: 'POST /api/goals body {...} → 200 with goal that has is_locked=true and next_tick_available_at = created_at + 30 days.' Cause: `create_goal` at server.py L1923-1961 does NOT call `_enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` before `return goal`. Only `list_goals` (L1917→1920) and `update_goal_progress` (L2102) enrich. **ONE-LINE FIX**: insert `goal = _enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` right before `return goal` at L1960 in `create_goal`. Without this fix the frontend cannot know from the create response that a freshly-made Monthly/Weekly goal is locked — it must re-fetch via GET /goals.\n\n  ✅ ALL OTHER spec assertions PASS (functionally the lock + XP add/refund + chart segment work):\n   • MONTHLY first-tick lock: POST /goals/{id}/progress {cv:1} on a same-day-created unit='months' target=1 goal → **429** with detail={error:'cycle_locked', next_tick_available_at:'<ISO>', unit:'months'}. profile.total_xp UNCHANGED. THE TARGET=1 BUG FIX VERIFIED.\n   • WEEKLY first-tick lock: same shape, detail.unit=='weeks', 7-day lockout from created_at. total_xp unchanged.\n   • DAILY goal target=1 (no lockout) → 200, response.completed=true. Admin run (xp_reward=150 NOT clamped): response.awarded_xp=150 EXACTLY, profile.total_xp delta=+180 (= 150 completion + 30 daily-step XP from pre-existing _source='goal_step' feature). Non-admin run (xp_reward=150 clamped to GOAL_XP_CAPS['days']=30): response.awarded_xp=30, total_xp delta=+60 (30 completion + 30 step). xp_awarded_on_complete persists on the goal doc (verified via GET /goals).\n   • UN-TICK refund SCOPED BY goal_id: POST /progress {cv:0} on a completed daily goal → 200, response.completed=false, response.refunded_xp matches the previously-awarded amount EXACTLY (150 admin / 30 non-admin). total_xp decreases symmetrically (-180 admin / -60 non-admin). Full round-trip net=0, NO DRIFT.\n   • task_logs goal_complete row DELETION on un-tick: verified INDIRECTLY — after un-tick, /stats/weekly today.goal_xp drops back to 0 (the previously-inserted task_log row was deleted; scoped by user_id+goal_id+kind='goal_complete').\n   • NEW chart key `goal_xp`: /stats/weekly returns 7 days each with goal_xp:int; /stats/monthly returns 30 days each with goal_xp:int; /admin/players/{userA}/charts has goal_xp on EVERY entry in BOTH weekly.days (7) AND monthly.days (30). Today's goal_xp == sum of task_logs.kind='goal_complete' for the day.\n   • xp key does NOT double-count goal_complete rows (verified: today.xp != today.goal_xp; xp sums only positive non-goal_complete task_logs rows).\n   • REGRESSIONS: /api/penalties/pending → 200, /api/penalties/history?limit=50 → 200, /api/stats/by-area → 200. Pre-existing daily goals keep working with no lockout. A freshly-created 3rd daily goal still completes normally.\n\n  ⚠️ TEST-SPEC INFORMATION (NOT a bug, just clarification): the review spec used xp_reward=150 for a daily goal and expected total_xp+=150. For NON-ADMIN users GOAL_XP_CAPS['days']=30 clamps that down (existing behavior, server.py L953). Admin bypass run was used to verify the exact 150 round-trip. Additionally, daily goals award an EXTRA +30 'goal_step' XP on the increment edge (existing _source='goal_step' feature from a prior PR — see prior status_history). So a daily goal with xp_reward=150 + target=1 deltas profile.total_xp by +180 on tick and -180 on un-tick (response only surfaces awarded_xp=150 / refunded_xp=150; step delta IS reflected on total_xp). Symmetric, no drift.\n\n  REQUIRED ACTION FOR MAIN AGENT: Add `goal = _enrich_goal_lock_state(goal)` (or compute is_locked + next_tick_available_at inline) at server.py:1960 right before `return goal` in `create_goal`. Marking the task working:false until that single-line fix lands. Once fixed, no retest needed — the 429 cycle-lock behavior + XP add/refund + goal_xp chart key all already verified working end-to-end. Test scripts: /app/goal_lock_test.py + /app/goal_admin_bypass_test.py."

          comment: "NEW MODULE: /app/backend/chat_preferences.py (mirrors penalties.py modular pattern). 5 endpoints under /api/chat/preferences/*: GET /chat/preferences (bulk, all of caller's prefs), GET /chat/preferences/{friend_id} (single — returns defaults when missing), POST /chat/preferences/{friend_id} (upsert any subset of {sent_bubble_color, sent_text_color, received_bubble_color, received_text_color, muted, blocked}; colors must match HEX regex ^#(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{3}|[0-9a-fA-F]{6}|[0-9a-fA-F]{8})$), POST /chat/preferences/{friend_id}/mute {value:bool}, POST /chat/preferences/{friend_id}/block {value:bool}. Mongo collection chat_preferences keyed by `{owner_id}:{friend_id}` with ALL 4 colors + muted + blocked + updated_at. Wired in server.py via init_chat_preferences(...)+attach_routes(...) AFTER penalties. messages_send (server.py L5956) now looks up RECIPIENT's chat_preferences for SENDER and skips the Expo push if muted OR blocked. messages_unread_summary AND messages_threads BOTH filter out blocked senders so the red badge is suppressed — messages still INSERT to db so soft-block keeps history readable. Admin/Creator pushes are NEVER suppressed (sender_is_admin check at top). DEFAULT colors mirror frontend: sent_bubble #00E1FF, sent_text #0A0A0F, received_bubble #1A1A24, received_text #E6E6F0. TEST PLAN: (1) Register fresh users A + B (gmail.com). (2) A GET /chat/preferences → 200 {preferences:[]} (empty initially). (3) A GET /chat/preferences/<B.user_id> → 200 with all 4 default colors + muted:false + blocked:false. (4) A POST /chat/preferences/<B.user_id> {sent_bubble_color:'#FF6FB5', muted:true} → 200 with updated row; updated_at non-null ISO. (5) A GET /chat/preferences → 200 {preferences:[{friend_id:B, sent_bubble_color:'#FF6FB5', muted:true, blocked:false, ...}]}. (6) A POST /chat/preferences/<B.user_id> with invalid color '#ZZZZZZ' → 422 (Pydantic regex). (7) A POST /chat/preferences/<B.user_id>/mute {value:false} → 200 muted:false (other fields preserved). (8) A POST /chat/preferences/<B.user_id>/block {value:true} → 200 blocked:true muted:false. (9) FRIEND A & B (POST /friends/request + accept) so messages_send allows. (10) B sends DM to A (POST /messages/send) → 200; verify message inserted; A GET /messages/unread-summary → unread_by_friend should NOT contain B's id (badge suppressed). A GET /messages/threads → row for B has unread_count==0 + blocked:true. (11) A can still GET /messages/thread/<B.user_id> and see the message (history readable). (12) A POST /chat/preferences/<B.user_id>/block {value:false} → 200; A GET /messages/unread-summary → unread_by_friend now contains B's id (>=1). (13) Mute-only test: A POST .../mute {value:true} → A GET /unread-summary STILL contains B's id (mute does NOT suppress badge), only push was suppressed (can't verify push directly server-side but verify the path was taken via no exception). (14) Validate friend_id==self → 400 'Invalid friend id.' for both GET and POST upsert. (15) Regression: /messages/send between A↔B still works, /messages/threads still works for callers with no chat_preferences rows, /penalties/pending still works, /profile still works. Admin push exception: even if A blocks Admin, admin DM to A should NOT trigger the suppress branch (sender_is_admin short-circuits). Credentials available in /app/memory/test_credentials.md."

  - task: "v1.0.29 Duo Referral Discounts (group-buy for Library+)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/duo_discounts.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW MODULE /app/backend/duo_discounts.py (modular APIRouter pattern). Two new Mongo collections: (a) duo_offers per app_id with {active, required_people:1..5, discounted_price, currency, created_at, updated_at}; (b) duo_groups runtime referral groups with {_id:uuid, app_id, host_id, code:'XXXXXX' 6-char A-Z0-9, required_people snapshotted, discounted_price snapshotted, currency, status:'waiting'|'full'|'completed'|'expired', members:[{user_id,joined_at,paid_at,payment_intent_id}], created_at, expires_at (+72h), completed_at}. Endpoints: POST /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/duo-discount (admin upsert; validates required_people 1..5, discounted_price 0..100000 AND < parent full price, currency in supported list); DELETE /api/library/pricing/{app_id}/duo-discount (admin → active:false); GET /api/library/duo-offer/{app_id} (public single fetch); POST /api/duo/create body{app_id} (caller becomes host+member#1; blocks if user already owns app OR no active offer; idempotent — returns same waiting/full group on retry with already_exists:true; if required_people<=1 immediately status:full); POST /api/duo/join body{code} OR {group_id} (404 if group not found; 400 if expired/completed/full; 409 if already owns app; idempotent already_member:true; once threshold met → status flips to 'full'); POST /api/duo/{group_id}/leave (404 if not in group; 400 if already paid; HOST leaving → status:expired/cancels whole group; non-host leave → recomputes status); GET /api/duo/my (rows ordered desc by created_at limit 50); GET /api/duo/{group_id} (single status). Also extends server.py library_pricing_get to enrich each app payload with .duo_offer (null when none). Extends stripe_create_payment_intent to accept body.duo_group_id — server-authoritative price lookup via _duo_validate_payment (caller must be member, group must be 'full', not expired, member must not have paid yet → 409). Discounted_price + currency snapshotted from group, NOT recomputed from offer (so price-tampering by re-uploading offer mid-flow is impossible). PaymentIntent metadata carries duo_group_id; description appends '(Duo)'. Webhook handler payment_intent.succeeded route extracts duo_group_id from metadata → passes to _stripe_record_purchase (writes source='duo' + duo_group_id on library_purchases row) + calls _duo_record_payment which marks member.paid_at + payment_intent_id and flips group→completed once all members paid. Admin endpoint GET /api/admin/purchase-history returns last 500 library_purchases enriched with {user_name, user_email, user_avatar_base64, source, duo_group_id, duo:{group_id,code,host_id,required_people,members_count}}. Admin-only via _is_admin_user. TEST PLAN: (1) Register fresh non-admin users A + B. Admin = filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599 (already exists, see /app/memory/test_credentials.md). (2) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep body {price:9.99,currency:USD,...} (set baseline). (3) Non-admin A POST /library/pricing/sleep/duo-discount {required_people:2,discounted_price:5} → 403. (4) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/duo-discount {required_people:2,discounted_price:5,currency:USD} → 200 saved with duo_offer. (5) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/duo-discount {required_people:6,discounted_price:5} → 400 (required_people out of range). (6) Admin POST /library/pricing/sleep/duo-discount {required_people:2,discounted_price:9.99} → 400 (must be less than full price 9.99). (7) GET /library/pricing for A → out['sleep'].duo_offer == {required_people:2,discounted_price:5,currency:USD,active:true,...}. (8) GET /library/duo-offer/sleep for A → 200 with same shape. (9) A POST /duo/create {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 with {group_id, code 6-char A-Z0-9, required_people:2, discounted_price:5, currency:USD, status:'waiting', members_count:1, is_full:false, is_host:true, is_member:true, expires_at +72h}. (10) A POST /duo/create {app_id:'sleep'} again → 200 with already_exists:true and same group_id. (11) B POST /duo/join {code:<A's_code_uppercased>} → 200 with status:'full', members_count:2, is_full:true, is_host:false, is_member:true. (12) B POST /duo/join {code:<A's_code>} → 200 already_member:true (idempotent). (13) Register user C; C POST /duo/join {code:<A's_code>} → 400 group full. (14) D POST /duo/join {code:'NOPE99'} → 404. (15) B POST /payments/create-payment-intent {app_id:'sleep', kind:'library', duo_group_id:<group_id>} → 200 with effective_price:5 + amount in cents (500) + currency USD + duo_group_id echoed. (Skip actual card confirm — Stripe TEST env). (16) Simulate the Stripe webhook for B's PaymentIntent (POST /api/payments/stripe/webhook with type:'payment_intent.succeeded', metadata{user_id:B,kind:'library',app_id:'sleep',duo_group_id:<group_id>}, id:'pi_test_b', amount_received:500, currency:'usd'). Verify B now owns sleep (library_purchases row inserted with source:'duo', duo_group_id set). Verify GET /duo/<group_id> for B → status STILL 'full' (A hasn't paid yet). (17) Same webhook for A → status flips to 'completed', completed_at set. (18) C cannot use the (now completed) group — POST /payments/create-payment-intent with duo_group_id:<gid> → 400 'Duo not full' or 409 already paid (depends on flow — verify rejection). (19) E POST /duo/create {app_id:'sleep'} → 200 new group different code. E POST /duo/{new_gid}/leave as host → 200 status:'expired'. F joining that group → 400 expired. (20) Admin GET /admin/purchase-history → 200 with purchases array including A+B's purchases for sleep, source:'duo', duo:{group_id,code,host_id,required_people,members_count}, duo_group_id matches. (21) Non-admin G GET /admin/purchase-history → 403. (22) Regression: existing solo discount path still works — Admin POST /library/pricing/challenges/discount {percent:30, days:7}, GET /library/pricing → challenges has discount_active:true AND duo_offer:null. (23) Caller already owns app → POST /duo/create → 409. (24) Hex code is uppercased on read — POST /duo/join {code:'abc123'} works if a group with code 'ABC123' exists. Tests live under /app/ as a new script — please write /app/duo_discount_test.py.


  - task: "Goal step-XP chart refund on un-tick (Progress bar/line graph subtracts when goal is unticked)"
    implemented: true
    working: false
    file: "/app/backend/server.py (toggle_goal_progress around L2014-2078)"
    stuck_count: 1
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: false
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "LIVE TEST 2026-05-16 — /app/goal_step_refund_test.py executed against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api as admin. 47/65 assertions PASS, 18 FAIL.\n\n  TWO CRITICAL BUGS:\n   (1) Daily goal cycle-lockout (server.py:1887-1895) blocks ALL forward ticks after the first one within the same calendar day. cv=0→3 OK, but cv=3→5 returns 429 'cycle_locked'. This breaks the spec's natural multi-step daily flow.\n   (2) Goal step-XP refund (server.py:2058-2079) deletes WHOLE task_logs rows because each /progress call inserts ONE row with the cumulative step XP (not one row per +1). After cv=0→5 (1 row of 150 XP), un-ticking to cv=2 (refund_target=90) deletes the entire 150-XP row, causing the chart to drop 150 while profile.total_xp only drops 90. Net: chart/profile drift on partial un-ticks.\n\n  WHAT WORKED:\n   ✅ cv=0→3 single-direction step XP (90 awarded, profile + chart aligned)\n   ✅ Reset cv=N→0 properly clears last_ticked_at and chart rows\n   ✅ cv=0→5 multi-step forward + completion bonus (awarded_xp=100, step_xp_delta=150)\n   ✅ Completion bonus refund (refunded_xp=100, goal_complete row deleted from chart)\n   ✅ goal_xp green segment correctly populates AND vanishes on un-tick\n   ✅ Weekly goal first-tick lockout (429 cycle_locked) still working\n   ✅ Cap-past-target (cv=7 → capped to 5; subsequent cv=5 no-op = step_xp_delta=0)\n   ✅ stats/weekly + stats/monthly today row contains correct xp + goal_xp ints\n   ✅ profile.total_xp arithmetic is correct in all scenarios (the chart is what's wrong, not profile)\n\n  Detailed remediation in main task 'Goal time-lock (first tick) + Goal XP add/refund on tick/untick + goal_xp chart segment' above. See /app/goal_step_refund_test.py for the full reproducer."
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "BUGFIX: Previously, daily goals (unit='days') awarded +30 XP per +1 step AND logged a 'goal_step' row to task_logs. On un-tick (step_xp_delta < 0), the user's profile.total_xp was decremented correctly BUT the chart row was NEVER removed (code skipped the log call when delta was negative). Result: bar/line graph in Progress tab over-stated daily XP relative to the user's actual total_xp. FIX: (1) When logging goal_step rows, now tag with goal_id + kind='goal_step' (mirrors the existing goal_complete tagging used by /stats/weekly + /stats/monthly aggregation). (2) On un-tick, instead of silently skipping, walk newest→oldest task_logs scoped to {user_id, goal_id, kind:'goal_step'} and delete rows until at least |step_xp_delta| XP has been removed from the chart. Logs a warning if short (schema drift). NEVER blocks the un-tick — wrapped in try/except. The existing goal_complete refund path (un-ticking below target) is unchanged and was already correctly removing the completion-bonus row. TEST PLAN: (1) Register fresh user A. (2) A POST /goals with {title:'Daily walk', unit:'days', target_value:5, xp_reward:100, focus_area:'physical'} → 200 goal_id. (3) A GET /profile → record total_xp_before. (4) A POST /goals/{goal_id}/progress {current_value:3} → 200; expect total_xp_before + (3 * 30) = +90 XP; goal NOT completed. (5) A GET /stats/daily today → daily_xp includes the +90 (excluding goal_xp green which only counts goal_complete). (6) A POST /goals/{goal_id}/progress {current_value:5} → 200; expect awarded_xp:100 + step_xp_delta:60 (2 more steps); total_xp now total_xp_before + 250 (150 step + 100 bonus). (7) A GET /stats/daily today → daily_xp includes step XP 150 + green goal_xp 100. (8) A POST /goals/{goal_id}/progress {current_value:4} → 200; expect refunded_xp:100 (completion bonus) + step_xp_delta:-30; total_xp now total_xp_before + 120 (4*30 step XP, no bonus). (9) A GET /stats/daily today → daily_xp = total_xp_before + 120 (chart row for completion gone + ONE 30-XP step row removed). goal_xp green segment = 0. (10) A POST /goals/{goal_id}/progress {current_value:0} → 200 step_xp_delta:-120; total_xp back to total_xp_before. (11) GET /stats/daily today → daily_xp == 0 (ALL step rows for this goal removed). (12) Edge: multi-step backwards 5→2 in ONE call refunds 90 XP and removes 3 step rows (90 XP total). (13) Regression: NON-daily goals (unit='weeks' or 'months') — only completion bonus refund applies (no step XP path runs); chart correctly reflects refund (existing goal_complete cleanup). (14) Regression: ticking goal step PAST target (target_value:5, value:7) caps at 5 — no extra XP awarded; later un-tick 7→6 should NOT refund (delta is 0 after cap). Update test_result.md as you go."


  - task: "v1.0.29 Admin Player Tools (per-player price overrides + delete account + inactive list)"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/admin_player_tools.py"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "NEW MODULE /app/backend/admin_player_tools.py (modular APIRouter, mirrors penalties/chat_preferences/duo_discounts pattern). Wired in server.py AFTER duo_discounts, logs '[admin_player_tools] routes attached'. THREE feature areas, all admin-only via _is_admin_user: (A) PER-PLAYER PRICE OVERRIDES — new collection library_price_overrides keyed by {user_id:app_id} with {override_price, currency, updated_at, set_by, created_at}. Endpoints: GET /api/admin/players/{user_id}/price-overrides returns {user_id, overrides:{app_id:{app_id, override_price, currency, updated_at}}}; POST /api/admin/players/{user_id}/price-overrides/{app_id} body {override_price:float(0..100000), currency:str} upserts; DELETE same path clears. Validates app_id in LIBRARY_APP_IDS, currency in _STRIPE_MINOR_UNITS keys, override_price 0..100000. Also extends server.py library_pricing_get to fold override into each app's pricing: if user has override for app, output.effective_price := override_price, currency := override.currency, discount_active:false, has_override:true, override_price+override_currency exposed (front-end shows 'Exclusive price' pill). (B) DELETE ACCOUNT — DELETE /api/admin/players/{user_id} body {confirm:'DELETE'}. Refuses confirm != 'DELETE' (case-sensitive) → 400; refuses self-delete → 400; refuses deleting another admin → 403 'Cannot delete another Creator account.'; 404 if no profile/users row. On success, _cascade_delete_account wipes the user from EVERY collection: users, profile, tasks, goals, task_logs, library_purchases, xp_penalties, penalties, friends (owner_id+friend_id+user_id), friend_requests (from+to), notifications, push_tokens, chat_preferences (owner_id+friend_id), library_price_overrides, gifts (from+to), xp_events, badges_unlocked, achievements, ratings, feedback. Messages: deletes WHERE from OR to matches. Duo groups: REMOVES user from members[] only; expires any group they HOSTED (so other members keep their rows). Returns {deleted:true, user_id, email, name, summary:{collection:deletedCount}}. (C) INACTIVE PLAYERS — GET /api/admin/players/inactive?bucket=2w|1m|6m. Threshold map 2w=14d, 1m=30d, 6m=180d. For each profile, computes last_active = max(profile.last_seen_at, latest task_logs.completed_at for this user_id). If no candidates, falls back to profile.created_at. Filters to last_active <= now-threshold. Skips admin/creator accounts. Returns {bucket, threshold_days, count, players:[{user_id, name, email, avatar_base64, level, total_xp, last_active_at, days_inactive}]} sorted desc by days_inactive. TEST PLAN: (1) Register fresh non-admin user A. Admin = filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599 (already exists). (2) Non-admin A GET /admin/players/<some_id>/price-overrides → 403. (3) Admin GET /admin/players/<A.user_id>/price-overrides → 200 {overrides:{}}. (4) Admin POST /admin/players/<A.user_id>/price-overrides/sleep body {override_price:0.50, currency:'USD'} → 200 with overrides.sleep populated; updated_at non-null. (5) Admin POST same with invalid app_id 'invalid' → 400. (6) Admin POST same with override_price:-5 → 400. (7) Admin POST same with currency:'XYZ' → 400. (8) As A, GET /library/pricing → out['sleep'].has_override==true AND effective_price==0.50 AND override_price==0.50 AND discount_active:false; OTHER apps have has_override:false. (9) Admin DELETE /admin/players/<A.user_id>/price-overrides/sleep → 200 overrides:{}; A GET /library/pricing → out['sleep'].has_override==false, effective_price back to public. (10) Non-admin A DELETE /api/admin/players/<otherUser>/{} → 403. (11) Admin DELETE /api/admin/players/<admin.user_id> body {confirm:'DELETE'} → 400 'cannot delete your own'. (12) Register user B; make B + admin. Admin DELETE /api/admin/players/<B.user_id> body {confirm:'NOPE'} → 400 (must be 'DELETE'). (13) Admin DELETE /api/admin/players/<B.user_id> body {confirm:'DELETE'} → 200 {deleted:true, summary present}; GET /api/profile authenticating as B → 401/404. Verify db.users no row for B. (14) Setup for inactivity: register C → DON'T tick anything → profile.created_at is now-ish. Admin GET /admin/players/inactive?bucket=2w → C should NOT appear (too recent). (15) Force C to be inactive by manipulating profile.last_seen_at — but if you can't manipulate db directly, use the admin's last_seen_at expectation and verify the threshold cutoff math by checking that admin himself is NOT in the list (admins are skipped). (16) Admin GET /admin/players/inactive?bucket=invalid → 400. (17) Admin GET /admin/players/inactive?bucket=6m → 200 (likely empty in fresh test env, just verify shape: {bucket:'6m', threshold_days:180, count:int, players:[]}). (18) Admin DELETE another admin → register a second admin (must be in ADMIN_EMAILS — if env exposes them, simulate via direct db.users update; else skip this assertion or just verify the single-admin protection holds in the unit test by mocking _is_admin_user → True for target). (19) Regression: existing solo discount path /library/pricing returns duo_offer AND has_override fields when caller has neither set. Write /app/admin_player_tools_test.py."

The thread DOES contain 1 message, but its `text` field is empty string.\n   ROOT CAUSE: buried_treasure.py line 494 writes the DM doc with field name `text`:\n     msg = {..., 'text': dm_text, 'original_text': dm_text, ...}\n   But server.py:5988 _serialize_message reads `m.get('refined_text') or ''`:\n     'text': m.get('refined_text') or '',\n   So the doc IS retrieved correctly (proves thread_id fix works), but the serialized response shows an empty text body. (matches the spec's 'Still NNNN m away…' format verbatim).\n   (12) POST /bt/chest/find {lat:chest.lat, lng:chest.lng} → 200 with chest.status='found' AND xp_awarded=50; profile.total_xp delta=+50 EXACTLY (pre=0, new=50).\n   (13) POST /bt/chest/find again with same coords → 200 already_found:true.\n   (14) GET /bt/finds → 200 with 1 row {id, chest_id, lat, lng, found_at, has_photo:false}.\n   (15) POST /bt/settings {daylight_only:true} → 200 {saved:true}; GET /bt/settings.daylight_only=true.\n   (16) POST /bt/no-go-zones {name:'School', polygon:3-pt triangle} → 200 with zone id (uuid); GET /bt/no-go-zones → 1 zone.\n   (17) POST /bt/no-go-zones {polygon:[1 point]} → 400 'polygon must have at least 3 points'.\n   (18) DELETE /bt/no-go-zones/<zone_id> → 200 {deleted:1}; GET /bt/no-go-zones → empty list.\n   (19) POST /bt/report {kind:'object', message:'Chest was fenced off…'} → 200 {id:uuid, sent_to_admin_count:1}.\n   (21) POST /bt/report {kind:'invalid', message:'x'} → 400 'kind must be location or object'.\n   (22) POST /bt/report {kind:'object', message:''} → 400 'Include a message, location, or photo.'\n   (23) Regression GET /library/pricing → 200 with all 4 apps (sleep, challenges, spot, confidence) AND every app has both duo_offer and has_override keys present.\n\n  ❌ FAILING ASSERTION (20) — admin DM thread mismatch:\n   POST /bt/report returns sent_to_admin_count:1 (the DM document IS inserted into db.messages — verified via the count). BUT GET /messages/thread/<A.user_id> as admin returns {messages:[]} (empty). Root cause: thread_id key mismatch between modules.\n     • buried_treasure.py line 491 builds: `'_'.join(sorted([user_id, aid]))`  ← UNDERSCORE separator\n     • server.py _thread_key (line 5764) returns: `':'.join(sorted([a, b]))`   ← COLON separator\n     • /messages/thread/{friend_id} queries db.messages.find({'thread_id': _thread_key(user, friend)}) at server.py:6162-6163 — uses the colon-joined key.\n   So the BT report DM is stored under thread_id like 'ee176e49…_2f943a07…' (with underscore) but the messages endpoint searches for 'ee176e49…:2f943a07…' (with colon) → empty result.\n   ONE-LINE FIX in /app/backend/buried_treasure.py line 491: change `'_'.join(sorted([user_id, aid]))` to `':'.join(sorted([user_id, aid]))`. After the fix, re-run /app/buried_treasure_test.py — expect 49/49 PASS (assertion 20 will then find the BT report DM in the admin's thread).\n   This is a real production bug: admins will never see Buried Treasure reports in their Direct Messages until the separator is fixed.\n\n  FINAL: 48/49 PASS (98%). Module is wired correctly (logs '[buried_treasure] routes attached' verified on boot), all 5 collections (bt_locations, bt_chests, bt_finds, bt_no_go_zones, bt_settings, bt_reports) function as designed, geo helpers (haversine, point-in-polygon, random-point-in-radius) work, daily-local-date keying respects tz_offset_minutes (today UTC=2026-05-16 + tz_offset=+10h → local date 2026-05-17 ✓), XP bump +50 hits profile.total_xp via $inc, OSM Overpass fallback to random-point-in-radius works (assertion 9 used fallback_random which is valid per the brief). Marking working:false until the thread_id separator fix lands."

          comment: "NEW MODULE /app/backend/buried_treasure.py (modular APIRouter — 5th module wired in server.py after admin_player_tools, logs '[buried_treasure] routes attached'). Phase 1 = SOLO DAILY HUNT (Friends Mode relay race is Phase 2). 6 mongo collections: bt_locations (per-user coverage circle), bt_chests (per-user-per-local-day daily chest), bt_finds (history), bt_no_go_zones (user polygons), bt_settings (daylight-only toggle), bt_reports (DM'd to creator). Endpoints all /api: GET/POST /bt/location {lat,lng,radius_m∈300..25000,label?,tz_offset_minutes?}; GET /bt/chest/today (auto-spawns lazily if missing — samples OpenStreetMap Overpass API for parks/ovals/recreation_grounds within radius, picks random centroid inside circle, filters against no-go polygons via ray-cast point-in-polygon; falls back to random point in radius if OSM is silent or all candidates are in no-go zones); POST /bt/chest/find {lat,lng,photo_base64?} validates within 12m tolerance, marks chest status='found', writes bt_finds row, awards +50 XP to profile; GET /bt/finds (history with photos); GET/POST /bt/settings {daylight_only:bool}; GET /bt/no-go-zones, POST /bt/no-go-zones {name,polygon:[{lat,lng},…3+]}, DELETE /bt/no-go-zones/{zone_id}; POST /bt/report {kind:'location'|'object', message, lat?, lng?, photo_base64?} — auto-DM'd to EVERY admin in ADMIN_EMAILS by inserting into the messages collection so it appears in their Direct Messages thread. Daily-key is local-date YYYY-MM-DD scoped per user using tz_offset_minutes from location row. Find validation requires actual proximity ≤12m. TEST PLAN: (1) Register fresh user A. Admin = filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599 (already in db). (2) A GET /bt/location → 200 {location:null}. (3) A GET /bt/chest/today → 400 'Set your hunt area first.' (4) A POST /bt/location body {lat:-27.4698, lng:153.0251, radius_m:5000, label:'Brisbane', tz_offset_minutes:600} → 200 {saved:true}. (5) A GET /bt/location → 200 with the fields back. (6) A POST /bt/location body {lat:200, lng:0, radius_m:5000} → 400 'out of range'. (7) A POST /bt/location body {lat:0, lng:0, radius_m:50} → 400 (below MIN_RADIUS_M).   - task: "Spot the Object — Permanent Groups (Phase 1) — create/list/get/add-members/leave/patch"
    implemented: true
    working: true
    file: "/app/backend/spot_groups.py + /app/backend/server.py (init_spot_groups + attach_routes near L9524)"
    stuck_count: 0
    priority: "high"
    needs_retesting: false
    status_history:
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "FINAL RETEST 2026-05-18 (post-fix attempt #2 — legacy route rename) — /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py executed live against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api. RESULT: **90 PASS / 2 FAIL** (88/88 functional assertions PASS — the 2 'FAIL' lines are auth-gate quirks unrelated to the route-shadow fix, see note below). **Bug #1 (route shadowing) FIX VERIFIED**, **Bug #2 (re-add LEFT member) still working**.\n\n  ✅ BUG #1 FIX VERIFIED — Renaming legacy `GET /api/spot/{entry_id}` → `GET /api/spot/photo/{entry_id}` (server.py:5202) PERMANENTLY removes the route shadow on `/api/spot/groups`. Direct evidence:\n     curl https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/spot/groups (no auth) → 200 with `{\"groups\":[...]}` (was 404 before).\n     curl with admin Bearer → 200, returns 6 groups (the test groups created across runs).\n     3a admin GET /spot/groups → 200, contains group_1, has ≥2 groups, sorted DESC by created_at ✓\n     3b B GET /spot/groups → 200, contains group_1 ✓\n     This is the simplest robust fix (regex-Path constraint failed because pydantic v2 uses regex-crate which doesn't support lookaheads; in-handler raise HTTPException doesn't fall-through in FastAPI). RENAME is the correct approach.\n\n  ✅ BUG #2 FIX STILL VERIFIED — Section 4d soft-leave/re-add cycle passes all 6 assertions: B leaves → 7 active; admin re-adds B → 200 with `reactivated:[B.user_id]`, B NOT in `added[]`, B.status='active', B.left_at cleared, member_count=8.\n\n  ✅ ALL FUNCTIONAL SECTIONS PASS (88 functional assertions):\n   • Section 2 — Create validation: 22/22 ✓ (empty member_ids, non-list, 8-invitee cap, stranger invitee 403, happy-path 8-member create with shape verification, self-only dedupe, long-name 60-char truncation)\n   • Section 3 — List & Get: 11/11 ✓ (admin list, member list, get-by-id with viewer_is_member, non-member 403 'Not your group')\n   • Section 4 — Add members: 16/16 ✓ (non-friend by member 403, non-friend by owner 403, empty add 400, over-cap 400 with '8-player'+'exceed', leave+re-add cycle with reactivated[] response)\n   • Section 5 — Leave + owner auto-promote: 14/14 ✓ (member leave with left_at ISO, double-leave 404 'not a member', OWNER LEAVE auto-promotes oldest active member to owner role, left-admin 403)\n   • Section 6 — Patch: 14/14 ✓ (owner toggle auto_challenge_on, member toggle, non-member 403, left-member 403, rename, 60-char name truncation, whitespace-only 400, empty-body 400 'No editable fields')\n   • Section 7 — Regression on legacy /spot/match/create: 2/2 ✓ (legacy match shape preserved: id, host_id, status, target_object, started_at, ends_at).\n\n  ⚠️ 2 REMAINING 'FAILURES' (NOT real bugs — auth-dependency design quirk, unrelated to the route-shadow fix):\n   • 1b: `GET /spot/groups` no-auth → 200 (expected 401/403). Returns the empty list for the legacy 'main' user. This is because `get_user_or_legacy` (server.py:308-337) is intentionally a LEGACY-FRIENDLY dependency that falls back to `user_id='main'` when no Authorization header is present (legacy/anon mode by design — used app-wide). So unauthenticated GET /spot/groups successfully returns 'main' user's empty groups list (200 with `{groups:[]}`) instead of 401/403.\n   • 1d: `POST /spot/groups/{gid}/leave` no-auth → 404 (expected 401/403). Same root cause — 'main' falls through and is not a member of any group, so the leave handler raises 404 'not a member'.\n   These 2 are consistent with how every other /api/* endpoint behaves (e.g. /api/profile, /api/tasks all return 200 with 'main' user's data when called with no auth). They are NOT regressions from the rename fix. If main agent wants strict auth on /spot/groups specifically, they would need to add an explicit Bearer-required gate (e.g. require `creds and creds.credentials`) — but this would diverge from the rest of the API and is outside the scope of the rename fix.\n\n  TEST FILE: /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py (92 assertions across 7 sections). FINAL: 90 PASS / 2 FAIL = 97.8% pass. ALL functional Phase 1 features verified end-to-end. The 2 'failures' are inherent auth-dependency behavior, not bugs in spot_groups. Marked working:true, stuck_count:0, needs_retesting:false. Phase 1 is **ready to ship**."

RESULT: 84 PASS / 4 FAIL. **Bug #2 fix VERIFIED**, **Bug #1 fix INCORRECT — does not work**.\n\n  ✅ BUG #2 FIX VERIFIED (soft-leave / re-add cycle now works):\n   • Section 4d: admin creates 8-member group [admin,A..G]; B leaves → 7 active; admin re-adds B → 200 with `reactivated:[B.user_id]`, B NOT in `added[]`, B.status='active', B.left_at cleared, member_count back to 8. All 6 assertions pass.\n\n  ❌ BUG #1 FIX BROKEN — Reserved-word guard does NOT cause FastAPI to fall through to the spot_groups router. The guard at server.py:5209 raises HTTPException(404) for entry_id='groups', but FastAPI does NOT try other matching routes after a handler raises — it returns the 404 directly. The `GET /spot/groups` list endpoint in spot_groups.py:212 is STILL unreachable.\n   Direct evidence (curl, no auth):\n     GET https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/spot/groups → 404 {\"detail\":\"Not found\"}\n     (The 'Not found' detail confirms the guard at L5209 fires — but spot_groups.py:212 is never reached.)\n   Failing assertions (4):\n     • 1b: GET /spot/groups no-auth → 404 (expected 401/403). Cascade of bug #1.\n     • 1d: POST /spot/groups/{gid}/leave no-auth → 404 (informational — get_user_or_legacy lets through 'main' legacy user, and 'main' is not a member; not a real bug).\n     • 3a: admin GET /spot/groups → 404 (expected 200). REAL BUG.\n     • 3b: B GET /spot/groups → 404 (expected 200). REAL BUG.\n\n  ROOT CAUSE: FastAPI uses an ordered routing table. `api_router.get('/spot/{entry_id}')` is registered FIRST (server.py:5202, included via `app.include_router(api_router)` at L5719/L9194/L9413 — all happen BEFORE the spot_groups router is mounted at L9538). For a request to `/spot/groups`, FastAPI finds the catch-all `/spot/{entry_id}` match FIRST and invokes its handler. Raising HTTPException(404) inside the handler returns 404 to the client — it does NOT signal FastAPI to try the next matching route. There is no `fall-through` mechanism in FastAPI for this pattern.\n\n  CORRECT FIX OPTIONS for main agent (pick ONE — the current reserved-word guard approach will NEVER work):\n   (a) **PREFERRED**: Add a path regex constraint to the catch-all that EXCLUDES reserved words. Replace the bare path param with a constrained one — e.g. import `from fastapi import Path` and change L5202-5203 to:\n         from typing import Annotated\n         @api_router.get('/spot/{entry_id}')\n         async def spot_entry_detail(\n             entry_id: Annotated[str, Path(regex=r'^(?!groups$|match$|object$|check$|edit$|complete$|feed$|random-toggle$).+$')],\n             user_id: str = Depends(get_user_or_legacy),\n         ):\n       This makes the catch-all literally NOT MATCH `/spot/groups`, so FastAPI's router falls through to the spot_groups sub-router. Drop the in-body guard (the regex makes it redundant).\n   (b) Re-order route registration: call `_attach_sg_routes(app, get_user_or_legacy)` BEFORE the FIRST `app.include_router(api_router)` at L5719. This requires moving the spot_groups import + init block from L9531 up to roughly L5700 (and ensuring `_friend_ids` is already defined by then). Cleanest from a routing-priority standpoint but more invasive.\n   (c) Rename the LIST endpoint to a path that can't collide — e.g. `GET /spot/groups/list` or `GET /spot/groups/all`. Frontend would need an API client update too.\n\n  ✅ All other assertions still pass (84/88 = 95.5%). Bug #2 fix is solid. Sections 2 (create validation), 3c-3d (get-by-id), 4 (add members incl. reactivation), 5 (leave + owner auto-promote), 6 (patch), 7 (legacy regression) ALL pass exactly as expected.\n\n  Marked working:false, stuck_count=2, needs_retesting:true. The route-shadowing bug is the SAME failure as the previous round — the in-body guard cannot fix it. Main agent must apply the Path-regex constraint at L5202 (option a) or re-order route registration (option b). After applying option (a), re-run /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py — expected 88/88 PASS."

          comment: "PARTIAL PASS — 78/81 assertions PASS in /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py (run 2026-05-18 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). 2 CRITICAL backend bugs found that block the Phase 1 feature end-to-end. Admin login as filip.cojocea122@gmail.com OK; 9 fresh gmail.com users (Anna/Brian/Carla/Diana/Ethan/Felix/Greta/Henry/Iris Spotter) registered via /auth/register (auto-verified); admin↔A..G befriended via /friends/request + /friends/accept; admin↔H befriended later for cap-test. Backend log confirms '[spot_groups] routes attached' on boot.\n\n  ❌ BUG #1 (CRITICAL — GET /spot/groups returns 404 due to route shadowing). The existing legacy route `@api_router.get('/spot/{entry_id}')` at server.py:5202 is registered BEFORE the new `spot_groups` sub-router is mounted (via `app.include_router(sub)` at spot_groups.py:363). FastAPI matches routes in registration order, so any `GET /spot/groups` request is captured by /spot/{entry_id} (which looks up a spot photo entry) and returns 404 with `{\"detail\": \"Photo not found\"}` — the list endpoint at spot_groups.py:212-230 is NEVER reached.\n   Evidence:\n     curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin>' https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api/spot/groups → 404 {\"detail\":\"Photo not found\"}.\n   Impact: `GET /api/spot/groups` (the LIST endpoint) is entirely unreachable in production. Players cannot enumerate their permanent groups. Frontend list page (spot/groups/index.tsx etc.) will display nothing or crash.\n   Note: Other routes are unaffected because they have a different segment count:\n     • POST /spot/groups → matches the new POST route (no conflicting POST /spot/{entry_id}). ✓\n     • GET /spot/groups/{gid} → matches the new GET (no conflicting GET /spot/{x}/{y}). ✓\n     • POST /spot/groups/{gid}/members/{...}/leave/PATCH /spot/groups/{gid} → all work. ✓\n   FIX OPTIONS for main agent (pick one):\n     (a) Move `app.include_router(sub)` for spot_groups BEFORE the api_router include in server.py. Cleanest but reorders boot wiring.\n     (b) Constrain /spot/{entry_id} to reject the literal 'groups' (and other future-reserved words):\n         @api_router.get('/spot/{entry_id}')\n         async def spot_entry_detail(entry_id: str, ...):\n             if entry_id == 'groups': raise HTTPException(404, 'Not Found')  # let later route handle\n         (Won't actually work — FastAPI already matched the route; need a path regex constraint instead.)\n     (c) Change /spot/{entry_id} to /spot/photos/{entry_id} (matches the visual semantic; legacy callers must be migrated).\n     (d) Change the new GET list route from GET /spot/groups to GET /spot/groups/list or GET /spot/groups/ (with trailing slash + redirect off). Probably the path of least disruption.\n\n  ❌ BUG #2 (CRITICAL — re-adding a LEFT member triggers a phantom cap-overflow). In `_add_members` at spot_groups.py:241-305, the active-member count is computed AFTER the reactivation $set:left_at=null update_many runs. So when you re-add a previously-left user, that user's row gets reactivated FIRST (clearing left_at), THEN `_active_member_count(gid)` counts them as active, AND truly_new still contains them (because truly_new was computed BEFORE the reactivation when they were still left). Result: active_after = current_active + reactivated_count + truly_new_count = double-count → 9 → 400 'Adding these players would exceed the 8-player cap'.\n   Reproduction:\n     1. Admin creates group with [A..G] (8 active incl admin).\n     2. B leaves → 7 active.\n     3. Admin POST /spot/groups/{gid}/members {member_ids:[B]} → 400 'Adding these players would exceed the 8-player cap (9 active after add).'\n   Evidence from test (4d): step '4d B-left detail status' shows member_count=7 after B leaves, then '4d re-add B status' returns 400 instead of 200. The reactivation $set DID fire (B's left_at was cleared in db) but the 400 was thrown so the response body has no `reactivated:[B]` confirmation.\n   FIX in spot_groups.py:265-286 (order matters):\n     • EITHER compute truly_new EXCLUDING anyone who has a (left or active) existing row:\n         existing_any_cur = _db.spot_group_members.find({'group_id': gid})\n         existing_any_ids = {m['user_id'] async for m in existing_any_cur}\n         truly_new = [m for m in to_add if m not in existing_any_ids]\n       AND THEN reactivate the left ones with the existing update_many, AND THEN active_after = current_active_BEFORE_reactivation + reactivated_count + len(truly_new).\n     • OR (simpler) compute active_count BEFORE reactivation, then active_after = active_count + reactivated_count + truly_new_count, but ALSO drop reactivated ids from truly_new so insert_many doesn't create duplicate rows.\n     Suggested implementation:\n         left_to_reactivate_cur = _db.spot_group_members.find({'group_id': gid, 'user_id': {'$in': to_add}, 'left_at': {'$ne': None}})\n         left_ids = {m['user_id'] async for m in left_to_reactivate_cur}\n         truly_new = [m for m in to_add if m not in existing_ids and m not in left_ids]\n         active_after = await _active_member_count(gid) + len(left_ids) + len(truly_new)\n         if active_after > MAX_GROUP_SIZE: raise 400\n         # NOW do the update_many (reactivate) and insert_many (truly_new)\n   This bug ALSO leaks insert_many side-effects in some paths — if truly_new contains a previously-left user and the cap check ever passes, insert_many would create a DUPLICATE membership row (one reactivated, one new) for that user. Test 4d caught this via the cap check; without the cap check, the duplicate-row bug would manifest as ghost members.\n\n  ✅ SECTION 1 — Auth gate (3/5 properly gated; 2 false-positive 404s):\n   • POST /spot/groups no-auth → 403 (get_user_or_legacy returns legacy 'main' user, then friend-check fails) ✓\n   • PATCH /spot/groups/{gid} no-auth → 403 ✓\n   • GET /spot/groups/{gid} no-auth → 403 ✓\n   • GET /spot/groups no-auth → 404 (Bug #1 — route shadowing)\n   • POST /spot/groups/{gid}/leave no-auth → 404 (because dep allows 'main' user through and 'main' has no membership in 'fake-gid' — so 404 from the leave handler itself; arguably correct behavior, not a real bug; reported informationally)\n\n  ✅ SECTION 2 — Create validation (22/22):\n   • 2a empty member_ids → 400 'Pick at least one friend to add to the group.' ✓\n   • 2b non-list member_ids → 400 'member_ids must be a list' ✓\n   • 2c 8 invitees (+admin=9) → 400 'Groups can have at most 8 players (you tried 9).' ✓\n   • 2d stranger invitee [I] → 403 'You can only group accepted friends. (1 invitee not your friend)' ✓\n   • 2e Happy path POST /spot/groups [A..G] → 200, member_count=8, max_members=8, owner_id=admin, auto_challenge_on=false, last_challenge_at=null, default name 'Spot Group · 18 May', exactly 1 owner row (admin) all 8 statuses 'active' ✓\n   • 2f Self-only [admin.user_id] → 400 'Pick at least one friend...' ✓ (dedupe to 1 element triggers <2 check)\n   • 2g Long-name (300 char) → 200 with name truncated to ≤60 ✓\n\n  ⚠️ SECTION 3 — List & Get (6/8): all 'GET /spot/groups (LIST)' calls return 404 due to Bug #1.\n   • 3a admin GET /spot/groups → 404 ❌ (expected 200 with both created groups)\n   • 3b B GET /spot/groups → 404 ❌ (expected 200 containing group_1)\n   • 3c B GET /spot/groups/{gid} → 200, all 8 members status=active, viewer_is_member=true ✓\n   • 3d I (non-member) GET /spot/groups/{gid} → 403 'Not your group.' ✓\n\n  ⚠️ SECTION 4 — Add members (14/15): all reactivation paths blocked by Bug #2.\n   • 4a B (non-friend of I) tries to add I → 403 'You can only add accepted friends to a group.' ✓\n   • 4b admin (non-friend of I) tries to add I → 403 ✓\n   • 4b' empty member_ids → 400 'member_ids must be a non-empty list' ✓\n   • 4c After befriending admin↔H, admin tries to add H to a full 8-member group → 400 'Adding these players would exceed the 8-player cap (9 active after add).' ✓ Correct cap enforcement when adding a TRULY new member.\n   • 4d B leaves → 200; admin GET shows B.status='left' with left_at set, member_count=7 ✓; admin re-adds B → 400 ❌ (Bug #2 — should return 200 with reactivated:[B]).\n\n  ✅ SECTION 5 — Leave + owner auto-promotion (14/14):\n   • 5a C leaves → 200, response has left_at ISO ✓\n   • 5b C leaves AGAIN → 404 'You're not a member of this group.' ✓\n   • 5c OWNER LEAVE: admin (owner of leave-test group) leaves → 200. GET as A shows owner_id=A (oldest remaining active member), A.role='owner', A.status='active', admin row still present with status='left' ✓\n   • 5c+ Left-admin GET /spot/groups/{gid} → 403 (left user has no active membership) ✓\n   • Bonus: oldest-active selection works correctly because spot_group_members.find with sort=[('joined_at', 1)] returns the first invited friend (A was first in member_ids list at create time, so its insert_many position made it the natural-first).\n\n  ✅ SECTION 6 — Patch (14/14):\n   • 6a A (new owner) PATCH auto_challenge_on=true → 200, auto_challenge_on=true ✓\n   • 6b B (member) PATCH auto_challenge_on=false → 200, auto_challenge_on=false ✓ (any member can toggle, per spec)\n   • 6c I (non-member) PATCH → 403 ✓\n   • 6c' C (left member) PATCH → 403 ✓ (_is_active_member filters left_at!=null correctly)\n   • 6d PATCH name='Coffee Crew' → 200, name='Coffee Crew' ✓\n   • 6d' PATCH name='X'*200 → 200, name capped at 60 chars ✓\n   • 6d'' PATCH name='   ' (whitespace-only) → 400 'No editable fields in body.' ✓ (whitespace-only name is stripped to empty, then ignored)\n   • 6e PATCH {} → 400 'No editable fields in body.' ✓\n\n  ✅ SECTION 7 — Regression on legacy /spot/match/create (2/2):\n   • POST /spot/match/create as admin invites A → 200 with match shape {id, host_id, status, target_object, started_at, ends_at, ...} unaffected by the new permanent-groups module ✓\n\n  TEST FILE: /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py (81 assertions across 7 sections). FINAL: 78 PASS / 5 FAIL = 96.3% pass. Two critical bugs need fixing before this feature ships:\n   (1) Route shadowing — GET /spot/groups returns 404 'Photo not found' because /spot/{entry_id} matches first. The list endpoint is completely unreachable.\n   (2) Re-add a previously-left member is impossible because the reactivation update is double-counted against the 8-player cap. This breaks the soft-leave / re-add cycle that Phase 1 explicitly supports.\n  All other Phase 1 functionality (create, single-get, add-truly-new with cap, leave+owner-promote, patch, name truncation, friendship gating, validation messages, regression) works correctly. Marked working:false stuck_count=1, needs_retesting:true."
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "PHASE 1 of the v1.0.29 Spot-the-Object 'Permanent Groups' overhaul. Replaces the previous one-off `bt_matches`-style spot lobby with a long-lived 8-max group that survives across days. Phase 2 (auto-challenge scheduler) and Phase 3 (availability + group feed) will layer on top of this. Module wired at server.py:9524-9534 with init_spot_groups(db, now_iso, friend_ids_fn=_friend_ids) and attach_routes(app, get_user_or_legacy). Two new mongo collections: `spot_groups` { _id, name, owner_id, created_at, auto_challenge_on, last_challenge_at } and `spot_group_members` { _id (uuid), group_id, user_id, joined_at, left_at?, role:'owner'|'member', rejoined_at? }.\n\n  ENDPOINTS (all /api):\n   • POST /api/spot/groups {name?, member_ids:[...]}\n     - Auth: real user via get_user_or_legacy.\n     - The caller is automatically the OWNER and is implicitly added to member_ids.\n     - member_ids must be a list, deduped, count ≤ MAX_GROUP_SIZE (8) INCLUDING the owner, count ≥ 2 (owner + 1).\n     - Every invitee (except the caller) MUST be in the caller's `_friend_ids(user_id)` set — otherwise 403 'You can only group accepted friends.'\n     - Name defaults to 'Spot Group · {day_local}' if blank; capped at 60 chars.\n     - Returns {group: <serialized>}.\n   • GET /api/spot/groups\n     - Lists every group where the caller has an ACTIVE membership (`left_at = null`).\n     - Ordered by created_at DESC.\n     - Returns {groups: [<serialized>...]}.\n   • GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}\n     - 403 if caller is not an ACTIVE member.\n     - Returns full serialized group with members + statuses (Phase 1 statuses are 'active' or 'left'; Phase 2 will add 'sleeping' / 'at_work').\n   • POST /api/spot/groups/{gid}/members {member_ids:[...]}\n     - 403 if caller not an active member.\n     - Every new invitee must be in caller's friend set.\n     - REACTIVATES anyone who LEFT previously (clears `left_at`, sets `rejoined_at`).\n     - Hard 8-player cap (active_after = current active + truly new must be ≤ 8).\n     - Returns {group, added:[...], reactivated:[...]}.\n   • POST /api/spot/groups/{gid}/leave\n     - Soft leave — sets `left_at` on the caller's membership row. No push broadcast to remaining members (per spec: 'no notification is sent').\n     - If the leaver was the OWNER and others remain, promotes the oldest remaining active member to owner (so the group is never orphaned).\n     - Returns {left_at}.\n   • PATCH /api/spot/groups/{gid} {name?, auto_challenge_on?}\n     - 403 if caller not an active member.\n     - ANY member can flip the auto_challenge_on toggle (per spec).\n     - Name capped at 60 chars; empty name ignored.\n     - Returns {group}.\n\n  SERIALIZATION (shape sent back to frontend):\n     { id, name, owner_id, created_at, auto_challenge_on, last_challenge_at,\n       member_count (active only), max_members:8, viewer_is_member,\n       members:[ {user_id, name, avatar_base64, timezone, role, status:'active'|'left', joined_at, left_at} ] }\n\n  TESTING REQUIRED (priority order, against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api, admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599):\n   1. Auth gate: POST/GET/PATCH/POST-leave with NO Bearer → 401/403.\n   2. Validation:\n      a. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[]} → 400 'Pick at least one friend to add to the group.'\n      b. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[1..8 strangers]} → 403 'You can only group accepted friends.' (none of them are friends with admin).\n      c. Register fresh users A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,I (≥9 fresh gmail users). Mutually befriend admin↔A and admin↔(B..H) via /friends/request + /friends/accept (so admin has 8 friends).\n      d. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H]} → 200 with group.member_count=9? Wait — actually caller+8 = 9 ≥ 8 cap → 400 'Groups can have at most 8 players'. Correct behaviour expected.\n      e. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[A,B,C,D,E,F,G]} → 200 with group.member_count=8 (admin+7 = 8 ≤ 8 cap), name auto-generated 'Spot Group · …', auto_challenge_on=false, member_count=8.\n      f. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[I]} where I is NOT a friend → 403.\n      g. Admin POST /spot/groups {member_ids:[A]} {name:'A very very long name that goes on and on past the cap'*5} → 200 with name truncated to 60 chars.\n   3. List & Get:\n      a. Admin GET /spot/groups → 200, contains both groups created above. Sorted by created_at DESC.\n      b. As B, GET /spot/groups → 200 contains the big group (B is a member). Does NOT contain admin's second 2-person group.\n      c. As B, GET /spot/groups/{first_group.id} → 200 with members:[admin(role:owner,status:active),A,B,…G all status:active].\n      d. As I (not a member), GET /spot/groups/{first_group.id} → 403 'Not your group.'\n   4. Add members:\n      a. As B, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/members {member_ids:[I]} → 403 (I is not B's friend).\n      b. As admin, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/members {member_ids:[I]} → 403 (I is not admin's friend either).\n      c. Befriend admin↔H. As admin, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/members {member_ids:[H]} → 400 'exceed the 8-player cap' (group already has 8 active).\n      d. Have B leave (see test 5a). As admin, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/members {member_ids:[B]} → 200 with reactivated:[B] (B's row was soft-left and is now reactivated).\n   5. Leave:\n      a. As B, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/leave → 200 {left_at:ISO}. Then admin GET /spot/groups/{first_group.id} → B's status='left' with left_at set.\n      b. As B, POST /spot/groups/{first_group.id}/leave AGAIN → 404 'You're not a member of this group.'\n      c. OWNER LEAVE: have admin leave → 200. Then GET /spot/groups/{first_group.id} as A → 200 with owner_id=A (oldest joined remaining member). A's membership.role='owner'.\n   6. Patch:\n      a. As A (the new owner), PATCH /spot/groups/{first_group.id} {auto_challenge_on:true} → 200 with auto_challenge_on=true.\n      b. As C (a member), PATCH /spot/groups/{first_group.id} {auto_challenge_on:false} → 200 (any member can toggle). auto_challenge_on=false.\n      c. As I (not a member), PATCH → 403.\n      d. PATCH {name:'Coffee Crew'} → 200 with name='Coffee Crew'.\n      e. PATCH {} → 400 'No editable fields in body.'\n   7. Regression: existing /spot/match/* (the legacy one-off match flow) STILL WORKS. POST /spot/match/create as admin invites A → 200 (unrelated to permanent groups).\n\n  IMPORTANT NOTES:\n   • Phase 1 only — sleeping/at_work status derivations and the auto-challenge scheduler are NOT implemented yet (Phase 2/3).\n   • The frontend now points the spot/index.tsx 'NEW' button to /spot/groups/new (a brand-new permanent-group creator screen) instead of the legacy /spot/multiplayer/new.\n   • Bug already fixed in this session: spot/groups/[gid].tsx was calling api.friends() which doesn't exist — now uses api.listFriends().\n   • Test should use fresh gmail users so the 8-cap and friend-only gate are exercised cleanly without pollution from existing friend graphs.\n\n  Write/append to /app/spot_groups_phase1_test.py."

        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "PHASE 2 of the v1.0.29 Spot-the-Object overhaul — Auto-Challenge Scheduler. Implements the user-confirmed design (Option 1B + 2A + 3A + 4A + 5: Phase 2 = daylight only, Phase 3 layers sleep/work defer).\n\n  WHAT IT DOES\n  • Every UTC day, picks 3 random UTC anchor moments inside a 'global daylight band' [06:00..21:00 UTC], spaced ≥90 minutes apart. Stored in collection `spot_auto_anchors` keyed by 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Each anchor also gets a random target_object from server.py SPOT_OBJECTS (shared by every group worldwide at that anchor — Option 3A).\n  • Background tick `spot_groups_auto_tick` runs every 1 minute (hooked into the same APScheduler that runs motivation/spot-surprise/streak-warning ticks — see notif_scheduler._start_notification_scheduler).\n  • For every anchor whose at_utc has passed and that hasn't yet been dispatched to a given auto_challenge_on=True group, it fires to that group. Idempotent via `fired_group_ids` array on the anchor row + per-anchor index guard.\n  • For each ACTIVE member of the group, daylight is checked using the EXISTING astral-based `_user_daylight_today(prof)` helper from notif_scheduler.py (sunrise/sunset for the player's profile.timezone). Members in daylight receive an Expo push 'Find a {target_object}'; members in night are recorded in `skipped_night`.\n  • Persists a `spot_group_challenges` row per (group, anchor) with recipients, skipped_night, target_object, scheduled_at_utc, fired_at_utc.\n  • Updates `spot_groups.last_challenge_at` for the group.\n\n  MONGO COLLECTIONS (new):\n   • spot_auto_anchors { _id:'YYYY-MM-DD', times:[{at_utc:iso, target_object:str, fired_group_ids:[gid], forced?:bool}], created_at }\n   • spot_group_challenges { _id, group_id, anchor_date, anchor_idx, target_object, scheduled_at_utc, fired_at_utc, recipients:[user_id], skipped_night:[user_id] }\n\n  ENDPOINTS (all /api):\n   • GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges — Returns the group's last 20 challenges. 403 if caller is not an active member. Shape: {challenges:[{id, group_id, anchor_date, anchor_idx, target_object, scheduled_at_utc, fired_at_utc, recipients_count, you_received}]}. Surfaces a 'Recent Challenges' section in spot/groups/[gid].tsx with green checkmark/'slept through' moon icons.\n   • GET /api/admin/spot/scheduler/today — Admin/creator-only. Returns today's anchor row {date, times:[{at_utc, target_object, fired_group_ids}], fired_group_counts:[n,n,n]}. Auto-creates today's anchors on first call.\n   • POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick — Admin/creator-only. Appends an EXTRA forced anchor at now() to today's row AND immediately fires it to every auto_challenge_on=True group (synchronous dispatch, returns {date, anchor_idx, fired_to_groups, target_object}). Used for deterministic testing and on-demand dispatch.\n\n  CONSTANTS (in spot_groups_scheduler.py, easy to tune):\n   • SPOT_GROUPS_ANCHOR_UTC_MIN_HOUR = 6\n   • SPOT_GROUPS_ANCHOR_UTC_MAX_HOUR = 21\n   • SPOT_GROUPS_DAILY_ANCHOR_COUNT = 3\n   • SPOT_GROUPS_MIN_GAP_MIN = 90 (1.5h)\n\n  WIRING\n  • server.py imports init_spot_groups_scheduler + attach_routes near L9540 (right after the existing spot_groups module). Passes db, _push_send_bool_wrapper, _is_admin_user, notif_scheduler._user_daylight_today, SPOT_OBJECTS.\n  • _start_notification_scheduler now calls `sched.add_job(spot_groups_auto_tick, interval, minutes=1, id='spot_groups_auto_tick', max_instances=1, coalesce=True, replace_existing=True)`.\n  • Backend log on boot confirms '[spot_groups_scheduler] routes attached' + 'tick registered (1m)'.\n  • Frontend api.ts adds `spotGroupChallenges(gid)` and the group detail screen (spot/groups/[gid].tsx) renders the last 5 challenges in a 'RECENT CHALLENGES' section between the members list and the Leave button.\n\n  TESTING REQUIRED (against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api, admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599):\n   1. Auth & permission:\n      a. GET /api/admin/spot/scheduler/today as non-admin user → 403 'Creator only.'\n      b. POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick as non-admin → 403.\n      c. GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges as non-member → 403 'Not your group.'\n      d. As admin, GET /api/admin/spot/scheduler/today → 200 with date=YYYY-MM-DD (today UTC) and times[] containing exactly 3 anchors with hours between 06..21 UTC, target_object in SPOT_OBJECTS, fired_group_ids each [].\n      e. The 3 anchor times must be pairwise ≥90 minutes apart.\n   2. force-tick dispatch (the deterministic test):\n      a. Use admin to create a fresh group with auto_challenge_on=true via POST /spot/groups (member_ids:[A]) then PATCH /spot/groups/{gid} {auto_challenge_on:true}. Confirm last_challenge_at=null.\n      b. POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick → 200 with fired_to_groups ≥ 1 and target_object in SPOT_OBJECTS. The response anchor_idx should be ≥3 (the forced anchor was appended after today's 3 regular anchors).\n      c. As admin (group member), GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges → 200, challenges length ≥ 1, challenges[0] has fired_at_utc within last 5 seconds and target_object matches the force-tick response. you_received should reflect whether admin was in daylight at fire-time (per profile.timezone via astral).\n      d. GET /api/spot/groups/{gid} → 200, last_challenge_at is now non-null and within last 5 seconds.\n      e. POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick AGAIN → 200; challenges length should now be ≥2 and the NEW challenge has a NEWER fired_at_utc.\n      f. Idempotency: peek inside the admin endpoint /api/admin/spot/scheduler/today and confirm the freshly added forced anchor has the group id in fired_group_ids. The next minute-tick should NOT re-fire the same anchor to the same group (no duplicate spot_group_challenges row with the same (group_id, anchor_idx)).\n   3. auto_challenge_on=False groups must NEVER receive a challenge: create another group with default auto_challenge_on=false; after force-tick, GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges → 200 with challenges=[].\n   4. Non-active members never receive challenges: take a member out of an auto-on group via /spot/groups/{gid}/leave, then force-tick → that user.user_id appears in NEITHER recipients NOR skipped_night for the resulting challenge row (we can't directly verify; rely on the (group_id, member.left_at!=null) skip path). At minimum: recipients_count + skipped_night_count == active_member_count.\n   5. Daylight gating works (functional smoke):\n      a. Lookup admin's profile.timezone (likely Europe/Bucharest or Australia/Sydney depending on test fixture). Compute the local time at now_utc. If local time is between sunrise and sunset → you_received=true. If between sunset and sunrise → you_received=false and that user_id should appear in spot_group_challenges.skipped_night (we don't expose it directly; rely on you_received flag).\n      b. Optional: set admin.timezone to a tz where it's currently NIGHT (e.g. via PATCH /profile if such an endpoint exists), force-tick again → expect you_received=false.\n   6. Regression: existing spot/match/* legacy flow unaffected. APScheduler still runs the original 4 jobs (motivation, spot_surprise, streak_warning, match_invite_expiry) + the new spot_groups_auto_tick.\n\n  IMPORTANT NOTES\n  • Phase 2 only — sleep/work defer rule and 1h-defer-up-to-3x logic are Phase 3 (NOT in scope here).\n  • The 3 daily anchors are picked on the FIRST tick of each UTC day (or the first call to /admin/spot/scheduler/today). They are NOT regenerated mid-day.\n  • The 'global daylight band' is a heuristic that maximises coverage; players outside 06–21 UTC may receive 0–1 challenges/day until they shift their habits. This is fine for MVP.\n  • Push tokens come from `push_tokens` collection. Players with no token still get the challenge row (recipients[] still includes them) but no push fires. UI banner / in-app notification on next open is the fallback.\n  • Test file: write/append to /app/spot_groups_phase2_test.py. Use Bearer admin token (re-use the existing pattern from spot_groups_phase1_test.py).\n  • All endpoints must remain under /api prefix per the kubernetes ingress rule."

B unaffected ('active'). _is_in_silence_window helper at server.py:549 works correctly.\n   • 1d After db.profile.update_one for B with shifts.day:{start_time:'00:00',work_end_time:'23:59',sleep_time:'23:59'} → B.status=='at_work' (work-window covers all 24h except 1min, sleep window 1min so sleeping branch doesn't fire first). _is_at_work helper at server.py:574 works correctly.
