          comment: "PHASE 3 backend of the v1.0.29 Spot-the-Object overhaul. Implements the user-confirmed design (1A reuse Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler + 2A per-group defer + 3A live status badges + 4C feed of auto-challenges + responses + 5A 6h auto-minimise on frontend + 6B simple photo viewer on frontend). This task covers the BACKEND ONLY; the frontend group-feed UI lands in a follow-up task.\n\n  WHAT IS ADDED\n  (1) **Live availability status on members.** `_serialize_group` now resolves each ACTIVE member's status to one of `'active' | 'sleeping' | 'at_work' | 'left'` via a new callback `_availability_fn(prof_dict)` wired by server.py:\n      • `'sleeping'` — `_is_in_silence_window(prof, local_now)` returns True (their Adaptive Work-Life Scheduler sleep window).\n      • `'at_work'` — NEW `_is_at_work(prof, local_now)` helper at server.py:608. Returns True ONLY when the current shift has `work_end_time` (HH:MM) set AND `start_time <= local_now < work_end_time` (with wrap-around for night shifts). Existing profiles without `work_end_time` will never trip this — fully backward-compatible.\n      • `'active'` — neither sleeping nor at_work.\n      • `'left'` — soft-left the group (left_at != null) — unchanged.\n     `local_now` derives from `prof.timezone` via zoneinfo, falling back to UTC. `_spot_groups_availability(prof)` is the wrapper that server.py exports to spot_groups (no circular import).\n  (2) **Phase 3 defer rule in the dispatcher.** `spot_groups_scheduler._dispatch_anchor_to_group` now applies the availability gate BEFORE daylight: members in `sleeping` go to `skipped_sleeping`, members `at_work` go to `skipped_work`, members outside daylight go to `skipped_night`. If `recipients` ends up empty (ALL members unavailable), the function defers instead of firing an empty challenge:\n      • Persists to NEW collection `spot_anchor_deferrals` { _id: 'YYYY-MM-DD:idx:gid', group_id, anchor_date, anchor_idx, attempts:[iso], next_try_at, dropped, updated_at }.\n      • Each defer pushes `next_try_at = now + SPOT_GROUPS_DEFER_HOURS (=1h)`.\n      • Hard cap: `SPOT_GROUPS_MAX_DEFERS = 3`. On the 3rd defer the row is marked `dropped=true` and `next_try_at=null`. Future ticks for this (group, anchor) short-circuit.\n      • Constants are module-level in spot_groups_scheduler.py for easy tuning.\n  (3) **Anchor row guard updated.** `_fire_anchor_if_due` no longer always adds the group to `fired_group_ids`: it does so ONLY on a real dispatch (recipients > 0) OR a definitive DROP. Soft defers leave the anchor open so the next tick (after the 1h cooldown) can retry. This is what makes the 'retry up to 3x' actually happen.\n  (4) **Group feed in `GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges`** — same endpoint as Phase 2, but each challenge now carries `responses:[ {id, user_id, photo_base64, taken_at, remaining_seconds} ]` + counters `response_count`, `you_responded`. Responses are inferred by querying `spot_completions` where user_id ∈ active members, success=true, target_object == challenge.target_object, taken_at ≥ challenge.fired_at_utc AND taken_at < the NEXT-newer challenge's fired_at_utc (or open-ended for the latest challenge). Same endpoint also now exposes `skipped_sleeping_count` / `skipped_work_count` / `skipped_night_count` so the UI can later show 'wakes at 07:00' hints (Phase 3 frontend).\n\n  NEW MONGO COLLECTION\n   • spot_anchor_deferrals — see schema above. Indexed by `_id` (synthetic key). No TTL; entries are scoped to one UTC date.\n   • spot_group_challenges — same shape, plus 2 new fields `skipped_sleeping:[user_id]` and `skipped_work:[user_id]` (Phase 2 rows lack these — UI must coerce missing to []).\n\n  NEW EXPORTED API SHAPE (challenges endpoint):\n   {\n     id, group_id, anchor_date, anchor_idx, target_object,\n     scheduled_at_utc, fired_at_utc,\n     recipients_count,                  # (was already there)\n     skipped_sleeping_count,            # NEW\n     skipped_work_count,                # NEW\n     skipped_night_count,               # NEW\n     you_received,                      # (was already there)\n     responses:[ {id, user_id, photo_base64, taken_at, remaining_seconds} ],  # NEW\n     response_count,                    # NEW\n     you_responded,                     # NEW\n   }\n\n  TESTING REQUIRED (against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api, admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599):\n\n  1. **Availability status on members.**\n     a. GET /api/spot/groups/{gid} (existing 'P1 Smoke Group') → 200 with members[].status ∈ {'active','sleeping','at_work','left'}. The admin's status depends on his profile.timezone & shift_schedule. If shift_schedule is disabled (or no work_end_time), status should be 'active' (not sleeping → admin has a non-empty schedule but no work_end_time set yet).\n     b. Members without any shift_schedule (e.g. test users we register fresh) should always come back as 'active'.\n     c. Members with shift_schedule.enabled=true and a sleep_time/start_time window CURRENTLY active should come back as 'sleeping'. (You can patch a test user's profile to force this — POST /profile or whichever PATCH endpoint exists.)\n     d. Members with shift_schedule.enabled=true AND a CUSTOM work_end_time on their current shift, where local_now is between start_time and work_end_time, should come back as 'at_work'. Skip this if you can't easily set work_end_time — the helper just won't trip.\n\n  2. **Defer logic (the big one).**\n     a. Create a fresh group with auto_challenge_on=true with admin + 1 fresh test user (let's call him `Bob`). Befriend admin↔Bob first.\n     b. Patch Bob's profile to set shift_schedule.enabled=true + a shift whose SLEEP window covers RIGHT NOW (e.g. shift_schedule.shifts.day.sleep_time='00:00', start_time='23:59' makes a 23h59m sleep window). Patch admin's profile similarly so BOTH are 'sleeping' right now. Confirm via GET /spot/groups/{gid} that both have status='sleeping'.\n     c. POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick #1 → 200. fired_to_groups should be 0 for this group (because both members are sleeping). Inspect spot_anchor_deferrals — a row for THIS group should exist with attempts=[<iso>] and next_try_at = now+1h.\n     d. POST /api/admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick #2 (immediately) → 200 fired_to_groups=0 again for this group BUT it shouldn't even attempt dispatch — the cooldown should short-circuit (verify via backend log: '[spot-auto.defer] … deferred attempt 1/3').\n     e. Manually push the next_try_at in spot_anchor_deferrals back to (now - 1m) for that group's deferral row (simulating 1h elapsed). force-tick #3 → fired_to_groups=0; attempts=[2 isos] now; next_try_at = now+1h. Repeat one more cycle. After the 3rd defer attempts.length should be 3 and `dropped=true`. force-tick #4 should still see 0 for this group (dropped state short-circuits).\n     f. Confirm GET /spot/groups/{gid}/challenges returns no NEW challenge rows for these forced ticks (only the previously-fired ones, if any).\n     g. Cleanup: unset the sleep override and confirm the next force-tick (with a fresh anchor) fires normally.\n\n  3. **Group feed (responses).**\n     a. Create a fresh group with auto_challenge_on=true with admin + Bob (both 'active' this time). force-tick → a challenge fires with target_object=<obj>.\n     b. As admin, POST /api/spot/complete {target_object:<obj>, success:true, photo_base64:'<small base64>', remaining_seconds:9} → 200. Same as Bob.\n     c. GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges → the latest challenge row should have responses[].length=2, response_count=2, you_responded=true (when fetched as admin), recipients_count=2, skipped_*_count=0.\n     d. Another force-tick fires a SECOND challenge for the same group with a DIFFERENT target. The response window for challenge #1 closes at challenge #2's fired_at_utc. If admin posts another response for target #1 AFTER challenge #2 fires, that response should NOT be attached to challenge #1 (taken_at >= challenge#2.fired_at_utc → falls outside #1's window).\n     e. Non-members (call as user with no membership): GET /api/spot/groups/{gid}/challenges → 403 'Not your group.'\n\n  4. **Regression — Phase 1 + Phase 2 still pass.**\n     a. Phase 1 group create/list/get/leave/patch still work.\n     b. Phase 2 force-tick on a group with all-active members still fires normally (recipients_count > 0).\n     c. The 1-minute APScheduler `spot_groups_auto_tick` still runs (check backend log for the per-minute 'Job spot_groups_auto_tick executed successfully' line).\n\n  Write tests to /app/spot_groups_phase3_test.py. Re-use the Bearer-token auth pattern from phase1/phase2 tests. Skip test 1c/1d if you can't patch shift_schedule via the existing /api/profile endpoint — at minimum exercise 1a, 1b, all of 2, all of 3, and 4."

          agent: "main"
          comment: "PHASE 4 BACKEND — major refactor of Spot Groups per user spec (1C+2A+3A+4A+5A). (full spec preserved in earlier history)"
        - working: true
          agent: "testing"
          comment: "PHASE 4 BACKEND — VERIFIED end-to-end via /app/spot_groups_phase4_test.py (119/119 assertions passing). Suite covers all 6 sections of the test plan against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api with admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com.\n\nResults by section:\n  1. INVITATION LIFECYCLE — POST /spot/groups returns members[].status='pending' for invitees and 'accepted' for creator (pending_count=3, accepted_count=1, all_accepted=false, started=false). Pending invitee CAN GET the group (viewer_status='pending'). POST /accept transitions pending→accepted (idempotent: re-accept returns {no_op:true}). POST /decline soft-leaves (sets left_at + declined=true, member shows status='left', pending_count drops, member_count excludes declined). Non-invitee GET /spot/groups/{gid} → 403, non-invitee /accept → 404, re-decline on already-left → 404.\n  2. START GATING — POST /start with any pending invitee → 400 with detail mentioning 'pending'. After all accept, POST /start → 200 with started=true, started_at set, auto_challenge_on=true (mirrored). Re-start returns {no_op:true}. Min-2 (admin+1 friend) /start works. Empty member_ids on create → 400. Non-member /start → 403.\n  3. PER-MEMBER TOGGLE — POST /notifications {on:false} sets notifications_on=false and member shows status='off'. {on:true} reverts. Non-member → 403, pending invitee → 403, missing 'on' field → 400.\n  4. DISPATCH ELIGIBILITY — Confirmed via /admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick + direct DB inspection of spot_group_challenges: a toggle-OFF member is silently excluded — NOT in recipients[], NOT in skipped_sleeping[], skipped_work[] or skipped_night[]. Challenge row carries round_ends_at_utc, round_seconds=120, resolved=false. GET /spot/groups/{gid}/challenges exposes all new fields: round_ends_at_utc, round_seconds, resolved, winners, losers, xp_per_winner, xp_per_loser, you_won, you_lost.\n  5. ROUND RESOLUTION + XP — /admin/spot/scheduler/resolve-now resolves all unresolved challenges. Winner XP = +5 × #losers; Loser XP = -1 × #winners with floor at 0 (verified — losers with total_xp=0 stayed at 0, not negative). Idempotency confirmed: 2nd resolve-now on an already-resolved challenge leaves winners/losers/xp fields unchanged (resolver short-circuits on resolved=true rows). Edge cases: zero-winner → xp_per_loser=0 (no XP applied to losers); zero-loser → xp_per_winner=0 (no XP applied to winners). The MATHEMATICAL fields xp_per_winner (=5*#losers) and xp_per_loser (=-1*#winners) may still be non-zero on the challenge row but they are NEVER APPLIED when the opposing party is empty — matching spec.\n  6. REGRESSION — Phase 1 list + create still work (new invitees in 'pending' state on Phase 4 created groups); /leave still works on accepted members; PATCH name still works; PATCH auto_challenge_on={False} → 400 'No editable fields' (correctly removed from editable surface); GET /admin/spot/scheduler/today still returns anchors; force-tick still dispatches.\n\nMINOR FIX APPLIED BY TESTING (test-side only, NO BACKEND CODE CHANGE):\n  • Test users register without a timezone and DEFAULT_TZ in notif_scheduler.py is 'Australia/Sydney', which puts them in 'night' at typical CI run times → dispatch defers. The test now sets timezone='UTC' on the admin (preserving + restoring a snapshot of their original profile incl. shift_schedule) and on every freshly-registered user via direct DB. This is an environment harness fix only, not a production bug.\n\nCROSS-TALK NOTE FOR MAIN: force-tick picks ONE target_object per anchor and dispatches it to ALL started groups. If admin is a member of several started test groups, a single /spot/complete on that target counts admin as a WINNER in every group's challenge for that anchor. This is correct production behaviour — the test relaxes per-user XP-delta assertions to '≥ this-challenge's amount' to accommodate. Recommended for production-style tests: use isolated single-group fixtures."

Switch wrapped in TouchableOpacity for web-test reliability.\n     - Recent Challenges card: shows trophy +X XP chip on you_won, remove-circle X XP chip on you_lost (Phase 4 resolved state).\n     - Empty-state copy updated: 'Once the game starts, challenges will appear here. Wait for all invitees to accept...' vs 'No challenges have fired yet today' depending on started.\n     - Photo viewer + 6h auto-minimise from Phase 3 unchanged.\n  • /spot/index.tsx:\n     - REMOVED the legacy Mode-3 'Spot with Friends' card that linked to /spot/multiplayer (per Option 5A).\n     - 'NEW' button on MY GROUPS continues to point to /spot/groups/new (which is now the only multiplayer entry point).\n     - Group row subtitle changed from 'X of 8 active · auto-challenges ON' to 'X of 8 players · <pending|game on|lobby>' driven by Phase 4 fields.\n  • /spot/multiplayer/* (3 legacy files) MOVED to /app/frontend/.archive_spot_multiplayer_<timestamp>/ so the one-off match flow is hidden from expo-router. Files preserved (not rm'd) for forensics.\n\n  VERIFIED VIA SCREENSHOT (/tmp/p4_group_detail.png + /tmp/p4_spot_index.png):\n   - Spot tab no longer has 'Spot with Friends' legacy card.\n   - MY GROUPS list shows new copy.\n   - Group detail shows the Lobby card with Start CTA (group not yet started but all_accepted=true).\n   - Members render with green Active badges + 'Filip · Creator · Owner · You' suffix on viewer's own row.\n   - Recent Challenges include trophy chips (+10 XP) for resolved rounds where viewer won.\n   - No console errors.           comment: "RE-TEST PASS — 61/62 assertions PASS in /app/global_rules_test.py (run 2026-05-16 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The one-line fix at /app/backend/buried_treasure.py L855 in `_load_match_or_404` (`if not m.get('free_for_all') and user_id not in (hider_id, seeker_id): raise 403`) IS WORKING — every previously-failing Rule 6 assertion now passes.\n\n  ✅ RULE 6 (Free-For-All) — 14/15 PASS (1 minor response-shape miss, see below):\n   • 6a Admin invite A → 200, match id returned ✓\n   • 6b A reject → 200, response.free_for_all=true, match.state stays 'pending_accept' after FFA reject ✓\n   • 6c A POST /find after rejecting → 403 with detail containing 'declined' ✓ (rejecter blocked)\n   • 6d Non-friend N POST /find → 403 with detail containing 'friends only' ✓ (friendship gate now reachable thanks to the FFA bypass in _load_match_or_404)\n   • 6e B (friend of admin) POST /find with correct lat/lng → 200 with state='found' ✓\n   • 6e B.total_xp gained +100 (XP_FIND_SEEKER) ✓\n   • 6e admin.total_xp gained +50 (XP_FIND_HIDER) ✓\n   • 6e.m Mongo: bt_matches doc has ffa_won_by=<B.user_id>, state='found' ✓\n   • 6f Late friend C POST /find after B already won → 400/409 (state changed) ✓\n\n  ❌ Minor (cosmetic — non-blocking): The /bt/match/{mid}/find endpoint RESPONSE BODY does not surface `winner_user_id` or `ffa_won_by` fields, even though they ARE stored correctly in Mongo. The data integrity assertion (6e.m) PASSES — the DB doc has ffa_won_by=B.user_id and the XP was correctly awarded. The issue is purely that `_serialize_match()` at buried_treasure.py:392-426 doesn't include these two keys in the output dict. Frontend wanting to display 'Who claimed the treasure' from the find-response alone would need an extra GET /bt/match/{mid} after the find (which also doesn't include these fields). Suggested fix: add `'winner_user_id': m.get('winner_user_id'), 'ffa_won_by': m.get('ffa_won_by')` to the `out` dict in _serialize_match. This is a minor follow-up — does NOT block FFA functionality (data + XP are correct).\n\n  ✅ RULE 1 (Leaderboard L1 gate) — 7/7 PASS (re-verified): Fresh 0-XP friend F hidden from admin's leaderboard, F sees themselves; after F earns 15 XP via /confidence/complete, F becomes visible.\n  ✅ RULE 2 (/spot/object queue) — 5/5 PASS: 80 calls, pool_size detected=57, no duplicates in first cycle, challenge_queues.cycle>=2 after exhaustion.\n  ✅ RULE 3 (per-group queue) — 11/11 PASS: G1+G2 get DIFFERENT targets at same anchor (G1='shoe' vs G2='cat'), 6 consecutive ticks on G1 yield 6 distinct objects (shoe/soft toy/anything green/fridge magnet/door handle/book), independent 'used' lists in Mongo.\n  ✅ RULE 4 (confidence per-user) — 7/7 PASS: U1 vs U2 differ on ALL 3 tracks; U1 same-day re-fetch is memoised; confidence_today_picks memo rows present.\n\n  ✅ REGRESSION: Buried Treasure normal accept flow (invite→accept→state='awaiting_burial', NO free_for_all flag) PASS. Phase 1-4 spot groups (create→accept→start→list_challenges) PASS.\n\n  FINAL: 61 PASS / 1 FAIL (cosmetic). All 5 previously-failing Rule 6 assertions now pass: 6e (B becomes winner + XP awards), 6f (late C blocked), 6d (non-friend blocked with correct error), 6c (rejecter blocked with correct error), 6g (XP deltas verified). The only failure is the response shape miss noted above — backend behaviour & data integrity are fully correct. Marked working:true, stuck_count reset to 0."
        - working: "NA"
          agent: "main"
          comment: "Locks in six user-confirmed rules (1A+2A+3A+4A+5A defaults).\n\n  RULE 1 — Public Leaderboard L1 Gate (server.py L4395 in friends_leaderboard). Players whose total_xp <= 0 are now excluded from /api/friends/leaderboard EXCEPT for the VIEWER themselves (who is always shown to avoid an empty screen for first-timers).\n\n  RULE 2 — Global non-repeat challenge queue (NEW module /app/backend/challenge_queue.py). Exposes `await next_item(db, scope, key, pool_id, full_pool, count=1) -> list[str]`. Per-(scope, key, pool_id) queue stored in collection `challenge_queues` { _id:'{scope}:{key}:{pool_id}', remaining:[…], used:[…], cycle:int, updated_at }. When `remaining` empties, the helper auto-reshuffles the full pool and bumps `cycle`. Module-level helpers `peek()` (debug) and `reset()` (admin) included.\n\n  RULE 3 — Spot the Object queue:\n    a) Solo (/api/spot/object) — pulls via next_item(scope='user', pool_id='spot_solo', full_pool=SPOT_OBJECTS). Falls back to random.choice if the helper raises.\n    b) Group dispatch (spot_groups_scheduler._dispatch_anchor_to_group) — now picks per-group from next_item(scope='group', pool_id='spot_group') so DIFFERENT groups will NEVER receive the same object at the same anchor (REVERSES the earlier 'shared object per anchor' Option 3A). The challenge row's `target_object` carries this group-specific value.\n\n  RULE 4 — Build Confidence per-user random (/api/confidence/daily). The old day-of-year deterministic _today_index pick is replaced with per-user queues (one per track: confidence_social / confidence_physical / confidence_gratitude). Picks are memoised per (user, UTC date, track) in NEW collection `confidence_today_picks` so reloading the screen the same day shows the SAME challenge; advancing the queue only happens once per UTC day per track per user.\n\n  RULE 5 — Treasure-Hunt selection rotation: NOT ENABLED YET because the current /bt/match/invite flow uses MANUAL seeker selection (hider picks a specific friend). The rotation queue would activate when a future 'auto-pick seeker' endpoint is added. Documented as deferred — challenge_queue module is ready to be used with scope='user', key=hider_id, pool_id='bt_seekers'.\n\n  RULE 6 — Free-For-All on reject (/app/backend/buried_treasure.py /bt/match/{mid}/reject + /bt/match/{mid}/find):\n    • /reject — when invited seeker taps reject, the match state stays in 'pending_accept' but gets flagged `free_for_all=true`, `free_for_all_started_at`, `free_for_all_started_by_reject_of=<rejecter user_id>`. Broadcast push 'Free-For-All Treasure!' to every friend of the hider (excluding the rejecter and the hider themselves). Hider also gets a 'Hunt re-released' notification.\n    • /find — accepts a winner in BOTH 'pending_accept' (FFA) AND 'in_progress' (normal) states. In FFA mode, ANY friend of the hider (verified via friend_ids_fn) can claim — except the rejecter and the hider. The winning user gets the standard XP_FIND_SEEKER, the hider gets XP_FIND_HIDER, and the doc now stores `winner_user_id` + `ffa_won_by` for analytics.\n\n  TESTING REQUIRED (against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api, admin filip.cojocea122@gmail.com / XL98CZW5599) — write to /app/global_rules_test.py:\n\n   1. Leaderboard L1 gate:\n      a. Register a fresh user FreshA. Befriend admin↔FreshA. Confirm FreshA.total_xp = 0.\n      b. GET /friends/leaderboard as admin → 200; FreshA should NOT appear in entries.\n      c. GET /friends/leaderboard as FreshA → 200; FreshA SHOULD appear (as the viewer, always shown).\n      d. Award FreshA 5 XP via any normal path (e.g. complete a task or use admin grant). Now GET /friends/leaderboard as admin → FreshA appears.\n\n   2. Spot Solo queue:\n      a. GET /spot/object 30+ times consecutively as same admin user. The returned `object` field must include every entry in SPOT_OBJECTS exactly once before any repeats. (Read SPOT_OBJECTS pool length first from code.)\n      b. After the full pool exhausts, the next pick should start a fresh shuffle — confirmable via `challenge_queues` row where cycle has incremented.\n\n   3. Spot Group queue:\n      a. Create 2 groups G1 and G2 both started+auto-on, with admin + a friend each.\n      b. POST /admin/spot/scheduler/force-tick. Inspect GET /spot/groups/{G1}/challenges + /G2/challenges. The newly fired challenges should have DIFFERENT target_object values (per-group queue).\n      c. Repeat force-tick multiple times — within each group, no repeat until that group's pool exhausts.\n\n   4. Build Confidence per-user queue:\n      a. Register users U1, U2. Both call GET /confidence/daily. Their `social.text` (or whichever field is the challenge identifier) should USUALLY differ (random per user; assert via challenge_queues rows that the queue order differs).\n      b. As U1, call GET /confidence/daily TWICE in the same UTC day — should return the SAME social/physical/gratitude challenges both times (memoised).\n      c. After UTC day rollover, the picks should advance to the next queued items. (Simulate via DIRECT mongo write to `confidence_today_picks` to clear yesterday's row, or just verify on second test run the next day.)\n\n   5. Free-For-All:\n      a. Befriend admin↔FreshA, admin↔FreshB, admin↔FreshC. Admin posts a Buried Treasure chest (POST /bt/chest/bury) and invites FreshA as seeker (/bt/match/invite). Match state = 'pending_accept'.\n      b. As FreshA, POST /bt/match/{mid}/reject → 200 free_for_all=true, free_for_all_started_by_reject_of=FreshA. State stays 'pending_accept'.\n      c. As FreshA, POST /bt/match/{mid}/find {lat, lng} → 403 'You already declined this hunt.'\n      d. As FreshD (not a friend of admin), POST /bt/match/{mid}/find → 403 'Free-For-All is open to the hider's friends only.'\n      e. As FreshB, POST /bt/match/{mid}/find {lat:<correct>, lng:<correct>} → 200 with state=found, winner_user_id=FreshB, ffa_won_by=FreshB.\n      f. As FreshC, POST /bt/match/{mid}/find → 409 or 400 (state changed).\n      g. Verify FreshB.total_xp incremented by XP_FIND_SEEKER and admin.total_xp by XP_FIND_HIDER.\n\n   6. Regression (existing flows still pass):\n      a. Phase 4 spot groups end-to-end (force-tick on a started group still fires and resolves a round).\n      b. /bt/match/{mid}/reject WITHOUT-FFA path: the legacy path is gone (every reject is now FFA). Document this in test results — if user wants 'pure reject' back, we'll add a body flag, otherwise FFA is the new default.\n      c. Build Confidence completions still flow via POST /confidence/complete (unchanged behaviour, just the daily pick is now per-user).\n\n  IMPORTANT NOTES:\n   • Rule 5 (turn rotation queue) is INFRASTRUCTURE-READY but not wired since /bt/match/invite uses manual selection. Skip rotation tests; just verify the challenge_queues collection schema is correct via Rule 2/3/4 tests.\n   • The friends_leaderboard fix only affects /friends/leaderboard. /admin/leaderboard/global is intentionally left as-is (admin needs to see signups with 0 XP).\n   • If 'pure reject' (non-FFA) behaviour is needed for any other UI, the legacy code path is preserved in git history of this commit."

    -message: "GLOBAL RULES RULE-6 RE-TEST — 61/62 assertions PASS in /app/global_rules_test.py (run 2026-05-19 against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api). The one-line fix at /app/backend/buried_treasure.py L855 in _load_match_or_404 (`if not m.get('free_for_all') and user_id not in (hider_id, seeker_id): raise 403`) WORKS — every previously-failing Rule 6 assertion is now green. SCOREBOARD: Rule 1 7/7, Rule 2 5/5, Rule 3 11/11, Rule 4 7/7, Rule 6 14/15 (was 4/15), Regression BT-normal-accept 4/4, Regression Spot-groups 5/5. ✅ Rule 6 successes: 6e B becomes winner with state='found' + Mongo ffa_won_by=B + B.total_xp+=100 + admin.total_xp+=50, 6d non-friend N gets 403 'Free-For-All is open to the hider's friends only.', 6c rejecter A gets 403 'You already declined this hunt.', 6f late C gets 400. ❌ Single minor failure (cosmetic, non-blocking): the /bt/match/{mid}/find RESPONSE BODY does not surface winner_user_id / ffa_won_by fields (they ARE persisted correctly to Mongo and the XP is correctly awarded — only the JSON response shape from _serialize_match() at buried_treasure.py:392-426 doesn't include these two keys). Suggested fix: add `'winner_user_id': m.get('winner_user_id'), 'ffa_won_by': m.get('ffa_won_by')` to the out dict in _serialize_match. Marked task working:true, stuck_count=0 — Rule 6 FFA is functionally complete. NO regressions on Rules 1-4, BT-normal-accept, or Spot-groups. DO NOT run frontend tests — ASK USER FIRST."
    -agent: "testing"
    -message: "GLOBAL RULES — 53/58 assertions PASS in /app/global_rules_test.py against https://xp-confidence.preview.emergentagent.com/api.\n\n  ✅ RULE 1 (Leaderboard L1 gate) — 7/7 PASS. Fresh 0-XP friend HIDDEN from admin's /friends/leaderboard; viewer F sees themselves; after F earns 15 XP via /confidence/complete, F becomes VISIBLE on admin's leaderboard.\n\n  ✅ RULE 2 (/spot/object per-user queue) — 5/5 PASS. 80 consecutive GET /spot/object calls. Pool size detected = 57 (deduped from SPOT_OBJECTS — note the static list at server.py L4652 has a duplicate 'spoon' entry that gets uniqued by dict.fromkeys in challenge_queue.py). First 57 picks are 57 DISTINCT objects (no repeats within a cycle). After exhaustion, repeats begin and challenge_queues row {_id:'user:<uid>:spot_solo'} has cycle>=2. Auto-reshuffle confirmed.\n\n  ✅ RULE 3 (per-group queue) — 11/11 PASS. Created 2 groups (R3 Group ONE, R3 Group TWO) each with admin+2 helpers. After force-tick #1, G1.target='spoon' vs G2.target='keyboard' → DIFFERENT objects at the same anchor (per-group queue working). 6 consecutive ticks on G1 produced 6 DISTINCT objects: spoon/piece of fruit/wristwatch/leaf/pillow/car. Mongo challenge_queues confirms two independent 'used' lists per group_id. NOTE: Test had to seed profile.timezone='Europe/London' for all helpers via direct Mongo write because the spot scheduler defers groups whose members are outside daylight (06:00–20:00 local fallback), and DEFAULT_TZ='Australia/Sydney' put fresh users at night UTC. Not a backend bug — same pattern as Phase 4 test harness.\n\n  ✅ RULE 4 (confidence per-user) — 7/7 PASS. Two fresh users U1, U2 GET /confidence/daily — all THREE tracks (social/physical/gratitude) returned different titles per user. Same user calling /confidence/daily twice same UTC day returned IDENTICAL picks. confidence_today_picks memo rows exist for each (user, date, track).\n\n  ❌ RULE 6 (Free-For-All find) — 5 CRITICAL FAILURES. /bt/match/{mid}/find returns 403 'Not your match.' for ALL non-participants in FFA mode — meaning the FFA-friend-find logic is DEAD CODE.\n\n    ROOT CAUSE: /app/backend/buried_treasure.py L851 in _load_match_or_404 raises HTTPException(403, 'Not your match.') if user_id is neither hider_id nor seeker_id — this fires BEFORE the FFA branch at L1056 can run. So even friends-of-hider who SHOULD be allowed to claim in FFA mode get rejected with the participant-gate error.\n\n    EVIDENCE:\n     • 6b. Admin invites A → 200, A rejects → 200 free_for_all=true, state stays 'pending_accept' (FFA flag correctly flipped) ✅\n     • 6c. A POST /find after reject → 403 'You already declined' ✅ (A is still seeker_id, so passes _load_match_or_404, then hits the L1059 rejecter-block correctly)\n     • 6d. Non-friend N POST /find → 403, but detail is 'Not your match.' NOT 'Free-For-All is open to the hider's friends only.' (cosmetic miss, but proves _load_match_or_404 is the gate)\n     • 6e. **B (friend of admin) POST /find → 403 'Not your match.'** — should be 200 with state=found, winner_user_id=B. Mongo doc still ffa_won_by=None, state=pending_accept.\n     • 6f. C (late friend) POST /find → 403 instead of 409 — for the same reason.\n\n    FIX (one-liner): in /app/backend/buried_treasure.py L847-853, change _load_match_or_404 to ALSO allow access when m.get('free_for_all') is True. Suggested implementation:\n      async def _load_match_or_404(mid, user_id):\n          m = await _db.bt_matches.find_one({'_id': mid})\n          if not m: raise HTTPException(404, 'Match not found.')\n          # FFA matches are open to friend-find; participant check defers to endpoint-specific logic.\n          if not m.get('free_for_all') and user_id not in (m.get('hider_id'), m.get('seeker_id')):\n              raise HTTPException(403, 'Not your match.')\n          return m\n    Then re-run /app/global_rules_test.py — Rule 6 should land 5/5 more PASS.\n\n  ✅ REGRESSION — Buried Treasure NORMAL accept flow still works (invite → accept → state='awaiting_burial', no free_for_all flag). Phase 1-4 spot groups flow still works (create → accept → start → list_challenges all 200).\n\n  ⚠️ DOCUMENTED BEHAVIOUR CHANGE — Per the spec, /bt/match/{mid}/reject now ALWAYS triggers Free-For-All (the legacy 'rejected' terminal state is gone). Confirmed: the rejecter receives state='pending_accept', free_for_all=true. If 'pure reject' (close the match) is needed in any UI it must be re-added as an explicit body flag.\n\n  Rule 5 (turn rotation queue) intentionally skipped — not wired to any endpoint per the test plan. challenge_queues schema verified indirectly via Rules 2, 3, 4.\n\n  Marked task working:false, needs_retesting:false (waiting on main agent's _load_match_or_404 fix). DO NOT trigger frontend testing — ASK USER FIRST."

    -agent: "main"
    -message: "CHALLENGE TASKS — Late-Answer UI wired up + solid-black modals. Frontend-only change (backend /api/challenge/past/{id}/answer already existed). Changes:\n  1) /app/frontend/src/api.ts — added `challengePastAnswer(id, body)` wrapper for POST /challenge/past/{id}/answer; extended ChallengeCompletion type with optional `can_answer`/`answer_deadline`.\n  2) /app/frontend/app/challenges/index.tsx —\n     • New `LateReflectModal` component (mirrors ReflectModal prompts: 'Did you complete?', 'How was it?', 'Was it difficult?', 'Write your experience', 5-star rating). Default `completed='no'` for late flow. Submits via challengePastAnswer.\n     • PastChallengeCard now receives `onReflect` prop; when card is `auto_uncompleted && can_answer`, shows a cyan 'Reflect on this challenge' button + new explanatory notice ('You didn't mark this as completed. Reflect on it now to still log it.'). When window has closed (`can_answer=false`), still shows the existing red 'window expired' notice.\n     • ReflectModal styling overhaul: dropped the transparent bottom-sheet (`backdrop` rgba(0,0,0,0.7) + popup card surfaceGlass) in favour of a SOLID-BLACK full-screen modal — `solidBlackSafe`/`solidBlackBackdrop`/`solidPopup` all use colors.bg (#05070D). Removed the draggable popup handle + click-outside Pressable dismiss; replaced with an explicit X close button at the top-right (`solidPopupCloseBtn`).\n     • Success step now uses `successWrap` (centered, solid-black) with a beefed-up `bigDoneBtn`: 22px vertical padding (vs 16), 24px ionicon (vs 18), 20px bold font (vs 15), stretches full width, alignSelf:'stretch', amber+green high-contrast. Same treatment applied to the new LateReflectModal success step (which displays awarded XP if completed=yes, or 'Reflection Saved' if completed=no since no XP is awarded for late-answering an uncompleted task).\n  No backend changes — backend endpoint /api/challenge/past/{completion_id}/answer at server.py L3305-3364 was untouched and previously verified working. Bundle compiles cleanly, /challenges screen renders without runtime errors after expo restart. NEEDS_FRONTEND_TESTING=ASK_USER (per protocol — do not auto-trigger expo_frontend_testing_agent)."
